definition.en: You think that the self must begin or end someplace. There must be a fence around it, a yard of identity in which you can feel safe. I have said many times that there are no limitations to the self.
definition.zh: 你们认为自我必须开始或结束于某处。它周围必须有一道篱笆，一个你们可以感到安全的身份庭院。我已说过很多次，自我没有限制。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[2]

definition.en: The inner self was too aware of its own multidimensionality, so in your terms it gave psychological birth to itself through the body in space and time. It knew itself as a physical creature. That portion of the self is the portion you recognize as your usual conscious self...
definition.zh: 内我太清楚它自己的多度次元性，所以用你们的说法，它透过时空中的身体给自己心理上的出生。它把自己认知为一个物质性的生物。自我的那个部分就是你们所认出的通常的有意识的自我……
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[3]

definition.en: First of all, the self that you are is ever-changing and never static. There is an inner self in the terms of those definitions, but that inner self, which is the source of your present being, speaks through your impulses.
definition.zh: 首先，你目前所是的这个自我是永远在变的，从不是静止的。以那些定义来说，有一个内我，但那个内我——你目前存在的源头——透过你的冲动发言。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[5]

definition.en: But beyond that self, there is another self, and still another self... of which I am fully aware.
definition.zh: 但在那个自我之外，还有另一个自我，以及再一个自我……我都完全觉知。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[6]

definition.en: The portions of the self are so constructed, so to speak, that when the stereophonic channel is turned on the selves then know their unity. Their various realities merge in the overall perceptions of the whole self.
definition.zh: 可以说，自我的各部分是这样建构的，以致当立体声道被打开时，这些自我便知道它们的统一性。它们不同的实相在全我的整体感知中融合。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[10]

explanation: 自我并非一个封闭、固定的实体，没有明确的边界。你通常认为的“自我”只是“内我”在物理世界中的一部分，即“自我（ego）”，它专注于物质现实，但并非全部。真正的自我是多维的、流动的，包含多个层次：有梦中的自己、可能的自己、以及更深层的内我。这些不同的“自己”并非彼此隔离，而是通过内在感官和潜意识相互连接、相互影响。内我通过冲动与你当下的自我对话，指引你朝向最理想的发展。你不需要抛弃或否定当下的自己来追求一个更伟大的版本，因为当下的自我本身就是内我的一部分，是它通过身体在时空中的“心理诞生”。信任并接纳你此刻所是的自己，正是通往更大自我认知的关键。自我不会因探索更广阔的维度而“流失”，

quotes.text: The ego considers itself the self and considers anything outside of its self as being either no self or another such separate identity, and so the individual man is led to believe that telepathy is basically a communication between two or more basically alone, separate and aloof selves. Telepathy does not operate at the level of the ego, although its actions may protrude into the domain of the ego. Telepathy operates within the inner self, within various levels, different levels of the subconscious, where the ideas of separation and limitations of self are not nearly so limiting.
quotes.translation: 自我视自身为自体，并将自体之外的一切视为非自体或另一个独立身份，因此个体人类相信心电感应本质上是两个基本孤独、分离且疏离的自体之间的沟通。心电感应并不在自我层面运作，尽管其活动可能渗入自我领域。心电感应运作于内在自体之中，在潜意识的不同层次间进行，在那里，关于分离与自体局限的观念远没有那么具有限制性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The basic consciousness is never physical. Yet within your system it must collect experience within the physical system, hence the physical body. But experience itself is not physical, and cannot be contained within physical matter. Therefore this experience, collected within the physical field, is held in codified form by this inner self or astral identity. It is only by understanding the connection between the physical and nonphysical self, and the communication systems that operate here, that the true nature of human personality can be studied. While we are dealing now with your own species, it should be realized that all consciousness also possesses its own astral identity. The inner senses are part of this nonphysical self. They allow the personality to retain its relationship with nonphysical reality, permitting the material self to focus within its earthly environment.
quotes.translation: 基本意识从来不是物质的。然而在你的系统内，它必须在物质系统中收集经验，因此有了物质身体。但经验本身并非物质，也无法被物质实体所容纳。因此，在物质领域收集的经验，由这个内在自体或星光体身份以编码形式保存。只有理解物质与非我自体之间的联系，以及在此运作的沟通系统，才能研究人类人格的真实本质。虽然我们现在讨论的是你们自己的物种，但应认识到所有意识也拥有其自身的星光体身份。内在感官是非物质自体的一部分。它们允许人格保持与非物质实相的关系，使物质自体能够聚焦于其尘世环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: You think that the self must begin or end someplace. There must be a fence around it, a yard of identity in which you can feel safe. I have said many times that there are no limitations to the self. You seem to be afraid that the self will bleed out and lose "itself" in a maze in which all identity is lost. Yet you recognize that your self is a far greater dimension than you usually suppose, so you speak in terms of reincarnation. This allows you to imagine greater realms of identity while still holding your concepts of selfhood intact. You think of being one self after another, each identity being neatly separated from the others by a passage of years, an obvious death and an obvious birth.
quotes.translation: 你认为自体必须有起点或终点。它周围必须有一道篱笆，一个你可以感到安全的身份庭院。我多次说过，自体没有限制。你似乎害怕自体将渗出并迷失在一切身份皆失的迷宫中。然而你认识到你的自体远比通常设想的维度更广阔，因此你谈论转世。这让你在保持自体概念完整的同时，想象更宏大的身份领域。你认为自己是一个接一个的自体，每个身份通过岁月的流逝、明显的死亡和明显的出生，与其他身份整齐地分隔开来。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 729
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: (All with emphatic rhythm:) The inner self was too aware of its own multidimensionality, so in your terms it gave psychological birth to itself through the body in space and time. It knew itself as a physical creature. That portion of the self is the portion you recognize as your usual conscious self, alive within the scheme of seasons, aware within the designs of time, caught transfixed in moments of brilliant awareness, with civilizations that seem to come and go. That is the self that is alert in the dear preciseness of the moments, whose physical senses are bound to light and darkness, sound and touch. That is the self that lives the life of the body.
quotes.translation: （全部以强调的节奏：）内在自体太清楚自己的多维性，因此用你们的说法，它通过身体在时空中心理上诞生了自己。它知道自己是一个物质生物。自体的那一部分是你所认知的平常意识自体，在季节的格局中活着，在时间的图景中觉知，在辉煌觉知的瞬间被凝固，伴随着似乎来来去去的文明。那就是在珍贵精确的瞬间保持警觉的自体，其物质感官与光明黑暗、声音触觉相连。那就是过着身体生活的自体。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is extremely important, since the dream world operates within the dimensions of your own psychic field, but utterly divorced from both space-time continuum and physical construction. Here you see the self truly spills over, not only into what you would call not-self, but into areas with which the conscious self is barely familiar. On an unconscious level however the self is very aware of the progress of these secondary personalities, and indeed uses this plane itself for the fulfillment and development of qualities originally attached to it, but incompatible with its main intents. The two planes constantly enrich and affect each other.
quotes.translation: 这极其重要，因为梦境世界运作于你们自己心理场的维度中，却完全脱离时空连续体和物质结构。在这里，你看到自体真正溢出，不仅进入你所谓的非自体，还进入意识自体几乎不熟悉的领域。然而在无意识层面，自体非常清楚这些次要人格的进展，并确实利用这个层面本身来实现和发展最初附着于它、但与它的主要意图不相容的特质。这两个层面不断相互丰富和影响。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Some correspondents write: "I realize that I am too egotistical." There are many schools for spiritual advancement that teach you to "get rid of the clutter of your impulses and desires," to shove aside the self that you are in search of a greater idealized version. First of all, the self that you are is ever-changing and never static. There is an inner self in the terms of those definitions, but that inner self, which is the source of your present being, speaks through your impulses. They provide in-built spiritual and biological impetuses toward your most ideal development (underlined). You must trust the self that you are, now.
quotes.translation: 一些通信者写道："我意识到自己太自我中心了。"有许多灵性提升的学派教导你"摆脱冲动和欲望的杂乱"，推开你现在的自体，以寻找一个更伟大的理想化版本。首先，你现在的自体是不断变化的，从未静止。按照那些定义，存在一个内在自体，但那个内在自体——你当前存在的源头——通过你的冲动说话。它们为你最理想的发展提供内置的灵性和生物动力（下划线强调）。你必须信任你现在所是的自体。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 872, AUGUST 8, 1979
9:15 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: And this part that you see and that appears in this room... and that can show joy, and show its existence and reality, that can call to you beyond space and time, that shows such energy, that shows you what energy can blow through such a small and slight frame... that self is a small part of my reality. For some time yet, you will need its familiarity. And you will need the human characteristics that you know... and that were mine... and they are still mine, for this self of mine that I show to you does still exist and grow. But beyond that self, there is another self, and still another self... of which I am fully aware.
quotes.translation: 你在这个房间所见的这个部分...能展现喜悦、展现其存在与实相、能跨越时空向你呼唤、展现如此能量、让你看到能量如何穿透如此纤细的躯体...这个自己只是我实相的一小部分。在一段时间内，你仍需要它的熟悉感。你仍需要你所知的人性特质...那些曾属于我的特质...它们现在仍属于我，因为我向你展现的这个自己确实依然存在并成长着。但在这个自己之外，还有另一个自己，以及再一个自己...我对此全然知晓。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You are not aware of your experiences in their entirety, for you experience events in a consecutive manner. You are therefore aware of your dreams only in a consecutive manner. You are hardly familiar with all of the dream experiences of your dreaming self, and barely familiar with any of their implications. The dreaming self is to some considerable degree conscious of the self which we shall here term the probable self. The probable self is somewhat like a twin self to the dreaming personality, for neither the experiences of the dreaming self nor the probable self occur within the complete radius of physical reality.
quotes.translation: 你并未完全觉察自己的经验，因为你以连续方式经历事件。因此你只能以连续方式觉察梦境。你几乎不熟悉做梦自己的所有梦境经验，也几乎不了解其中任何含义。做梦自己在相当程度上觉察到我们在此称为"可能自己"的自我。可能自己有点像做梦人格的双生自己，因为做梦自己的经验与可能自己的经验都不发生在物质实相的完整范围内。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Other portions of the self, on the one hand, are not so limited. It must be clearly understood however that these other portions of the self are incapable of the ego's intense focus within physical reality. Their focus is elsewhere. However, these selves are not limited as is the ego to one main field of perception only, in the manner which Dunne believes. Dunne does leave intervening areas between dimensions which may be perceived by an observer from a neighboring dimension, but all in all his serial selves are to some large degree prisoners of those dimensions in which they exist.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，自己的其他部分则不受如此限制。但必须清楚理解，这些自己的其他部分无法像自我那样在物质实相中强烈聚焦。它们的焦点在别处。然而，这些自己不像自我那样仅局限于单一主要感知领域——正如邓恩所认为的那样。邓恩确实在维度之间留下了可由邻近维度观察者感知的中间区域，但总体而言，他所说的系列自己在很大程度上仍是其所存在维度的囚徒。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Each of the twelve represented qualities of personality that belong to one individual, and Christ as you know him represented the inner self. The twelve, therefore, plus Christ as you know him (the one figure composed of the three) represented an individual earthly personality -- the inner self -- and twelve main characteristics connected with the egotistical self. As Christ was surrounded by the disciples, so the inner self is surrounded by these physically oriented characteristics, each drawn outward toward daily reality on the one hand, and yet orbiting the inner self.
quotes.translation: 十二种品质各自代表属于同一个体的人格特质，而你所知的基督代表内在自己。因此，十二门徒加上你所知的基督（由三者构成的一个形象）代表了一个个体的人格——内在自己——以及与自我相关的十二种主要特质。正如基督被门徒环绕，内在自己也被这些面向物质界的特质所环绕，每个特质一方面被拉向日常实相，另一方面又环绕着内在自己。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. Your stereophonic setting can be compared to what we have termed the inner ego. Now each of these selves experience time in their own manner, and as you should see by now this only means that they build their own realities according to the nature of their own perceptions. But the nature of their own perceptions is not chaotic. The portions of the self are so constructed, so to speak, that when the stereophonic channel is turned on the selves then know their unity. Their various realities merge in the overall perceptions of the whole self.
quotes.translation: 现在。你的立体声设置可比作我们所说的内在自我。现在这些自己各自以自己的方式体验时间，而你现在应该明白，这仅意味着它们根据自身感知的本质构建自己的实相。但它们的感知本质并非混乱。可以说，自己的这些部分被如此构建，以致当立体声通道开启时，这些自己便知晓其统一性。它们各自不同的实相在全我的整体感知中融合。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Still, to the unconscious the problem has been set and given. In the following deep protected areas of sleep, the higher centers of the inner self are allowed to function and come to the aid of the three-dimensionally oriented portion of the personality. This more liberated self sees the situation much more clearly, suggests a given line of action (but does not order it), and informs the dreaming self. The dreaming self then manufactures a group of dreams in which the solution is stated within a symbolic dream situation.
quotes.translation: 尽管如此，问题已被设定并交付给无意识。在随后的深层睡眠保护区中，内在自己的更高中心被允许运作，并协助人格中面向三维的部分。这个更解放的自己更清晰地看清局势，建议特定的行动路线（但不下达指令），并告知做梦自己。做梦自己随后制造一组梦境，在其中以象征性梦境情境陈述解决方案。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Pretend for a moment that you are a child, and I am trying to undertake the particular chore of explaining to you what your most developed, adult self will be like -- and in my explanation, I say that this adult self is to some extent already a part of you, an outgrowth or projection of what you are. And the child says, "But what will happen to me? Must I die to become this other self? I do not want to change. How can I ever be this adult self when it is not what I am now, without dying as what I am?"
quotes.translation: 暂且假设你是个孩子，而我正试图向你解释一个特别的任务——告诉你未来最成熟的成人自我会是什么样子。在我的解释中，我说这个成人自我在某种程度上已经是你的一部分，是你当前状态的延伸或投射。孩子问："但我会变成什么样？我必须死去才能成为这个另一个自我吗？我不想改变。如果我现在不是这个成人自我，我怎么可能在不以现在的我死去的情况下成为它呢？"
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At such times I'm apt to think about ideas of reincarnation and counterparts. Right here I'm dealing with just two of Seth's larger concepts. But without dwelling upon them too heavily, I may consider the notion of my larger, nonphysical "whole self" or "entity" being made up of a number of other psychically related physical selves projected into time. For Seth, basically there is no time, only a great "spacious present" that's a manifestation of a sublime, indescribable All That Is. Our gross physical senses, and indeed our very bodies, insist upon interpreting the spacious present in linear terms, however--through the inevitable processes of birth, aging, and death--so to help us get his point here Seth advances his ideas of reincarnational selves and counterpart selves in ways we can understand sensually.
quotes.translation: 在这种时候，我往往会思考转世和对应体的概念。这里我只涉及赛斯两个较大的概念。但不过分深究的话，我可以考虑我的更大、非物质的"整体自我"或"存有"的概念，它由许多投射到时间中的其他灵性相关的物质自我组成。对赛斯来说，基本上没有时间，只有一个伟大的"广阔当下"，它是崇高、难以描述的"一切万有"的显现。然而，我们粗重的物质感官，乃至我们的身体本身，都坚持用线性方式解释这个广阔当下——通过出生、衰老和死亡这些不可避免的过程——所以为了帮助我们理解他的观点，赛斯以我们能感官理解的方式提出了他的转世自我和对应自我的概念。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Even in God of Jane, which was published in 1981, Jane presented some relatively late material from Seth to show that he doesn't independently communicate with others. The idea that he'd done so can be inspiring, however. In Chapter 20 of her book, see Session 876 for August 27, 1979. After explaining how a couple of women (among others) had recently claimed that he had been in contact with them, Seth stated: "Now, I did not communicate with those women--but their belief in me helped each of them use certain abilities."
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: There are other self-conscious portions of the self however, with which the ego is not at all familiar, but of which the subconscious has intuitive knowledge. These self-conscious portions of the self exist in different reality systems. Before we go into our Dr. Instream material, let me remind you however that there is a whole self, composed of these various self-conscious selves, and that a portion of the self is indeed aware of the unity that exists to form the whole psychological gestalt.
quotes.translation: 然而，自我还有其他自我意识的部分，自我对此完全不熟悉，但潜意识却直觉地了解它们。这些自我的自我意识部分存在于不同的实相系统中。在我们进入我们的英斯准博士资料之前，让我提醒你，有一个整体自我，由这些不同的自我意识自我组成，而自我的一部分确实意识到形成整体心理完形的统一性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Your father's creativity, as mentioned [in earlier, unpublished sessions] had its side of secrecy, privacy and aloneness ... you identified creatively with his private nature. The writing self became latent as the sportsman did, yet the writing self and the artist were closely bound. You felt conflicts at times. It never occurred to you that the two aspects could release one another -- one illuminating the other -- and both be fulfilled. Instead you saw them as basically conflicting. Time spent writing meant time not spent painting. You believed the painting self had to be protected ... as you felt that your father had to protect his creative self in the household ...
quotes.translation: 正如[早期未发表的课程中]所提到的，你父亲的创造力有其隐秘、私密和孤独的一面……你创造性地认同了他的私密本性。写作自我变得像运动员一样潜伏，但写作自我和艺术家紧密相连。你有时感到冲突。你从未想到这两个方面可以相互释放——一个照亮另一个——并且两者都能得到实现。相反，你把它们视为根本冲突的。花在写作上的时间意味着花在绘画上的时间减少。你相信绘画自我必须被保护……就像你感觉你父亲必须在家庭中保护他的创造性自我一样……
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: n mentioning my "sportsman self," Seth referred to information he'd given about three of my probable selves in a private session on January 30, 1974 -- just a few days before starting "Unknown" Reality. The session contains many personal insights that I now recognize as being quite true. But even without Seth's help, interesting results can flow from an awareness of the probable-self concept: The reader can begin to intuitively consider his or her own probable selves, or those of others who may be closely related psychically or physically. I'm not writing here about rationalizing the existence of one or more probable selves to account for personal shortcomings in this reality, however, but of simply using the idea to enlarge our basic notions of the human potential. See Note I for Session 679.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: We must use analogies. It can be thought of as the most exterior projection of the inner ego. When you find yourself, so to speak, watching what you think of as the ego, then you are in contact with this portion of the self. It is aware of both conscious and subconscious motivations and realities, and it is also aware of projections into other fields of actuality. I have hinted before of these matters, for when I say that you will use this other portion of the self to examine waking consciousness and probe beneath it, I already presuppose a you that uses this self. In other words, you are already magnifying the limitations of the self and extending them.
quotes.translation: 我们必须使用类比。它可以被视为内在自我的最外在投射。当你发现自己，可以说，在观察你认为的自我时，你就与自我的这一部分接触了。它既意识到有意识和潜意识的动机与实相，也意识到投射到其他实相领域。我之前暗示过这些事，因为当我说你将使用自我的这一部分来检视清醒意识并深入其下时，我已经预设了一个使用这个自我的"你"。换句话说，你已经在放大自我的限制并扩展它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Recognized concepts of the self are the ego's interpretation of selfhood. They are projected into concepts of God and the universe. They meet with a certain biological validity because of the selectivity earlier mentioned, whereby only one series of neurological pulses is accepted -- and upon these rides the reality of the egotistical self. At one "time" a god interpreted in those terms served as a model for the egotistical behavior of one self toward another self.
quotes.translation: 公认的自我概念是自我对自我性的解释。它们被投射到神和宇宙的概念中。由于前面提到的选择性，它们具有一定的生物学有效性，即只有一系列神经脉冲被接受——而自我的现实就建立在这些脉冲之上。在某个"时间"，用这些术语解释的神，曾作为自我对另一个自我的自我行为模式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 686
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: An occurrence only remotely approaching this can be disastrous. Not because it is basically undesirable, for such an experience has the greatest potentials for development of the self. Such experiences can be disastrous simply because the self structure is not yet strong enough to assimilate and contain the intensity of the experience. In many cases dream experiences, as I have mentioned, are much more vivid and intense than waking experience. You do not even remember the majority of these. But the inner self is more flexible, you see, than the ego, and it can therefore contain greater intensities without undue alarm.
quotes.translation: 一种仅能勉强接近这种体验的事件可能具有灾难性。并非因其本质有害——此类经历对自我发展具有最大潜能——而是因为自我结构尚不够强大，无法吸收并容纳这种体验的强度。如我所提，许多梦境体验远比清醒体验更为生动强烈。你们甚至记不住其中大部分。但内在自我比自我意识更具弹性，因此能容纳更大强度而不致过度惊慌。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The dreaming self, dear friends, is not aware of the conscious self. The whole self, the entire inner self alone, holds knowledge of the direction in which it moves. The directions can be likened to conscious selves. Any individual on the physical level who has achieved great things has done so because his so-called conscious self was intuitively （and underline the word intuitively） aware of the selves of which he could not be consciously aware.
quotes.translation: 亲爱的朋友们，做梦的自我并不觉知意识自我。唯有整个自我、完整的内在自我，才知晓其行进方向。这些方向可类比为意识自我。任何在物质层面取得伟大成就的个体，皆因其所谓的意识自我能直觉地（请着重强调"直觉地"）觉察到那些无法有意识认知的自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

source_raw.title: （As Seth states, Jane did have a dream about the idea the following night, September 11,1963. She recalls it quite easily. However she has no written record of the dream, since this was before she had cultivated the habit of keeping a dream notebook. As mentioned many many sessions ago, however, her poem The Fence, written in May 1963, clearly foreshadows the Seth material, dealing with [but not always by outright name] such subjects as reincarnation, dreams, unperceived worlds, etc. [See the poem on page 28, Session 5, in Volume I of The Early Sessions]

quotes.text: This electrical pattern the personality, with all the experiences of its earthly time. It then can join or partake of the inner self. In other words, though the ego was adopted originally by the inner self, and was a product of physical heredity and environment, it does not die; but its existence is changed from physical reality into electrical reality. It is still individual. No individuality is lost, but it becomes a part of the inner self, and its experiences are added to the total experience of the many personalities that have composed the inner self.
quotes.translation: 这种电性模式承载着人格及其尘世所有经历。它随后能融入或参与内在自我。换言之，虽然自我意识最初由内在自我采用，且是物质遗传与环境的产物，但它不会消亡；其存在形态从物质实相转变为电性实相。它仍保持个体性。没有任何个体性会丧失，但它成为内在自我的一部分，其经历被添加到构成内在自我的众多人格的总经验中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Each channel will represent a portion of the self, each one existing in a different dimension, and yet all part of the whole self, or the whole tape. You can see that it would be highly ridiculous to say that the material on your Mono One was any more or any less valid than your material on Mono Two. Mono One and Mono Two could be compared then to self one and two; self one and two here, however, in the context of the ego as self one and the subconscious as you know it as self two. We are not using Dunnes terms here, in other words.
quotes.translation: 每个频道将代表自我的一部分，每个部分存在于不同维度，却都是整体自我（即整条录音带）的组成部分。由此可见，声称第一声道的内容比第二声道更有效或更无效是极其荒谬的。第一声道与第二声道可类比为自我一与自我二；不过在此语境中，自我一是指自我意识，自我二是指你们所知的潜意识。换言之，我们并未使用邓恩的术语。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Basically there are no real divisions to the self, but for the sake of explanation we must speak of them in those terms. First of all you had the inner self, the creative dreaming self--composed, again, of units of consciousness, awareized energy that forms your identity, and that formed the identities of the earliest earth inhabitants. These inner selves formed their own dream bodies about them, as previously explained, but the dream bodies did not have to have physical reactions. They were free of gravity and space, and of time.
quotes.translation: 本质上自我并无真正分割，但为便于解释，我们不得不使用这些术语。首先你们拥有内在自我、创造性的做梦自我——它同样由意识单元构成，即形成你们身份且曾形成最早地球居民身份的觉知能量。这些内在自我如先前所述，在自身周围形成了梦境身体，但梦境身体无需产生物理反应。它们不受重力、空间与时间的束缚。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner self is embarked upon an exciting endeavor, in which it learns how to translate its reality into physical terms. The conscious mind is brilliantly attuned to physical reality, then, and often so dazzled by what it perceives that it is tempted to think physical phenomena is a cause, rather than a result. Deeper portions of the self always serve to remind it that this is not the case. When the conscious mind accepts too many false beliefs, particularly if it sees that inner self as a danger, then it closes out these constant reminders. When this situation arises the conscious mind feels itself assailed by a reality that seems greater than itself, over which it has no control. The deep feeling of security in which it should be anchored is lost.
quotes.translation: 内在自我正投身于一项激动人心的尝试，学习如何将其实相转化为物质形态。意识心智因此与物质实相完美调频，却常被自身所感知的景象迷惑，误以为物质现象是原因而非结果。自我的更深层部分始终提醒它事实并非如此。当意识心智接受过多错误信念，尤其当它将内在自我视为危险时，便会屏蔽这些持续提醒。当此情况发生时，意识心智会感到被某种看似超越自身且无法掌控的实相所侵袭。它本应扎根的深层安全感就此丧失。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Each individual is a universe in a small package. (A one-minute pause.) As the physical planets move in order while being individual, so there can be a social order that is based upon the integrity of the individual. But that order would recognize the inner validity that is within the self; and the inner order, unseen, that forms the integrity of the physical body, likewise would form the integrity of the social body. The self, the individual, being its fulfilled self, would automatically function for the good of itself and for the good of society. The individual's good, therefore, is the society's good, and represents spiritual and physical fulfillment. This presupposes, however, an understanding of the inner self and an exploration into the unknown reality of the individual psyche.
quotes.translation: 每个个体都是一个浓缩的宇宙。（停顿一分钟。）如同物理行星在保持个体性的同时有序运行，社会秩序也可以建立在个体完整性的基础上。但这种秩序会认识到自我内在的有效性；那看不见的内在秩序，既构成物质身体的完整性，同样也会构成社会身体的完整性。自我——这个个体——当其实现圆满的自我时，会自动为自身利益与社会利益运作。因此，个体之善即社会之善，并代表灵性与物质的圆满实现。但这预设了对内在自我的理解，以及对个体心灵未知实相的探索。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 704
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: This sense of division within the self forces you to think that there is a remote, spiritual, wise, intuitive inner self, and a bewildered, put-upon, spiritually ignorant, inferior physical self, which happens to be the one you identify with. Many of you believe, moreover, that the physical self's very nature is evil, that its impulses, left alone, will run in direct opposition to the good of the physical world and society, and fly in the face of the deeper spiritual truths of inner reality. The inner self then becomes so idealized and so remote that by contrast the physical self seems only the more ignorant and flawed. In the face of such beliefs the ideal of psychic development, or astral travel, or spiritual knowledge, or even of sane living, seems so remote as to be impossible. You must, therefore, begin to celebrate your own beings, to look to your own impulses as being the natural connectors between the physical and the nonphysical self. Children trusting their impulses learn to walk, and trusting your impulses, you can find yourselves again.
quotes.translation: 自我内部的这种分裂感迫使你认为存在一个遥远、灵性、智慧、直觉的内在自我，以及一个困惑、受欺、灵性无知、低劣的物质自我——而你恰好认同于后者。此外，许多人相信物质自我的本质是邪恶的，其冲动若不加约束，会直接违背物质世界与社会的善，并公然对抗内在实相的深层灵性真理。于是内在自我被过度理想化、变得遥不可及，相形之下物质自我显得更加无知与缺陷。面对这样的信念，心灵发展、星光体出游、灵性知识甚至健全生活的理想，都显得遥不可及、无法实现。因此，你必须开始庆祝自己的存在，将你的冲动视为物质自我与非物质自我之间的自然连接器。信任自己冲动的孩子学会走路，而信任你的冲动，你就能重新找到自己。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: So far in our discussion, then, we have an inner self, dwelling primarily in a mental or psychic dimension, dreaming itself into physical form, and finally forming a body consciousness. To that body consciousness the inner self gives "its own body of physical knowledge," the vast reservoir of physical achievement that it has triumphantly produced. (Pause.) The body consciousness is not "unconscious," but for working purposes in your terms, [the body] possesses its own system of consciousness that to some extent, now (underlined), is separated from what you think of as your own normal consciousness. The body's consciousness is hardly to be considered less than your own, or as inferior to that of your inner self, since it represents knowledge from the inner self, and is a part of the inner self's own consciousness--the part delegated to the body.
quotes.translation: 那么，在我们的讨论中，目前我们有一个内在自我，主要栖居于心智或心灵维度，将自己梦入物质形态，最终形成身体意识。内在自我将"它自身的物质知识体"——它胜利产生的庞大物质成就库——赋予那个身体意识。（停顿。）身体意识并非"无意识"，而是为了在你方术语中运作的目的，[身体]拥有自己的意识系统，在某种程度上，现在（强调）与你认为的正常意识是分离的。身体意识绝不应被视为低于你的意识，或低于你内在自我的意识，因为它代表来自内在自我的知识，是内在自我自身意识的一部分——被委派给身体的那部分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am making generalizations here, but each individual case should be looked at in its own light. Such people as a rule, however, have an exaggerated version of the self (pause), so idealized (long pause) that its very existence intimidates practical action. They are afraid of making mistakes, terrified of betraying this sensed inner psychological superior. Usually, such an idealized inner self comes from the acceptance of highly distorted beliefs--again, concerning good and evil. You end up with what can amount to two main inner antagonists: a superior self and a debased self. The qualities considered good are attracted to the superior self as if it were a magnet. The qualities that seem bad (underlined) are in the same fashion attracted to the debased self. Both of them, relatively isolated psychological polarities, hold about equal sway. All other psychological evidences that are ambiguous, or not clearly understood by either side, group together under their own psychological banners. This is a kind of circular rather than linear arrangement, however, psychologically speaking.
quotes.translation: 我在此做概括性论述，但每个个案都应从其自身角度审视。然而，这类人通常拥有一个被夸大的自我版本（停顿），如此理想化（长停顿）以至于其存在本身便令实际行动畏缩。他们害怕犯错，恐惧背叛这个感知到的内在心理优越者。通常，这种理想化的内在自我源于接受高度扭曲的信念——再次涉及善恶问题。最终你得到两个主要的内在对抗者：一个优越自我与一个卑劣自我。被视为善的品质被吸引到优越自我，如同被磁铁吸引。被视为恶（强调）的品质同样被吸引到卑劣自我。两者作为相对孤立的心灵极性，拥有大致相等的支配力。所有其他模糊不清或未被任何一方明确理解的心理证据，则聚集在各自的心理旗帜下。然而从心理学角度说，这是一种循环而非线性的排列。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: At the beginning of the notes for this 920th session I wrote that the session isn't dictation for Dreams. Yet it is, of course, since in it Seth illuminates from still another perspective his concept of value fulfillment.8 The session grew out of the encounter we had yesterday afternoon with an unexpected visitor from out of state. Seth hardly mentions the individual involved, however, but instead goes into the subject of mental illness in more inclusive terms. I see many correlations between this evening's material and that in Session 917 for Chapter 8; there, among other things, Seth had discussed the reasoning mind, the imaginative mind, and schizophrenia. That session had been triggered, at least in part, by a letter Jane had found to be quite upsetting.

quotes.text: Beneath this, pure and simple, undistorted, there for the searching, absolutely free for the asking, is the knowledge inherent in the inner self pertaining to the inner universe as a whole, its laws and principles, its composition. Here you will find, undistorted, uncamouflaged, the innate knowledge of the creation of the camouflage universe, the mechanics involved, much of the material that I have given you, the method and ways by which the inner self as a basic inhabitant of the inner universe, existing in the climate of psychological reality, helps create the various planes of existence, constructs outer senses to project and perceive the various apparent realities or camouflages, how the inner self reincarnates on the various planes. Here you will find your answers as to how the inner self transforms energy for his own purposes, changes his form, adopts other apparent realities, and all this free for the investigation.
quotes.translation: 在这之下，纯粹、简单、未经扭曲、等待探索、完全免费索取的是内在自我固有的知识，关乎整个内在宇宙及其法则、原理与构成。在此你将找到未经扭曲、未经伪装的：关于伪装宇宙创造的内在知识、涉及的机制、我给予你们的许多资料、内在自我作为内在宇宙的基本居民（存在于心理实相的气候中）帮助创造各种存在层面、构建外在感官以投射和感知各种表面实相或伪装的方式方法，以及内在自我如何在各个层面转世。在此你将找到答案：内在自我如何为自身目的转化能量、改变形态、采纳其他表面实相——所有这些都免费供你探究。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: I do not believe that Dunne understood this. There is no serialization as he imagined, after a certain point, simply because this progression of selves through various times in a serial fashion is no longer necessary. The selves reach a point which is not a theoretical point, but a particular mathematically existent point, whereby these times and selves simply become one, or in our terms, an entity. I must stress that individuality is never lost. But this is too complicated a subject to cover this evening. We have explained it rather adequately in terms of action however, and gestalts of selves do not imply a giving-up of individuality at all. It should be remembered here that reincarnation is simply a fact, and one which is not accepted by Priestley or Dunne.
quotes.translation: 我认为邓恩并未理解这一点。不存在他想象的那种序列化，在某个点之后，这种自我以序列方式穿越不同时间的进程不再必要。自我达到一个点——这不是理论上的点，而是特定数学存在的点——在此这些时间与自我简单合一，或用我们的术语说，成为一个"存有"。我必须强调个体性从未丧失。但今晚这个话题过于复杂。我们已用"行动"术语相当充分地解释过，而自我的"完形"绝不意味着放弃个体性。此处应记住：转世只是一个事实，一个普里斯特利与邓恩都不接受的事实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: The portrait is of Joseph and Ruburt. These are not portraits of yourselves as past personalities in your terms, or of particular re-incarnational selves. They are pictorial representatives of the whole selves that you are. In your terms the selves that are the sum of your re-incarnational personalities. These whole selves then are a part of your entity. Your probable selves are also a part of your entity, however. If you want to think in terms of guides, in quotes "angels," then you have the reality behind those terms. Ruburt and Joseph are the names I use for you, for these names themselves imply your greater goals. These personalities are aware of your present existence, and give you advice when you are in the sleep state. They are a source of strength. You can draw upon their knowledge, and you can also draw upon the knowledge of your own re-incarnational selves, past, in quotes, and future.
quotes.translation: 这幅肖像是约瑟夫和鲁柏。这些并非你们所认为的过去人格画像，也不是特定转世自我的画像。它们是你们整体自我的图像化呈现——用你们的话说，就是你们所有转世人格的总和。这些整体自我是你们存有的一部分。然而，你们的可能性自我也是存有的一部分。如果你们想用指导灵、"天使"这类概念来理解，那么这些术语背后确实存在着实相。鲁柏和约瑟夫是我对你们的称呼，因为这些名字本身就暗示着你们更宏大的目标。这些人格知晓你们当前的存在，并在你们处于睡眠状态时给予建议。他们是力量的源泉。你们可以汲取他们的知识，也可以汲取自己"过去"和"未来"转世自我的知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: When his senses, his outer senses, do not perceive a physical object in his self-perspective （and hyphenate that please）, in his self-perspective , the object simply does not exist. If the object is touched and not seen or otherwise perceived, then in his self-perspective it exists only in the realm of his sensual perception of it. It does not exist to be seen if he does not see it. If his father, for example, sees the chair that the boy does not see, then the object exists as a thing to be seen in the father's self-perspective . Each individual himself creates a portion or a whole physical object. Many people appear to see an object, but the object that they see is not the same object, but only approximates an object.
quotes.translation: 当他的感官——外在感官——在自我视角（请加上连字符）中感知不到物质客体时，这个客体就不存在。如果客体被触摸到却未被看见或通过其他方式感知，那么在他的自我视角中，它只存在于他感官感知的领域。如果他没看见，它就不存在于可被看见的范畴。例如，如果他的父亲看见了那个男孩没看见的椅子，那么该客体作为可被看见的事物存在于父亲的自我视角中。每个个体都创造着物质客体的部分或整体。许多人看似看见同一个客体，但他们所见之物并非相同客体，只是近似于某个客体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: At the same time, you make this information available to all these other portions of your identity, who dwell in entirely different realities, and you receive from them comparable information. You do not lose contact with your ordinary waking self. You simply do not focus upon it. You turn your attention away. In the daytime you simply reverse the process. If you were looking at your daily normal self from the other viewpoint, you see, using an analogy here, you might find that physically waking self as strange as you now find the sleeping self. The analogy will not hold however, simply because this sleeping self of yours is far more knowledgeable than the waking self of which you are so proud.
quotes.translation: 与此同时，你们将这些信息提供给栖身于完全不同实相中的其他身份部分，并从他们那里接收类似信息。你们并未失去与日常清醒自我的联系，只是不再聚焦于它。你们只是转移了注意力。白天你们只是逆转这个过程。如果从另一个视角观察日常的清醒自我，打个比方，你们可能会发现这个物质清醒自我和现在觉得睡眠自我一样陌生。但这个比喻并不完全成立，因为你们的睡眠自我远比你们引以为傲的清醒自我更具智慧。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As far as self and not-self are concerned, the unit of self is organized, as you know, by the inner ego, which directs the whole energy field. The outer ego directs the manipulation of this gestalt in the physical universe. The outer ego is rather more tied to physical properties, and yet it can directly experience inner reality by a change of focus through aligning itself with the inner ego, focusing its energies with the inner rather than the outer senses. This is by far the most advantageous method of experiencing inner reality, because the outer ego is therefore consciously aware of what has been going on, and can use such knowledge in its own sphere.
quotes.translation: 就自我与非自我而言，如你所知，自我单元由内在自我组织，它引导着整个能量场。外在自我则主导这个完形在物质宇宙中的运作。外在自我更受物质属性束缚，但通过调整焦点与内在自我对齐，将能量聚焦于内在而非外在感官，它也能直接体验内在实相。这是体验内在实相最有利的方式，因为外在自我能清醒意识到所发生之事，并在自身领域运用这些知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The personality is not the whole self. It is a portion of the whole self, which is activated during a particular existence. The ego, as I have explained, does not vanish. However, it must always be remembered here that the ego is not the self-conscious self in its entirety by any means. It is simply a portion, a field of focus whereby the self attempts to objectify itself within the world of matter.
quotes.translation: 人格并非整体自我。它只是整体自我的一部分，在特定存在期间被激活。如我所解释的，自我并未消失。但必须始终谨记：自我绝非全然等同于自我意识。它只是自我试图在物质世界中客观化自身时的一个聚焦领域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The subconscious, and in fact all portions of the self with the exception of the ego, are capable of assimilating a wider area, so to speak, of action. Therefore to these other portions of the self, time has a much different essence than it has for the ego. The ego is indeed many things. It can be defined in relationship to many other aspects of reality. In relationship to action, and moment points, the ego is indeed that portion of the self which stands at the apex of the moment point, and is limited by the moment point. The ego is in this context the portion of the self which is utterly focused upon, and imprisoned by, the moment point.
quotes.translation: 潜意识——事实上除自我之外的所有自我部分——都能吸收更广阔的行动领域。因此对这些自我部分而言，时间具有与自我截然不同的本质。自我确实具有多重面向。它可以与实相的其他诸多方面相关联来定义。就行动与瞬间点而言，自我确实是立于瞬间点顶端、受限于瞬间点的那部分自我。在此语境下，自我是完全聚焦于并被囚禁在瞬间点中的那部分自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Consciousness of self involves a consciousness of self within, amid, and as a part of action. ego consciousness, on the other hand, involves a state in which consciousness of self attempts to divorce itself from action, an attempt on the part of consciousness to perceive action as an object. Here we see that ego consciousness, in this attempt, strives to perceive action not only as separate, but to perceive it in such a fashion that it appears to ego that action is not only separate from itself, that is separate from the ego, but that action is initiated by the ego, and a result rather than a cause of ego's own existence.
quotes.translation: 对自我的觉知涉及在行动之中、作为行动的一部分、且与行动同在的自我意识。而自我意识则涉及一种状态，其中自我意识试图与行动分离，意识试图将行动视为一个客体。在此我们看到，自我意识在这种尝试中，不仅努力将行动视为分离的，而且以这样一种方式感知行动，使得自我看来行动不仅与自身分离（即与自我分离），而且行动是由自我发起的，是自我自身存在的结果而非原因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The inner ego directs all activities which are deeply subjective. The outer ego as you know deals with the manipulation of the self in the physical environment. You must remember that the whole self is more than the sum of its parts; and also, since it is action, it is never the same. It is therefore impossible at any moment to pinpoint the personality. Therefore the whole self is not only the sum of the personality as you know it in your time, it is also the sum of what it has been and what it shall be. For as I have explained action is simultaneous, and time as you think of it is caused by your own physical perception.
quotes.translation: 内在自我引导所有深度主观的活动。而你们所知的外在自我则处理自我在物质环境中的操控。你们必须记住，整体自我大于其部分之和；同时，由于它是行动，它永远不会相同。因此，在任何时刻都不可能精确指出人格。所以整体自我不仅是你们在时间中所知的人格总和，也是它曾经所是及将来所是之总和。因为正如我所解释的，行动是同时性的，而你们所认为的时间是由你们自身的物质感知造成的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: "The gurus say: 'Give it all up.' One of those we read about today counsels: 'When you want to do one thing, do another instead. Do not do what you want to do, but what you should do.' Never trust the self that you are, the gurus say, but the self that you should be. And that self is supposed to be dead to desire, beyond wanting or caring; yet paradoxically, this nonfeeling leads to bliss. The gurus say that All That Is is within you, yet tell you not to trust yourself. If All That Is didn't want appearances, we wouldn't experience any! Yet appearances, the gurus say, are untruths, changing and therefore false.2
quotes.translation: "古鲁们说：'放下一切。'我们今天读到的一位导师劝诫：'当你想做一件事时，反而去做另一件。不要做你想做的，而要做你应该做的。'古鲁们说，永远不要信任你现在的自我，而要信任你应该成为的自我。那个自我应该对欲望无感，超越渴望或关心；然而矛盾的是，这种无感会带来极乐。古鲁们说一切万有在你之内，却又告诉你不信任自己。如果一切万有不想要表象，我们根本不会体验到任何表象！然而古鲁们说，表象是不真实的，是变化的，因此是虚假的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 710)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The physical body is therefore an attribute of consciousness. The atoms and molecules possess a self-consciousness to some degree, as well as a generalized consciousness. The self awareness of a human being is partially composed of the combination of self aware individual atoms and molecules that compose it. The generalized consciousness which you call the subconscious, is composed of the combination of generalized consciousness from the individual cells and molecules, but now we come down to the source of the self aware conscious individual behind or within each molecule. Where did it come from to begin with?
quotes.translation: 因此，物质身体是意识的一种属性。原子和分子在某种程度上拥有自我意识，以及一种普遍意识。人类的自我觉知部分由构成它的、具有自我意识的个体原子和分子的组合所组成。你们称之为潜意识的普遍意识，则由个体细胞和分子的普遍意识组合而成，但现在我们追溯到每个分子背后或内部的、具有自我意识的觉知个体的源头。它最初从何而来？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: (11:14.) I told you that the self was not limited, yet surely you think that your self stops where your skin meets space, that you are inside your skin. Period. Yet your environment is an extension of your self. It is the body of your experience, coalesced in physical form. The inner self forms the objects that you know as surely and automatically as it forms your finger or your eye.
quotes.translation: （11:14。）我告诉过你们，自我不受限制，然而你们肯定认为，你们的自我止于皮肤与空间相接之处，你们在皮肤之内。句号。然而，你们的环境是你们自我的延伸。它是你们经验的躯体，凝聚成物质形态。内在自我形成你们所知的物体，就像它形成你们的手指或眼睛一样确定而自动。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: But as it is not wise to dispense with the idea of nationalism without gradual growths of understanding and preparation, and while the idea of nationalism cannot suddenly be dispensed with, so also the ego cannot be, and will not be, overthrown overnight; and even when it is finally left behind, it will still be used as a handy reference point; and through all this the self will not lose but gain, for all expansion outward, and expansion inward is a gain, and all boundaries, whether inward or outward, are hampering and limiting. Basically, the self is not limited. The self does not need imaginary fences to protect its privacy, or its safety or its solitude. Only the ego is afraid of challenge, and therefore speaks of such limiting safety.
quotes.translation: 但正如在没有逐步的理解和准备的情况下，放弃国家主义观念是不明智的，且国家主义观念不能突然被废除，同样地，自我也不能且不会在一夜之间被推翻；即使最终被超越，它仍将被用作一个方便的参考点；而在此过程中，自我不会失去，反而会获得，因为所有向外的扩展和向内的扩展都是收获，而所有边界，无论是向内还是向外，都是阻碍和限制。基本上，自我不受限制。自我不需要想象的围栏来保护其隐私、安全或独处。只有自我害怕挑战，因此才会谈论这种限制性的安全。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Consider this analogy: The self as a moving circle, such as a Ferris wheel. A tree in front of the wheel will represent physical reality. The whole self, or the whole wheel, is composed of many selves in various positions, as the many people who sit on the Ferris wheel. As the wheel turns you call the person or the self who faces the tree the ego, simply because this is the portion that faces physical reality, represented by our tree. But the self who faces the tree one moment is not the self that faces it the next moment, and the operator of the wheel is never in evidence, you see.
quotes.translation: 试想这个比喻：自体如同一座移动的圆环，好比摩天轮。轮前的树木代表物质实相。整个自体或整座轮子，由处于不同位置的众多自体组成，如同坐在摩天轮上的众多乘客。当轮子转动时，你称面向树木的那个自体或人格为"自我"，只因这是面向物质实相（以树木为象征）的部分。但此刻面向树木的自体，下一刻已非同一自体，而轮子的操控者始终隐而不现，你明白吗？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You would not be able to perceive a sufficient amount of data to make any deductions. This is not to say that such planes or fields of actuality cannot in some manner be perceived, but they will escape physical perception or exploration. In dreams, when the self is somewhat free from camouflage, perhaps it is possible for the self then to travel spontaneously into such fields of actuality; but since they are as bizarre to the self as is the dream world itself, then there is no way, usually, for the sleeper to distinguish between the universe of dreams or other actualities.
quotes.translation: 你无法感知足够的数据来做出任何推论。这并非说这类实相层面或场域完全无法被感知，但它们会避开物质感知或探索。在梦境中，当自体暂时摆脱伪装时，或许能自发地进入这类实相场域；但由于这些场域对自体而言与梦境世界同样奇异，通常沉睡者无法区分梦境宇宙与其他实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You would learn the instrument far quicker, you see, if the impulse was originating with a probable self. It goes without saying then that probable selves exist in your "future" as well as your past. It is very poor policy to dwell negatively on unpleasant aspects of the past that you know, because some portions of the probable self may still be involved in that past. The concentration can allow greater bleed-through and adverse identification, because that part will be one background that you have in common with any probable selves who sprang from that particular source.
quotes.translation: 若冲动源自某个可能自体，你学习工具的速度会快得多，你明白吗。不言而喻，可能自体既存在于你的"未来"，也存在于你的过去。沉溺于已知过去的负面面向是极不明智的，因为可能自体的某些部分可能仍牵连其中。这种专注会导致更强烈的渗透与负面认同，因为那部分将成为你与源自该特定源头的所有可能自体共有的背景。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is one God, but within that God are many. There is one self, but within that self are many. There is one body, in one time, but the self has other bodies in other times. All "times" exist at once. (Long pause.) Historically speaking, mankind chose a certain line of development. In it his consciousness specialized, focusing upon sharp particulars of experience. But inherent always, psychologically and biologically, there has been the possibility of a change in that pattern, an alteration that would effectively lift the race into another kind of weather.
quotes.translation: 只有一个神，但神之内包含众多。只有一个自体，但自体之内包含众多。只有一个身体存在于一个时间，但自体在其他时间拥有其他身体。所有"时间"同时存在。（长停顿。）从历史角度看，人类选择了特定的发展路线。在此路线中，意识得以专业化，聚焦于经验的鲜明细节。但无论在心理层面还是生物层面，始终潜藏着改变这种模式的可能性——一种能有效将人类提升至另一种"气候"的转变。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: But the outer ego as you know is that portion of the whole self that is given over to the maintenance of physical manipulations, and it perceives only through those physical equipment, those outer senses. It sees its dominion in jeopardy and rises up in arms. It, the ego, is that small portion of the whole self which the whole self has allowed to be completely, or almost completely, bewitched by the self-hypnotic trance. It is that portion which believes implicitly in the given specifications of the hypnotist, and as such it will be last to leave the trance.
quotes.translation: 但你所知的外在自我，是整体自体中被指派维持物质操控的部分，它仅通过那些物质装备、那些外在感官进行感知。当它察觉自身领域岌岌可危时，便会武装反抗。这个自我，正是整体自体允许其完全或几乎完全被自我催眠状态所迷惑的那一小部分。它绝对相信催眠师给出的特定指令，因此将是最后脱离催眠状态的部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Entities, if they prefer, and under various conditions, when they become acquainted with others, may introduce to each other if they so prefer, various portions of their personalities. These various portions will be able to help each other and to aid in development. Take for example entity A and entity B. Self A personality of entity A may get along quite nicely with B's self B. As self 2 and B's self 2 may not get along well at all, so the entities will shift to those personalities which have the greatest rapport, and use them to establish a relationship.
quotes.translation: 当存有们彼此熟识后，若它们愿意，在不同条件下可向对方介绍自身人格的不同部分。这些不同部分将能相互协助、促进发展。以存有A与存有B为例：存有A的自体A人格可能与B的自体B相处融洽，而自体2与B的自体2可能完全不合，因此存有们会转向最具共鸣的人格，并以此建立关系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I have told you that through many existences you don egos for the purposes set by the inner self. It is necessary of course that you believe in your role. The difficulty is that you must believe in your role, and yet also realize that it is a role that you, the inner self, have chosen. Otherwise your connections with the inner self are nearly forgotten. The ego forgets that it is only a part of a much more complete personality structure, and therefore cuts itself off from abilities and insights that are necessary, even for its own comfort and stability.
quotes.translation: 我曾告诉过你，在多次存在中，你穿上自我（ego）是为了内在自己所设定的目的。当然，你必须相信自己的角色。困难在于，你既要相信这个角色，又要意识到这是你——内在自己——所选择的角色。否则，你与内在自己的联系几乎会被遗忘。自我（ego）忘记了自己只是一个更完整人格结构的一部分，因此切断了与必要的能力和洞见的联系，而这些对其自身的舒适和稳定也是必需的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You must again realize that we speak of the self as being so divided only for simplicity's sake. While the self is whole, it is however compartmentalized for efficiency's sake, but beneath consciousness the doors are open. Again, the conscious self is most necessary. However it cannot be stressed too strongly that consciousness is merely a state of focus, and not a self.
quotes.translation: 你必须再次认识到，我们之所以将自我（self）说成是分裂的，只是为了简化起见。虽然自我（self）是完整的，但为了效率，它被划分成不同部分，然而在意识之下，门是敞开的。同样，意识自我（conscious self）是最必要的。然而，再怎么强调也不为过：意识仅仅是一种聚焦状态，而不是一个自我（self）。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now I could indeed be Ruburt's number six self, you see. I am not, but I could be. It is entirely possible however, using Ruburt as an example, for Ruburts number six self, to communicate with Ruburts number one self; these communications sifting through the intervening selves however, and unfortunately. Now these various times of Priestley's and Dunnes have much in common with the planes of which I am speaking in our discussions, and the value fulfillment of our material is akin to Priestley's insistence on depth within any given moment.
quotes.translation: 你看，我确实可能是鲁柏的第六自我（self）。我不是，但我可能是。然而，以鲁柏为例，完全有可能让鲁柏的第六自我（self）与鲁柏的第一自我（self）沟通；但这些沟通会经过中间自我的筛选，不幸的是。现在，普里斯特利和邓恩的各种时间概念，与我们讨论中提到的层面有很多共同之处，而我们材料的价值完成（value fulfillment）类似于普里斯特利对任何给定时刻深度的坚持。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: But beyond this life, and before this life, and forming this life, there are identities and there are realities and these are not dead. Identity as experience is intimate. It is a psychic reality but it exists electromagnetically. Identity, the self that you are, is made up of the selves that you were and, my dear friends, the selves that you shall be. The selves that you were still exist. The selves that you shall be already exist. The lives that you have lived, in your terms, are still being lived. There is no past, present or future.
quotes.translation: 但在这生之外，在这生之前，并形成这生的，是身份（identities）和实相（realities），而这些并非死寂。身份作为体验是亲密的。它是一种心灵实相，但以电磁方式存在。身份，即你所是的自我（self），由你曾是的自我（selves）和我亲爱的朋友们，由你将是自我（selves）所组成。你曾是的自我（selves）仍然存在。你将是自我（selves）已经存在。你曾活过的生命，按你的说法，仍在被活过。没有过去、现在或未来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The fact is that the whole self is constantly experiencing data from all of the inner senses. The inner ego is of course aware of this. The subconscious is sometimes aware of this, and the outer ego is aware of very little. I have explained the reasons for this in past sessions. The outer ego must concentrate much of its energy toward survival in and manipulation of the outer camouflage world. This world has already been created by the inner self, and its continuing existence is determined by the constant vigilance of the inner self.
quotes.translation: 事实上，整个自我（whole self）不断体验来自所有内在感官（inner senses）的数据。内在自我（inner ego）当然知道这一点。潜意识有时知道这一点，而外在自我（outer ego）则知之甚少。我在过去的课程中解释过原因。外在自我（outer ego）必须将大部分能量集中于在外在伪装世界（outer camouflage world）中的生存和操控。这个世界已由内在自己（inner self）创造，其持续存在由内在自己（inner self）的持续警觉决定。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: When the outer ego, from the surface of its consciousness, reflects the outer world, it sees reflections of the inner ego which are the images within its own eye; and as the self creates matter subconsciously within its own eye, and as the self creates matter subconsciously and not consciously, and as the self creates matter in line with inner and not outer expectations, so then does the ego, in viewing the material universe, come face to face with the face of its own inner ego; and the outer ego cannot escape from this inner self.
quotes.translation: 当外在自我（outer ego）从其意识表面反映外在世界时，它看到的是内在自我（inner ego）的反映，这些反映是其自身眼中的影像；正如自我（self）在自身眼中潜意识地创造物质，而非有意识地创造物质，也正如自我（self）根据内在而非外在期望创造物质，那么自我（ego）在观看物质宇宙时，便与自身内在自我（inner ego）的面孔面对面；而外在自我（outer ego）无法逃离这个内在自己（inner self）。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: This out ego, among its purposes, has the duty of expressing not only itself but to a lesser extent those various hidden personalities which compose the subconscious. In larger terms and in more comprehensive terms, the whole self, the whole field of the complete subconscious and inner self, reaches back to the entity. There is no end to the past projection or existence of the subconscious of any given self; and though it is not understood in your field, there is no end to the forward thrust of the subconscious of any given self.
quotes.translation: 这个外在自我，在其诸多目的中，不仅负有表达自身的职责，也在较小程度上表达构成潜意识的那些各种隐藏人格。从更宏大、更全面的角度来看，整个自我、完整潜意识与内在自我的整个领域，都回溯到存有。任何特定自我的潜意识，其过去的投射或存在都没有尽头；尽管在你们的领域里不被理解，任何特定自我的潜意识向前推进也没有尽头。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: As I have said, there are gentle, imperceptible gradations between what is called self and what is called not-self. Your idea, or psychologists' idea of environment for example, will come close to what I mean. The self indeed however reaches out in many ways to form, mold and construct his own environment, even as it in turn reaches out to affect his core of self.
quotes.translation: 如我所说，在所谓自我与所谓非自我之间，存在着温和而难以察觉的渐变。例如，你们或心理学家对环境的观念，将接近我的意思。自我确实以多种方式向外延伸，去形成、塑造并构建自己的环境，正如环境反过来也向外延伸，影响自我的核心。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: There is no battle between the intuitive self and the conscious mind. There only seems to be when the individual refuses to face all the information that is available in his conscious mind. (Pause.) Sometimes it seems easier to avoid the frequent readjustments in behavior that self-examination requires. In such cases an individual collects many secondhand beliefs. Some contradict each other; the signals given to the body and to the inner self are not smoothly flowing or clear-cut, but a muddied jumble of counter-directions.
quotes.translation: 直觉自我与意识心之间并无冲突。只有当个体拒绝面对其意识心中所有可得信息时，冲突才看似存在。（停顿。）有时，回避自我检视所要求的频繁行为调整似乎更容易。在这种情况下，个体收集了许多二手信念。有些信念相互矛盾；传递给身体和内在自我的信号并非流畅清晰，而是一团混乱、方向相反的泥泞杂烩。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The keystone here is that you are only aware of your own constructions. This is most important. You truly construct your own environment, and it in turn then has its influence upon you. When I speak of environment I include for simplicity's sake that which is close to the self in its nebulous form, but which is still called not-self. Theoretically, this even personal environment reaches indefinitely. However, we will speak of it usually as including those rather nebulous gradations that seem to be between self and not-self.
quotes.translation: 这里的关键在于，你只意识到自己的建构。这一点极为重要。你确实构建了自己的环境，而环境反过来又对你产生影响。为简便起见，当我谈论环境时，包括那些以模糊形式贴近自我、但仍被称为非自我的部分。理论上，即便是个人环境也无限延伸。然而，我们通常将其视为包括那些看似介于自我与非自我之间的相当模糊的渐变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Any dream is experienced differently by these various aspects of the self. There is not one objective dream that is merely perceived in various fashions. The various levels of the self create their own dreams, which do have meanings to all layers of the personality. But you cannot think of a dream as a concrete block that is for example chipped off in pieces that then apply to these imaginary levels. All of these aspects of the self are so intertwined that arbitrary distinctions must be made for you simply to explain them.
quotes.translation: 任何梦境都被自我的这些不同面向以不同方式体验。并不存在一个仅以不同方式被感知的客观梦境。自我的各个层次创造自己的梦境，这些梦境确实对人格的所有层面都有意义。但你不能把梦境想象成一个具体的块状物，比如被敲成碎片，然后适用于这些假想的层面。自我的所有这些面向都如此紧密交织，以至于必须做出人为区分，仅仅是为了向你们解释。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Through dreams the self communicates with the self, and with all layers of the self. For the self is not one concrete thing. The self has no boundaries, the self is not limited. Consciousness is the direction in which the self looks, again, but the ego is not aware of the whole self. The ego is not even familiar with the context or the meaning of your own dreams.
quotes.translation: 通过梦境，自我与自我沟通，并与自我的所有层面沟通。因为自我并非一个具体的东西。自我没有边界，自我不受限制。意识是自我注视的方向，再次强调，但自我并不知晓整个自我。自我甚至不熟悉你自己梦境的背景或含义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The self that sits in class is not the self that wonders in a dream state, and the self that wonders in a dream state is, my dear friends, far more educated than the, self that sits in the classroom. The self you call yourself, what does it know? To whom must it listen? The self that you call yourself knows relatively little. It is perhaps 16 years old. Indeed, the inner identity knows, and the inner identity knows that it knows. All of you are on a threshold. Uncounted millions have been at that threshold.
quotes.translation: 坐在教室里的自己并非在梦境中漫游的自己，而亲爱的朋友们，在梦境中漫游的那个自己，远比坐在教室里的那个自己更有学识。你们称之为"我"的这个自己，它知道什么？它必须听从谁？你们称之为"我"的这个自己知道得相对很少。它大概只有十六岁。确实，内在身份知道，而且内在身份知道它知道。你们所有人都站在一个门槛上。无数的人曾站在那个门槛上。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Therefore, consciousness-of-self can appear with or without the existence of an ego. Consciousness-of-self is an attribute then of all physical species, regardless of their classification. Personality, human personality, is simply the name given to this class of self, as applied and seen within human beings. Personality changes and acts upon all other action. Personality, then, can be seen to operate as a field of action in identity; but identity that is conscious of its relation to action as a whole.
quotes.translation: 因此，自我意识可以在有或没有自我存在的情况下出现。自我意识是所有物理物种的属性，无论其分类如何。人格，人类人格，只是赋予这类自我的名称，应用于人类身上并被人类所见。人格改变并作用于所有其他行动。因此，人格可以被视为身份中的一个行动场域；但这是意识到自身与整体行动关系的身份。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now this event X is only one of a literally numberless amount of probable events which the conscious self could experience. For its purposes however the conscious self chooses this particular event X. But again, this event X, until the conscious self experiences it, is only one of many other probable events, different in no basic manner from the others. It becomes real, actual and different from those other probable events, only when it is experienced by our conscious self, or by this conscious self.
quotes.translation: 现在这个事件X只是意识自我可能体验的无数可能事件中的一个。然而，为了其目的，意识自我选择了这个特定的事件X。但再次强调，在意识自我体验它之前，这个事件X只是众多其他可能事件中的一个，与其他事件在基本性质上并无不同。只有当被我们的意识自我或这个意识自我体验时，它才变得真实、实际，并与其他可能事件不同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: A portion of you, therefore, is aware of each and every dream encounter and experience. Dreams are no more hallucinatory than your physical life is. Your waking physical self is the dreamer, as far as your dreaming self is concerned: You are the dreamer it sends on its way. Your daily experiences are the dreams that it dreams, so when you look at your dreaming self or consider it, you do so with a highly prejudiced eye, taking it for granted that your "reality" is real, and its reality is illusion.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们的一部分知晓每一个梦境遭遇和体验。梦并不比你们的物质生活更具幻觉性。就你们的做梦自己而言，你们醒时的物质自己才是做梦者：你们是它派遣上路的做梦者。你们日常的经历就是它所做的梦，所以当你们审视自己的做梦自己或思考它时，你们带着高度偏见的眼光，理所当然地认为你们的"实相"是真实的，而它的实相是幻象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Selves are not destroyed. They change into other selves, and yet are still themselves, for each new self is also the previous self which changed through acting upon itself. There will be no huge contraction of action back into itself, in those terms. There may very well be a conscious realization of each self, that it is a part of the original action self.
quotes.translation: 自己不会被毁灭。它们转变为其他自己，却仍然是它们自己，因为每个新自己也是通过作用于自身而改变的先前自己。用那些术语来说，不会有行动大幅收缩回自身的情况。很可能每个自己都会意识到，它是原始行动自己的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: "When Ruburt left the church, the concept of the sinful self was still there, but the methods that earlier served to relieve its pressures were no longer effectively present. The concept was shifted over to the flawed self of scientific vintage. Science has no sacraments. Its only methods of dealing with such guilt involve standard psychoanalytic counseling--which itself deepens the dilemma, for counseling itself is based upon the idea that the inner self is a reservoir of savage impulses. Period.
quotes.translation: "当鲁柏离开教会时，有罪自己的概念仍然存在，但早期用来缓解其压力的方法已不再有效存在。这个概念被转移到了科学年代的有缺陷自己上。科学没有圣礼。它处理此类罪疚的唯一方法涉及标准的精神分析咨询——这本身加深了困境，因为咨询本身基于这样的理念：内在自己是野蛮冲动的蓄水池。句号。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 11. When Mass Events is published, see Session 835 for February 7, 1979, in Chapter 6.

quotes.text: Channels, psychological and psychic, always exist, sending communications back and forth through the various levels of the self, and the ego accepts necessary information and data from inner portions of the personality without question. Its position in fact depends in a large manner upon this unquestioning acceptance of inner data. The ego, in other words, the "exterior" self that you think of as your self -- that portion of you maintains its safety and its seeming command precisely because inner layers of your own personality constantly uphold it, keep the physical body operating, and maintain communications with the multitudinous stimuli that come both from outside conditions and inside conditions. The soul or entity is not diminished but expanded through reincarnations, through existence and experience in probable realities -- something that I will explain later.
quotes.translation: 通道——心理与灵性的通道——始终存在，在自我的各个层面之间来回传递讯息，而自我毫无质疑地接受来自人格内在部分的必要资讯与资料。事实上，自我的地位在很大程度上依赖于对这种内在资料的不加质疑的接纳。换言之，你们视为"自我"的那个"外在"自我——它之所以能维持安全与表面上的掌控，正是因为你们人格的内在层面不断支撑着它，维持肉体的运作，并与来自外在环境与内在条件的无数刺激保持沟通。灵魂或存有并不会因转世、因在可能实相中的存在与经验而缩减，反而会扩展——这一点我稍后会解释。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At least partially because of their brutal history, Iranians--Persians--are strongly self-centered; preservation of the self is given an overriding impetus. The world is seen as being full of peril. Causality, the interrelation of cause and effect, is often ignored or misunderstood in the Iranian quest for immediate advantage. Influence counts for much more than obligation; the concept of long-term mutual trust is seen as basically adversarial; goodwill means little. Yet, such egocentric characteristics often are sublimated into the seemingly contradictory practice of martyrdom--the two are united within the Iranian interpretation of Moslem theology. In a land ruled by a body of theocratic law the needs of the country must ultimately prevail, as in the case of attack from without, say. There is no area in which Moslemic precepts do not apply.
quotes.translation: 至少部分由于他们残酷的历史，伊朗人——波斯人——具有强烈的自我中心倾向；自我保护被赋予压倒性的动力。世界被视为充满危险。因果关系——因与果的相互关联——在伊朗人追求即时利益时常常被忽视或误解。影响力远比义务重要；长期互信的概念基本上被视为对抗性的；善意几乎毫无意义。然而，这种自我中心的特质常常被升华到看似矛盾的殉道实践中——这两者在伊朗对穆斯林神学的诠释中结合在一起。在一个由神权法律体系统治的土地上，国家的需求最终必须占上风，例如在遭受外部攻击的情况下。没有哪个领域是穆斯林教条不适用的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Outward extensions of the self can be more clearly objectified, the concentration at the outward extensions being less, and identity correlations being kept in more concentrated areas within the boundaries of the physical self. The eye sees but it cannot see itself. In like manner the self is, but is not consciously able to examine that which it is. Therefore man must take his abilities and travel inward, since going outward will not allow him to perceive the inner portions of himself.
quotes.translation: 自我的外在延伸可以更清晰地客观化，因为对外在延伸的专注较少，而身份关联则更集中在物质自我边界内的区域。眼睛能看见，却无法看见自身。同样，自我存在，却无法有意识地检视它之所是。因此，人必须带着他的能力向内旅行，因为向外走无法让他感知到自身的内在部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The ego feared for its position, frightened that it would dissolve back into the inner self from which it came. Yet in its emergence it provided the inner self with a new kind of feedback, a different view not only of itself; but through this, the inner self was able to glimpse possibilities of development of which it had not previously been aware. In your terms, by the time of Christ, the ego was sure enough of its position so that the projected picture of God could begin to change.
quotes.translation: 自我曾为其地位而恐惧，害怕它会消融回它所源自的内在自我。然而，在它的浮现过程中，它为内在自我提供了一种新的反馈，一种不仅对自身的不同视角；而且通过这一点，内在自我得以瞥见它先前未曾意识到的发展可能性。用你们的说法，到基督的时代，自我已足够确信其地位，以至于投射出的上帝形象开始改变。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It should be fairly easy to understand now how the second dilemma evolved from the first. I have said that the second dilemma resulted in, and constantly results in, consciousness of self. Now. Consciousness of self is not the same thing as consciousness of ego self. Consciousness of self is still consciousness directly connected with action.
quotes.translation: 现在应该相当容易理解第二个困境是如何从第一个演变而来的。我曾说过，第二个困境导致了，并持续导致着自我意识。现在，自我意识与自我（ego self）的意识并非同一回事。自我意识仍然是直接与行动相连的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The self then, unknown to the ego, perceives itself in a vast variety of experiences, and in, indeed, a vast number of realities. Each of these so-called realities, for one blends into the other, could be termed, or viewed as, a separate field. Each is therefore composed of the characteristic perception patterns that happen to lie within it, and these so-called minor fields could then be termed other selves, or minor selves, from the standpoint of the self that we are considering.
quotes.translation: 因此，自我——不为自我所知——在各种各样的经验中，甚至是在大量的实相中感知自身。这些所谓的实相，每一个都与其他实相相互交融，可以被视为或看作一个独立的场域。因此，每个场域都由恰好位于其中的特征性感知模式构成，而从我们正在考虑的自我角度来看，这些所谓的次要场域便可被称为其他自我或次要自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The core of the self is obviously aware of all realities to some extent. If portions of the self did not coincide then it would be impossible for the whole personality to ever operate as a unit. Here we simply have spirals, so to speak, of ever moving actions that compose the whole self. But portions of these spirals coincide, and in this analogy the spirals of action not only have those dimensions which you understand, but other dimensions with which you are not yet familiar.
quotes.translation: 自我的核心显然在某种程度上觉察到所有实相。如果自我的各个部分不相重合，那么整个人格就永远无法作为一个整体运作。这里我们不妨说，存在着构成整个自我的不断运动的行动螺旋。但这些螺旋的部分区域是重合的，在这个类比中，行动螺旋不仅具有你所理解的维度，还具有你尚未熟悉的其他维度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Some of these may involve the thoughts of what you would call a reincarnational self, focused in another period of history as you know it. You may instead, "pick up" an event in which a probable self is involved, according to your inclination, your psychic suppleness, your curiosity, your desire for knowledge. In other words, you may become aware of a far greater reality than you now know, use abilities that you do not realize you possess, know beyond all doubt that your own consciousness and identity is independent of the world in which you now focus your primary attention. If all of that were not true, I would not be writing this book and you would not be reading it.
quotes.translation: 其中一些可能涉及你所称的转世自我的思想，聚焦在你所知的另一段历史时期。根据你的倾向、你的心灵柔韧性、你的好奇心、你对知识的渴望，你反而可能"捕捉"到涉及一个可能自我的事件。换句话说，你可能会意识到一个远比你现在所知更宏大的实相，运用你尚未意识到自己拥有的能力，确凿无疑地知道你的意识和身份独立于你目前主要关注的世界。如果这一切都不是真的，我就不会写这本书，你也不会读它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All layers of the personality are indeed conscious layers. They simply operate in the manner of compartments, so that often one portion of the self is not aware of other portions.
