source_raw.title: (First, after holding session 805 we took a six-week break from sessions of any kind. We didn't plan to do this; it just developed, and we eventually realized that it did so through Jane's simple need for a change in routine. We had plenty of other things to do: I was still occupied daily with writing notes and appendixes for Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality; on June 4 Jane received the page proofs for Cézanne, and began correcting them for the printer; on the 14th of the month "our" contractor began converting half of our garage into a writing room for Jane, and adding a large back porch [see the end of Note 2 for Session 801]. All of that building activity was much noisier and more disruptive than the work had been for the front porch, and forced some changes in our schedules, including more night work, as we manipulated around those distractions.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: "A belief in a 'god who provides,' by whatever name, is indeed a psychological requirement for the good health of the body and mind. Ruburt did not want to face such issues. (Long pause.) He felt that they opened the door to all of organized religion's psychological quicksand of emotionalism. The sinful-self material is doing its work, opening the necessary doorways of desire and intent. When Ruburt has typed those small later poems, the path will seem much clearer to him. The innocent self is being uncovered.
quotes.translation: 相信一位"提供一切的上帝"——无论其名称为何——确实是身心健康所必需的心理需求。鲁柏不愿面对这类议题。(长停顿)他觉得这扇门一旦打开，就会让所有组织化宗教的情感主义心理流沙涌入。"有罪自我"的资料正在发挥作用，开启欲望与意图的必要通道。当鲁柏打完那些后期短诗时，道路在他眼中会清晰得多。纯真自我正在被揭露。

source_raw.title: 7. In Chapter 2 of Dreams, in Volume 1, see Note 1 for Session 885, which we held on October 24, 1979. Through a series of misunderstandings, the people at Ankh-Hermes had published an abridged translation of Seth Speaks without having permission for the cutting from Jane and me. We still feel regret that the company had to go to all of that extra expense in order to publish a second edition of the book. I also wrote that "all concerned must wait for at least another year before a full-length version of Seth Speaks will be published in the Dutch language." Actually, almost two years passed before we received our copies of the new edition.

quotes.text: The inner self is in a state of constant growth. The inner portion of each man, therefore, projected this knowledge outward. The need, the psychological and spiritual need of the species, demanded both interior and exterior alterations of great import. Qualities of mercy and understanding that had been buried could now surface. Not only privately but en masse they surged up, adding a new impetus and giving a natural "new" direction -- beginning to call all portions of the self, as it knew itself, together.
quotes.translation: 内在自我处于持续成长的状态。因此每个人的内在部分将这种知识投射向外。物种的心理与灵性需求，要求进行内外兼具的重大变革。被埋藏的慈悲与理解品质如今得以浮现。它们不仅私下涌现，更集体迸发，注入新动力并赋予自然的"新"方向——开始召唤自我（以其所认知的自身）的所有部分聚合。

quotes.text: It helps form the new individual, and then it must withdraw. The new self unit must be free and not hampered by the demands that could otherwise be put upon it. The new individual has a deeply buried memory of its past lives, but the personal consciousness of the last reincarnated self must not be superimposed upon this new identity. The new personality, in its small astral body, does visit with other portions of the entire identity. It is even given lessons of a kind, but it is very much its own self.
quotes.translation: 它协助形成新个体，随后必须退隐。新的自我单元必须保持自由，不受可能强加于它的要求所束缚。新个体深埋着对前世记忆，但最后转世自我的个人意识不得叠加于这个新身份之上。新人格在其微小的星光体中，确实会与整体身份的其他部分交流。它甚至接受某种形式的课程，但始终保有鲜明的自我特质。

quotes.text: The inner self and the deeper layers of the subconscious are relatively free however from the egos false gods, and it is for this reason that precognitions are at all possible. For these layers of the self are simply able to perceive a larger portion of action than the ego is able or willing to perceive. As a rule, the ego will not even accept the information that is derived in such a manner, for to do so would be to deny those artificial precepts of continuity upon which it feels its dominance rests.
quotes.translation: 然而内在自我与潜意识深层相对不受自我虚假神祇的束缚，正因如此，预知才成为可能。因为这些自我层面能够感知比自我所能或愿意感知的更广阔的行动片段。通常，自我甚至拒绝接受以这种方式获得的信息，因为这样做将否定那些它认为其统治根基所在的连续性人为准则。

quotes.text: The self, in this manner, looks about. The direction in which the self looks is not the self. In dreams the self looks elsewhere, and the "I" is a conscious "I", and the working ability is tremendous. The inner self perceives realities that it observes in many directions, being free from the intense focus within limited directions of camouflage existence.
quotes.translation: 自我以这种方式环顾四周。自我注视的方向并非自我本身。在梦境中，自我望向别处，而"我"是觉知的"我"，运作能力极为强大。内在自我感知它在多重方向观察到的实相，因为它不受限于伪装存在的有限方向性聚焦。

quotes.text: When you start out by trying to be practical in cold terms you rarely succeed, because you close yourself away from what does not seem to be practical in your terms. But your terms are not the only terms that apply. The inner self, and I will make these differentiations for you clearly either now or later, the inner self is nourished by many springs. To cut off one is a danger. To cut off many is disastrous, and prevents any sort of practicality, since half of your abilities will not be used.
quotes.translation: 当你一开始就试图以冷酷务实的方式行事时，很少能成功，因为你将自己封闭在那些看似不符合你标准的事物之外。但你的标准并非唯一适用的标准。内在自我——我现在或稍后会为你清晰区分这些概念——由多重源泉滋养。切断一个源泉是危险的。切断多个源泉则是灾难性的，且会阻碍任何形式的务实性，因为你一半的能力将无法被运用。

quotes.text: If however we changed our arbitrary boundary points, then the minor selves at either end would now seem to be portions of other selves. For practical purposes it may be said that a self is composed of a gestalt of perception patterns, within which a fairly constant efficiency is maintained. This is the best definition I can give you at this time.
quotes.translation: 然而，若我们改变任意设定的边界点，则两端的小我便会成为其他自我的一部分。就实用目的而言，可以说自我是由感知模式的完形所构成，在此范围内维持着相当恒定的效能。这是我目前能给出的最佳定义。

quotes.text: From the viewpoint of this emerging self you can view to some extent the system in which the earlier self was mainly imprisoned. Now I speak of imprisonment. I do not speak in terms of compulsory confinement however. Your perceptions simply kept you where you were. You could not clearly see even where you were, for the dimensions were not clear to you from the inside. You could not scale the wall, so to speak. You had to grow taller, if you will forgive me for using another analogy.
quotes.translation: 从这个正在浮现的自我视角，你可以在某种程度上看清先前自我主要受困的系统。我在此谈及"受困"，并非指强制性的禁锢。你的感知只是将你固着于原处。你甚至无法清晰看见自己所处的位置，因为从内部无法看清维度。可以说你无法翻越围墙——必须让自己长得更高，请容我再用一个比喻。

quotes.text: Therefore, in the dream state communication is possible between all portions of the self. The personality appears in its truest state if it seems, in dreams, that you are free of space and time. It is indeed because the basic self is free of space and time. If you appear to hear voices out of the past, if you seem to see into the future, it is because the dream state is a more or less faithful approximation of a basic reality in which your time and space simply do not exist.
quotes.translation: 因此在梦境状态中，自我的所有部分都能相互沟通。当你在梦中看似摆脱时空束缚时，人格会呈现最真实的状态。这确实是因为基本自我本就超越时空。若你听见来自过去的声音，或预见未来景象，皆因梦境状态或多或少忠实反映了时空不存在的根本实相。

quotes.text: There is much here to be studied about personality. From your viewpoint the information available to you can be practically endless. There is much to be learned about perceiving other realities. Trance states are only a beginning. The self is made of action. Teaching the self to expand is most important, and the ways are nor difficult... this is what makes the experiment interesting. This is not beyond human abilities. The structure of the personality makes this possible.
quotes.translation: 关于人格的研究大有可为。从你的视角来看，可获取的信息近乎无穷。感知其他实相的方法值得深入探索。出神状态仅是开端。自我由行动构成。教导自我扩展至关重要，方法并不困难...这正是实验的趣味所在。这并未超越人类能力范畴。人格结构使这一切成为可能。

quotes.text: The identity, the basic identity, of these portions of the self, are carried by what you could compare to the subconscious that you know. This is difficult, but listen: in these portions of the self it is the subconscious that carries the burden of identity, and it is the ego whose experiences are of a dreamlike, plastic nature. I use these terms to make the point clear. Do not let them confuse you. These portions of the self would seem topsy-turvy to you for this reason.
quotes.translation: 这些自我部分的身份认同——基本身份认同——由你可类比为已知潜意识的载体所承载。这有些难以理解，但请细听：在这些自我部分中，是潜意识承载着身份认同的重担，而自我则经历着如梦似幻、可塑性的体验。我使用这些术语是为了阐明要点，切勿让它们混淆你。正因如此，这些自我部分在你看来会显得颠倒错乱。

quotes.text: Your reincarnational selves have as many probable lives as you do. Your beliefs and actions in your present alter "their" experiences, as each of them, in their presents, change yours. If you see these reincarnational selves as one entity, then this becomes quite natural. The whole self is changed by all of its comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 你的转世自我与你拥有同样多的可能人生。你当下的信念与行动会改变"他们"的体验，正如每个转世自我在其当下改变你的体验。若将这些转世自我视为一个整体，这一切便显得顺理成章。整体自我因所有理解而不断改变。

quotes.text: It goes without saying, again, that these communications follow in the lines of emotional attraction. For one thing, there are also individual differences which operate, so that any given personality will be more open to particular influences, and will tend to ignore others. For while there are no boundaries as such about the self, for practical purposes the self is a core of characteristic actions and reactions with which all environment is manipulated and handled.
quotes.translation: 不言而喻，这些沟通遵循情感吸引的路径。此外，个体差异也在运作，使得特定人格对某些影响更为开放，而倾向于忽略其他影响。虽然自我本身并无明确界限，但就实际目的而言，自我是特性化行动与反应的核心，所有环境都通过它被操纵和处理。

quotes.text: Value fulfillment opens the many eyes of the self to its various portions. It enables the self to expand, to join in a gestalt with other selves. It is only your ego which leads you to believe that such an expansion would result in a lessening of consciousness on the one hand, or an invasion of other selves on the other.
quotes.translation: 价值完成开启自我的众多眼睛，使其看见自身的各个部分。它使自我得以扩展，与其他自我形成完形。唯有你的自我会让你相信，这种扩展一方面会导致意识的减弱，另一方面会侵犯其他自我。

quotes.text: You may also be part of a self operating within other fields, and operating also within another system of units. The inner self operates as a relay station, as a reference point for the various seemingly disconnected selves. It is only through contact with the inner self that knowledge of the whole self can be found.
quotes.translation: 你也可能是在其他领域运作的自我的一部分，同时也在另一个单位系统中运作。内在自我充当着中继站，作为各种看似不相连的自我之参考点。唯有通过与内在自我的接触，才能获得对整体自我的认知。

quotes.text: (11:40) To some extent there is also an assault upon simple creaturehood. Its images and experience, furthermore, are seldom forgotten, and the so-called new ego is born with the memory of their imprint. Some psychologists like to say that you cry out unconsciously against the natural method of your birth.* But here you have the situation where a self is faced with its own annihilation, while another "self" arises after conscious participation with its death.
quotes.translation: (11:40) 在某种程度上，也存在对单纯生物性的攻击。此外，其意象与经验很少被遗忘，所谓的新自我带着这些印记的记忆诞生。有些心理学家喜欢说，你无意识地抗拒自己出生的自然方式。但这里的情况是，一个自我面临自身的湮灭，而另一个"自我"在意识参与其死亡后浮现。

quotes.text: Now: Take another photograph of yourself at a different age than the first one you chose. Ask yourself simply: "Am I looking at the same person?" How familiar or how strange is this second photograph? How does it differ from the first one you picked this evening? What similarities are there that unite both photographs in your mind? What experiences did you have when each photograph was taken? What ways did you think of following in one picture that were not followed in the other one? Those directions were pursued. If they were not pursued by the self you recognize, then they were by a self that is probable in your terms. In your mind follow what directions that self would have taken, as you think of such events. If you find a line of development that you now wish you had pursued, but had not, then think deeply about the ways in which those activities could now fit into the framework of your officially accepted life.2 Such musings, with desire -- backed up by common sense -- can bring about intersection points in probabilities that cause a fresh realignment of the deep elements of the psyche. In such ways probable events can be attracted to your current living structure.
quotes.translation: 现在：拍摄一张与你所选第一张照片不同年龄的自己。简单地问自己："我看的是同一个人吗？"这第二张照片有多熟悉或陌生？它与你今晚选的第一张照片有何不同？在你心中，有哪些相似之处将两张照片联结？每张照片拍摄时你经历了什么？在一张照片中你曾想遵循、而在另一张中未遵循的方向是什么？那些方向被追寻了。如果它们未被你认可的自我追寻，那么它们就是被你所谓的可能自我所追寻。在你心中，想象那个自我本会采取的方向，如同你思考这些事件一般。如果你发现一条你现在希望曾追寻但未追寻的发展路线，那么深入思考这些活动现在如何能融入你官方认可的生活框架。这种带着欲望的沉思——辅以常识——能在概率中创造交叉点，导致心灵深层元素的全新重组。通过这种方式，可能事件能被吸引到你当前的生活结构中。

quotes.text: You know where physical reality is, then, on the dial of your multidimensional television set. While focused within that living scene you can learn to travel through it, leaving the "surface" picture intact and whole. In a way you program yourself, going about your daily duties as conscientiously and effectively as usual -- but at the same time you discover an additional portion of your own reality. This does not diminish the physical self. Instead, in fact, it enriches it. You discover that the psyche has many aspects. While fully enjoying the physical aspect you find that there is some part of you left over, so to speak; and that part can travel into other realities. It can also then return, bringing the physically oriented self "snapshots" of its journeys. These snapshots are usually interpreted in terms of your home program. Otherwise, they might make no sense to the physical self.
quotes.translation: 那么，你知道物质实相在你们多维电视机的哪个频道上。当你专注于那个活生生的场景时，你可以学会穿越它，同时保持"表面"画面完整无缺。从某种意义上说，你为自己编程，像往常一样认真有效地履行日常职责——但与此同时，你发现了自己实相的额外部分。这并不会削弱物质自我。事实上，它反而丰富了物质自我。你发现心灵有许多面向。在充分享受物质面向的同时，你会发现还有一部分自己"剩余"下来，可以这么说；而那一部分可以旅行进入其他实相。它也可以返回，为面向物质的自我带来它旅程的"快照"。这些快照通常根据你本来的程序来解读。否则，它们对物质自我可能毫无意义。

But there was even more creative expression to come through Jane this evening, not only in the session itself of course, but after it, as I try to explain in the concluding notes.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: We're not against reincarnation, then, only careful about our beliefs concerning it. Within the context of my discussion, reincarnation is Seth's historical version of his counterpart concept, which is that each of us is physically connected with certain other males and females who are living at the same time we are, and who are exploring physical life from a variety of viewpoints in ways that no one physical self could possibly match. This means that each reincarnational self has its own cluster of counterpart selves within its own time period, and that all are interconnected on nonphysical levels, joining together like magical gears meshing in constantly changing patterns across time and reality. And once one understands ideas of reincarnation and counterparts in these terms, it becomes difficult to think of one without the other, so inevitable do they appear to be.
quotes.translation: 因此，我们并不反对转世，只是对我们关于转世的信念保持谨慎。在我讨论的语境中，转世是赛斯对其"对等人物"概念的历史版本诠释，即我们每个人在生理上都与某些同时代生活的其他男性和女性相连，他们以各种不同的视角探索物质生命，其方式没有任何一个物质自我能够企及。这意味着每个转世自我在其自身的时间段内都有自己的一群对等自我，所有这些在非物质层面上相互连接，像魔法齿轮一样啮合在一起，在时间和实相中不断变化着模式。一旦人们以这些术语理解了转世和对等人物的概念，就很难只想到其中一个而忽略另一个，它们看起来是如此不可避免。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I referred to a "successful" progression because reaching into the future is evidently much more difficult. By its very nature a future life cannot be proven--records checked, and so forth. Anything goes. Jane and I have read of many systems designed to regress the individual to past lives. Often such "trips" are mediated by hypnosis. It can even happen spontaneously, and I had a most exhilarating glimpse of a past life of my own that way. (See Session 721 in Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality.) However, neither of us have had such an outright encounter with a future self--that we know of. I'd say that under hypnosis the urge to fantasize the future lives must be a tempting one; but what's the explanation for achieving little more than a formless future state while "under," no matter how hard one tries? The failure to get there, to turn time around, could be taken as a sign of resistance on the part of the present self. (Or even a past self or selves, but that's too complicated a subject to go into here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Nevertheless it also partakes of the strange unity that unites all personalities beneath the camouflage pattern, while at the same time it is only partially connected to the camouflage pattern. In some respects art creations are a meeting of the dream world and the world of camouflage patterns, but in a deeper way art creations represent the appearance or materialization in the actual element of physical time of inner realities. That is, the inner individualistic self forces its vision and knowledge into the world of camouflage pattern, giving its dreams a physical reality denied to the usual dream. And here the use of energy for this purpose is conscious, that is the strong hidden self actually makes use of the camouflage conscious self and molds the two into a reality that combines two planes. This I hope will become clear as you read it over.
quotes.translation: 然而，它也参与了那种奇特的统一性，这种统一性将所有的人格统一在伪装模式之下，同时它又只与伪装模式部分相连。在某些方面，艺术创作是梦境世界与伪装模式世界的交汇，但更深入地说，艺术创作代表了内在实相在物质时间的实际元素中的显现或物质化。也就是说，内在的个体化自我将其愿景和知识强行注入伪装模式世界，赋予其梦境一种通常梦境所不具备的物质实相。在这里，为此目的的能量使用是有意识的，即强大的隐藏自我实际上利用了伪装的意识自我，并将两者塑造成一个结合了两个层面的实相。我希望当你反复阅读时，这一点会变得清晰。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: As inner energy forms more complicated gestalts through the processes that I have earlier explained, then inner energy continues its communication with the physical matter that it creates about it. When inner energy desires to construct a more complicated gestalt, then it must telepathically communicate this intent and purpose through the matter which it collects about itself, forming a more complicated inner telepathic pattern first, that can then be filled in with physical matter. The manners in which telepathy operates are difficult to explain because of the divergence of definition held by the ego and by the inner self of any given individual. To the human being it must appear that telepathy is a mental or psychic communication occurring between two or more entirely separate selves. The ego, you see, has one definition for the self and its own idea of the limitations of itself.
quotes.translation: 当内在能量通过我之前解释的过程形成更复杂的完形时，内在能量便继续与其创造并环绕自身的物质进行沟通。当内在能量想要构建一个更复杂的完形时，它必须通过它收集在自身周围的物质，以心灵感应方式传达这一意图和目的，首先形成一个更复杂的内在心灵感应模式，然后才能用物质来填充。心灵感应运作的方式很难解释，因为任何特定个体的自我和内在自我对心灵感应的定义存在分歧。对人类而言，心灵感应必定表现为发生在两个或更多完全分离的自我之间的心智或心灵沟通。你看，自我对自我有它自己的定义，并且对自身的局限性有它自己的看法。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You are all pieces, you see, of the whole; but you are not all the same pieces of the whole, but rather individualized pieces of the whole. You do not all fit together like a crossword puzzle that any idiot can put together. You are still highly individualized portions of the whole. You are the whole, but you are all highly unique. You fit into different portions of the whole. The self or structure or personality travels outward and inward and (if you will forgive me) in all directions. It is action. It constantly changes. Each self as you know it has its own abilities and inclinations and sympathies. It has its own particular place within the Pyramid Gestalt. It can contact that whole self which in your terms does not yet exist, but which is of course always present. In your search you must contact that whole portion of yourself toward which you are growing--toward which I hope you are growing. This is your individual circuit, so to speak. All ways are one way, but your way is your own way. And you can travel no other. He is--Baba--highly advanced indeed. He is a way, however; he is not the end. He is not completed. He is right, but he is wrong in taking pleasure in his rightness. (I have never been known for my own Humilitye. It ill-behooves me to speak.) Nevertheless he who is and knows that he is, is. He has no need for words and he has no need to proclaim himself, for he speaks without the necessity for words and he is heard. Those who are really heard have no need for words. I speak to you now in words because without words now, there would not be the necessary understanding that must be reached before I can become wordless.
quotes.translation: 你们都是整体的碎片，你们看；但你们并非整体中相同的碎片，而是整体中个体化的碎片。你们并不像任何傻瓜都能拼凑的填字游戏那样拼合在一起。你们仍然是整体中高度个体化的部分。你们就是整体，但你们都是高度独特的。你们适应整体的不同部分。自我或结构或人格向外和向内旅行，并且（请原谅我这么说）向所有方向旅行。它是行动。它不断变化。你所知的每个自我都有其自身的能力、倾向和同情心。它在金字塔完形中有其自身特定的位置。它可以接触那个在你们看来尚不存在、但当然始终存在的整体自我。在你的探索中，你必须接触你正在向其成长的那部分整体自我——我希望你正在向其成长。这可以说是你个人的回路。所有的道路都是一条道路，但你的道路是你自己的道路。你无法走其他的路。他——巴巴——确实高度先进。然而，他是一条道路；他不是终点。他尚未完成。他是对的，但他因自己的正确而沾沾自喜却是错的。（我从来不以谦逊著称。由我来说这话并不合适。）然而，那个存在并知道自己存在的人，就是存在。他不需要言语，也不需要宣告自己，因为他无需言语就能说话，并且被听到。那些真正被听到的人不需要言语。我现在用言语对你们说话，因为如果没有言语，就无法获得必要的理解，而在我能够变得无言之前，必须达到这种理解。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: He requires undue amounts of praise and attention from others, since he obviously will get little from himself. In a fashion, to an extent he will refuse to be accountable for his actions--therefore taking them out of the frame of judgment within which other people must operate. He then can avoid putting his "talents and superior abilities" to the test, where he feels he would certainly fail. He half realizes that the superior self and the debased self are both of psychological manufacture. His abilities are not really that grand. His failures are not nearly that disastrous. The belief in these highly contrasting elements of personality keep him in a state of turmoil, however, so that he feels powerless to act in any concerted fashion.
quotes.translation: 他需要他人过度的赞美和关注，因为他显然从自己那里得不到多少。在某种程度上，他会拒绝为自己的行为负责——从而将它们置于其他人必须运作的判断框架之外。这样他就可以避免将他的"才华和卓越能力"付诸检验，因为他觉得自己肯定会失败。他隐约意识到，优越的自我和卑劣的自我都是心理上的制造品。他的能力并没有那么伟大。他的失败也远没有那么灾难性。然而，对这些高度对立的人格元素的信念使他处于动荡状态，以至于他感到无力以任何协调一致的方式行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: At the beginning of the notes for this 920th session I wrote that the session isn't dictation for Dreams. Yet it is, of course, since in it Seth illuminates from still another perspective his concept of value fulfillment.8 The session grew out of the encounter we had yesterday afternoon with an unexpected visitor from out of state. Seth hardly mentions the individual involved, however, but instead goes into the subject of mental illness in more inclusive terms. I see many correlations between this evening's material and that in Session 917 for Chapter 8; there, among other things, Seth had discussed the reasoning mind, the imaginative mind, and schizophrenia. That session had been triggered, at least in part, by a letter Jane had found to be quite upsetting.

quotes.text: A particularly vivid dream is every bit as real to the inner self as a vivid psychological experience that occurs within the waking state. It is important here that we realize that as far as the basic self is concerned no distinctions are made in this respect. The personality creates its dreams; the dreams are then experienced. The experience is indelibly recorded, and then changes the personality, again, in the same manner that any experience would.
quotes.translation: 一个特别生动的梦境，对内在自我而言，其真实性丝毫不亚于清醒状态下发生的生动心理体验。此处重要的是，我们要认识到，就基本自我而言，在这方面并无区别。人格创造其梦境；然后梦境被体验。体验被不可磨灭地记录下来，然后改变人格，同样，以任何体验都会采取的方式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The enemy is obvious. His intentions are evil. Wars are basically examples of mass suicide -- embarked upon, however, with all of the battle's paraphernalia, carried out through mass suggestion, and through the nation's greatest resources, by men who are convinced that the universe is unsafe, that the self cannot be trusted, and that strangers are always hostile. You take it for granted that the species is aggressively combative. You must out-think the enemy nation before you yourself are destroyed. These paranoiac tendencies are largely hidden beneath man's nationalistic banners.
quotes.translation: 敌人是显而易见的。他的意图是邪恶的。战争基本上是集体自杀的例子——然而，是在所有战斗装备齐全的情况下，通过集体暗示，以及国家最庞大的资源，由那些确信宇宙不安全、自我不可信任、陌生人总是充满敌意的人发起的。你理所当然地认为人类具有侵略好斗性。你必须在被摧毁之前先于敌人思考。这些偏执倾向大多隐藏在人类的民族主义旗帜之下。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Chapter 6 -- headings as given last time (in Session 834).
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: On the one hand, they believe that the self is evil, and on the other they are convinced that the self should not be so. They react extravagantly. They often see society as the "enemy" of good. Many -- not all, now -- criminals possess the same characteristics you ascribe to heroes, except that the heroes have a means toward the expression of idealism, and specific avenues for that expression. And many criminals find such avenues cut off completely.
quotes.translation: 一方面，他们相信自我是邪恶的，另一方面，他们又确信自我不应该是这样的。他们反应过度。他们常常将社会视为“善”的敌人。许多——并非全部，注意——罪犯拥有与你赋予英雄相同的特质，只是英雄拥有表达理想主义的手段，以及表达的具体途径。而许多罪犯发现这样的途径被完全切断了。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

SESSION 860, JUNE 13, 1979
9:19 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You can literally choose between health and illness; between a concentration upon the mental more than the physical, or upon the physical more than the mental. Such private decisions affect the genetic heritage of the species. Your intent is all-important -- for you can alter your own genetic messages3 within certain limits. You can cause a cell, or a group of cells, to change their self-image, for example; and again, you do this often -- as you healed yourselves of diseases because of your intent to become well. The intent will be conscious, though the means may not be. Period. In such a case, however, the self-healing qualities of the cells are reinforced, and the self-healing abilities of the species are also strengthened.
quotes.translation: 你真的可以在健康与疾病之间做出选择；在专注于精神多于身体，或专注于身体多于精神之间做出选择。这样的私人决定会影响物种的遗传遗产。你的意图至关重要——因为你可以在一定限度内改变你自己的遗传信息。例如，你可以让一个细胞或一组细胞改变它们的自我形象；而且，你经常这样做——就像你因为意图康复而治愈了自己的疾病一样。意图会是意识的，尽管手段可能不是。句号。然而，在这种情况下，细胞的自愈品质得到加强，物种的自愈能力也得到增强。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Earlier this evening I reminded Jane of the conversation about cells, versus their components, that we'd had because of Seth's material at 9:38 in Session 705. Some of tonight's book work refers to the questions growing out of our talk I think, as does Seth's brief clarification near the end of the session.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: And that self can see through physical reality. And to that self, physical reality is like a breath of smoke in air... and that self does not need the characteristics that you know and find so endearing. And yet it is not an unemotional self; it is a self that has condensed emotions; and, it is not distant.
quotes.translation: 那个自我可以看穿物质实相。对那个自我而言，物质实相就像空气中的一缕烟……而那个自我不需要你所知道并觉得如此可爱的那些特质。然而，它并非一个没有情感的自我；它是一个浓缩了情感的自我；而且，它并不遥远。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The self, the whole self, the entity, that is your true self, does indeed exist now. You are even in contact with it now. It is true that in one sense you have never left this self. It is also true that in another sense you have indeed. Your have purposefully explored other systems as you explore this one.
quotes.translation: 自我，整体自我，存有，那才是你的真正自我，确实存在于当下。你甚至现在就在与它接触。确实，在某种意义上，你从未离开过这个自我。同样真实的是，在另一种意义上，你确实离开了。你有目的地探索其他系统，就像你探索这个系统一样。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: In your sleep you may have greeted friends who are strangers to your waking self. But consider the other side of the coin. For when you are asleep, you cannot find the street upon which you live your waking hours, and when you are asleep you do not know you waking self. The sleeping self is your identity.
quotes.translation: 在睡眠中，你或许曾与那些对你清醒的自我而言全然陌生的朋友打招呼。但请思考硬币的另一面。因为当你沉睡时，你找不到自己清醒时居住的那条街道，也认不出那个清醒的自我。沉睡的自我才是你的身份认同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Until this is realized your psychology will raise more questions than it answers. The basis for all experience is depth perception, and value fulfillment. The self organizes data basically in a manner that psychology has not found. The organization of such data is not simply the result of pre-adulthood tendencies, inclinations or experience. Such tendencies are highly colored by previous existences, by past lives, and this prehistory, existing as the electromagnetic property of the whole self, is the blueprint which is followed by the structure of the chromosomes.
quotes.translation: 除非领悟到这一点，否则你的心理学提出的问题将永远多于答案。所有经验的基础是深度感知与价值完成。自我组织资料的方式，心理学至今尚未发现。这种资料的组织并非单纯源自成年前的倾向、偏好或经验。这些倾向深受前世存在的影响，而作为整体自我电磁属性存在的这种史前史，正是染色体结构所遵循的蓝图。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: There are many portions of the self, as you know. They do assume a form that is visible within their own dimension. These portions of the self are all simultaneous; in various stages of consciousness you become aware of other portions of the self, and an I identifies itself with one or another of these.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，自我有许多部分。它们确实会在自身维度内呈现可见的形态。这些自我部分同时存在；在不同意识阶段中，你会觉察到自我的其他部分，而"我"会与其中某个部分产生认同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now basically, consciousness itself is a type of barrier, and anything that has consciousness experiences time to some degree. This degree may be so slight that to you it would not seem to exist at all. Self-consciousness presents a larger barrier, therefore the sense of time is greater. Psychological time is the lowest common denominator, so to speak, from your viewpoint. That is, many of the barriers disappear. Psychological time represents on your plane the closest you can come to the experience of timelessness as far as your physical laws are concerned.
quotes.translation: 从根本上说，意识本身就是一种屏障，任何具有意识的事物都会在某种程度上体验时间。这种程度可能微弱到在你看来完全不存在。自我意识构成更大的屏障，因此时间感也更强烈。从你的视角来看，心理时间可以说是最小公分母。也就是说，许多屏障在此消失。就物理法则而言，心理时间代表你在这个层面上所能最接近无时间体验的状态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The stability of the whole self is dependent upon the mobility of each portion of the self. When the physical system is in good operating order, then the various portions of the self are operating properly. The fault will not always lie with the conscious or subconscious, however, but in other layers.
quotes.translation: 整体自我的稳定性依赖于每个自我部分的流动性。当物理系统运作良好时，自我的各个部分便正常运作。然而，问题并不总出在意识或潜意识层面，而是存在于其他层次。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: It is true that the inner self in the last analysis is the durable self. Nevertheless the various outer egos are extremely important, and without them the inner self would be blocked. Nor are these inner abilities as a rule ever left unfulfilled. The outer egos are not gobbled up, so to speak, after a particular existence, but remain in control of those characteristics and abilities which they have been so important in forming and training.
quotes.translation: 归根结底，内在自我确实是持久的自我。然而，各种外在自我极为重要，没有它们，内在自我将受到阻碍。这些内在能力通常也绝不会被闲置。可以说，外在自我在特定存在结束后并不会被吞噬，而是继续掌控那些它们曾如此重要地形成与训练的特质和能力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: We have to some extent explained the reality of the personality within the electrical system. We have to some extent explained its basic origin within inner reality. We have explained the fact that the potentialities of the personality and of the self are basically unlimited. But during its alliance within the physical system, it is diffused within the cellular structure, and interrelating actions between the biological system, the electrical system, and the personality structure actually form the reality of the human individual.
quotes.translation: 我们已在某种程度上解释了电性系统内人格的实相，也在某种程度上解释了它在内在实相中的基本起源。我们解释了人格与自性的潜能基本上无限的事实。但在它与物质系统联盟期间，它被分散于细胞结构内，而生物系统、电性系统与人格结构之间的相互作用，实际上形成了人类个体的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The portrait that you sent to the gallery is evocative. It continues to grow. It is not completely at the mercy of a completed camouflage. The whole self is never completely constructed on your plane. At best it finds expression now and then. A camouflage plane, merely by being what it is, makes it impossible for the whole self to find expression. There is almost hypnotic focus of energy for a particular time for a particular reason.
quotes.translation: 你寄给画廊的那幅画令人回味。它持续生长，并未完全受制于已完成的伪装。完整的自性从未在你的层面上完全建构完成，充其量只是偶尔得以表达。伪装层面仅因其本质，便使完整自性无法表达。能量几乎像被催眠般聚焦于特定时间与特定理由。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Once more, the field of probabilities does not consist of imaginative fancy, nor of nebulous activity. It is quite as real as the physical universe. The experiences that are encountered there by other portions of the self are used by the whole self, and the knowledge thus gains is invaluable. Not only in terms of overall experience, but also as a means of training the ego, and the subconscious, to choose between various activities.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，可能性场域并非由想象幻想或模糊活动构成，它与物质宇宙同样真实。自性的其他部分在那里遭遇的经验，被完整自性所运用，由此获得的知识无比珍贵。不仅就整体经验而言，更是训练自我与潜意识在不同活动间选择的方法。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Self structures and identities are not the same. Identity requires no structure. Identity knows, and knows that it knows. self structures know and do not know that they know. self structures are a part of identity. Identities can contain pure knowledge without translation, and use it to seed various existences and to form realities. They do this consciously in your terms. They are far more conscious than you can presently imagine.
quotes.translation: 自性结构与身份认同并不相同。身份认同不需要结构。身份认同知道，且知道自己知道。自性结构知道，却不知道它们知道。自性结构是身份认同的一部分。身份认同能包含未经翻译的纯粹知识，并用它来播种各种存在、形成实相。以你们的说法，它们有意识地这样做。它们的意识程度远超你们目前所能想象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Left alone, the self and the body are so entwined that the separation would be smooth. The body would automatically follow the wishes of the inner self. In the case of suicide, for example, the self is to some extent acting out of context with the body, which still has its own will to live.
quotes.translation: 若不加干预，自性与身体如此紧密交织，分离将十分顺畅。身体会自动遵循内在自性的意愿。例如在自杀案例中，自性在某种程度上与身体脱节行动，而身体仍保有自身的生存意志。

quotes.text: Now. The inner self exists in quite a different fashion than that seen by Dunne. For the inner self can indeed perceive events that will occur after physical death. It is not, and never was, imprisoned by ego time. Its perceptions of other times are merely inhibited by the ego. The inner self can perceive events that will occur to itself after physical death, and it also can see events that will occur in which it is not involved.
quotes.translation: 现在。内在自性的存在方式与邓恩所见截然不同。因为内在自性确实能感知肉体死亡后会发生的事件。它不曾、也从未被自我时间所囚禁。它对其他时间的感知仅受自我抑制。内在自性能感知自身在肉体死亡后发生的事件，也能看到它未涉入的事件。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: The infant sees mental images before birth, before the eyes are open. Your memory, it seems, is your own -- yet I have told you that you have a history of other existences. You remember other faces, even though the mind you call the conscious one may not recognize the images from that deep inner memory. It must often clothe them in fantasy. You are yourself. Your self is secure in its own identity, unique in its characteristics, meeting life and the seasons in a way that has never happened before, and will never happen again -- yet still you are a unique version of your greater self. You share in certain overall patterns that are in themselves original.
quotes.translation: 婴儿在出生前、在眼睛睁开前就能看到心灵影像。你的记忆看似属于你自己——但我曾告诉你，你拥有其他存在形式的历史。你记得其他面孔，即使你称之为意识心的心智可能无法辨识来自深层内在记忆的影像，它常常必须将这些影像披上幻想的外衣。你就是你自己。你的自性在其独特身份中安然无恙，特性独一无二，以从未有过、也永不再现的方式迎接生命与季节——然而你仍是更伟大自性的一个独特版本。你共享某些本身即具原创性的整体模式。

quotes.text: Now. It should be obvious that the psychological framework must be different when the time system of experience is different. You can see easily for yourself the individual psychological variations that exist simply between the ego and the subconscious, but these portions of the self are very close. Other portions of the self, that deal in what you would call probable realities, are very different in their psychological makeup.
quotes.translation: 现在。当经验的时间系统不同时，心理架构必然不同，这应该显而易见。你很容易就能看出仅存在于自我与潜意识之间的个体心理差异，但这些自性部分非常接近。处理你们所谓或然实相的其他自性部分，其心理构成则截然不同。

quotes.text: The very idea of protection, however, as you know, implies a threat--so if you believe in threats you had better have protection. It was not necessary for Ruburt to see the form again--merely to sense the reality of that powerful energy, and realize that it worked on his behalf. In a fashion the form also represented the innocent and powerful inner self, or spontaneous self, or naturally magical self--the terms are synonymous.
quotes.translation: 然而正如你所知，保护这个概念本身就暗示着威胁——所以如果你相信威胁，最好有保护措施。鲁柏无需再次看见那个形态——只需感知那股强大能量的实相，并意识到它在为他运作。在某种程度上，这个形态也代表了纯真而强大的内在自性，或自发自性，或自然神奇的自性——这些术语是同义词。

quotes.text: Yet the inner self offers so many answers, for the inner self is a portion of each individual; and yet it operates outside of physical systems of reference. It is of itself free of all distorted effects peculiar to the physical system. The study of dreams--your scientists consider such work beneath them. Why has no one suspected that dream locations, for example, have not only a psychological reality, but a definite actuality?
quotes.translation: 然而内在自性提供了如此多的答案，因为内在自性是每个个体的一部分；但它又在物质参考系之外运作。它本身不受物质系统特有的所有扭曲效应影响。对梦境的研究——你们的科学家认为这种工作有失身份。为何没人怀疑过，例如梦境地点不仅具有心理实相，还具有确切的真实性？

quotes.text: The relative, neutral passivity is attained as various portions of the whole self once more collect together. The acceleration, which is mental, begins as those divergent portions of the whole self-interact. This is an electromagnetic sequence or action that will eventually help provide the impetus for projection of the whole self into another field of actuality, as the rate of acceleration increases and becomes more unified.
quotes.translation: 当整体自性的各个部分再次聚集时，就达到了相对中性的被动状态。当整体自性那些分散的部分开始互动时，属于心智层面的加速便开始了。这是一个电磁序列或行动，随着加速率增加并变得更统一，最终将有助于为整体自性投射到另一个实相场提供动力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You can feel what you cannot necessarily describe verbally, for you are more than the physical brain that you have now. I am no poet, but as in one of Ruburt's poems, think of the brain as a web you form about the inner self. This webwork helps you manipulate in a world of space and time, and is as nebulous, precarious and delicate as any spider's web -- and in as precarious a balance. You form this and then perceive the world, but your viewpoint is very small and the garden you perceive very intimate. You have, however, far greater abilities of perception. I want you to understand the nature of your inner self, or soul, for it is a focal point of reality from which other realities spring. It is not imprisoned in tiny boxes of days or weeks or months, or even of centuries.
quotes.translation: 你能感受到未必能用言语描述的东西，因为你比你现在拥有的物质大脑更伟大。我不是诗人，但正如鲁柏一首诗中所言，把大脑想象成你围绕内在自性编织的网。这张网帮助你在时空世界中运作，它像任何蜘蛛网一样虚无缥缈、脆弱易碎——且处于同样岌岌可危的平衡中。你形成这张网，然后感知世界，但你的视角非常狭小，你感知的花园非常私密。然而你拥有远为强大的感知能力。我要你了解你内在自性或灵魂的本质，因为它是实相的一个焦点，其他实相由此生发。它并非囚禁在日、周、月甚至世纪的小盒子里。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Intently:) I am saying that the individual self must become consciously aware of far more reality; that it must allow its recognition of identity to expand so that it includes previously unconscious knowledge. To do this you must understand, again, that man must move beyond the concepts of one god, one self, one body, one world, as these ideas are currently understood.5 You are now poised, in your terms, upon a threshold from which the race can go many ways. There are species of consciousness. Your species is in a time of change. There are potentials within the body's mechanisms, in your terms, not as yet used. Developed, they can immeasurably enrich the race, and bring it to levels of spiritual and psychic and physical fulfillment. If some changes are not made, the race as such will not endure.
quotes.translation: （专注地：）我说的是，个体自我必须有意识地认识到更广阔的现实；它必须允许自己对身份的认知扩展，以包含先前无意识的知识。要做到这一点，你必须再次理解，人类必须超越目前所理解的单一神、单一自我、单一身体、单一世界的概念。以你们的时间观来看，你们正站在一个门槛上，种族可以从这里走向许多方向。存在多种意识物种。你们的物种正处于变革时期。以你们的时间观来看，身体机制中存在着尚未被利用的潜能。如果开发这些潜能，它们将无可估量地丰富种族，并将其带到灵性、心灵和身体满足的层次。如果不做出某些改变，这个种族将无法存续。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: In the last few sentences there is an intuitive "definition" of probable and reincarnational selves, and counterparts, in relationship to the self that you know. In your case, however, you can change your own pacing, add variations, or even begin an entirely new composition if you choose to. Now many people have done this in very simple, mundane ways by suddenly deciding to use abilities they had earlier ignored. A man of letters, for instance, at the age of 40 suddenly remembers his old love of carpentry, reads do-it-yourself manuals, and begins his own home repairs. After disdaining such activities as beneath him for years, he suddenly discovers an intimate relationship with earth and its goods, and this appreciation adds to words that before may have been as dry as ash.
quotes.translation: 在最后几句话中，对可能自我、转世自我以及对应体，与你们所知的自我之间的关系，有一种直觉性的"定义"。然而，就你们的情况而言，你们可以改变自己的节奏，增加变奏，甚至可以选择开始一首全新的乐章。许多人已经以非常简单、平凡的方式做到了这一点，比如突然决定运用他们之前忽视的能力。例如，一位文人到了40岁突然记起自己对木工的热爱，阅读DIY手册，开始自己修理房屋。在多年鄙视这类活动为有失身份之后，他忽然发现了与大地及其物产的亲密关系，这种欣赏为之前可能如灰烬般干涸的文字增添了色彩。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "Seth's data and my own on counterparts make sense to me. I feel (as Seth mentioned in the 721st session) that I wasn't Nebene, or two different Roman soldiers per se, but rather that my whole self chose to manifest such personalities together; that I, too, am such a manifestation at a "later" time, then, and that from my own vantage point I can tune in to those other lives. But I question, at least provisionally, any idea of past or counterpart lives that I lived one hundred percent. At this writing, I think that I am living my only one hundred percent life now, with the privilege of occasionally being able to focus upon scattered portions of those other existences emanating from my whole self, which has its basic reality outside of our space-time concepts.10
quotes.translation: "赛斯关于对应体的资料和我自己的理解对我而言是合理的。我感觉（正如赛斯在第721节课中提到的）我并不是内贝内本人，也不是两个不同的罗马士兵，而是我的整个自我选择同时显现这些人格；那么，我也是在'后来'时间中的这样一个显现，并且从我的有利位置，我可以调谐到那些其他生命。但我至少暂时质疑任何我百分之百经历过的过去或对应生命的观念。在写这段话时，我认为我现在正在经历我唯一百分之百的生命，同时享有偶尔聚焦于那些从我的整个自我（其基本现实存在于我们的时空概念之外）散发出的其他存在的零散部分的权利。

quotes.text: You cannot begin to have a true psychology, again, unless you see the living self in a greater context, with greater motives, purposes and meanings than you now assign to it, or for that matter than you assign to nature and its creatures. You have denied many impulses, or programmed others so that they are allowed expression in only certain forms of action. If any of you do (underlined) still believe in the Freudian or Darwinian selves, then you will be leery about impulses to examine your own consciousness, afraid of what murderous debris might be uncovered. I am not speaking merely in hypothetical terms. For example, a well-intentioned woman was here recently. She worried about her overweight condition, and [was] depressed at what she thought of as her lack of discipline in following diets. In her dismay, she visited a psychologist, who told her that her marriage might somehow be part of the problem. The woman said she never went back. She was afraid that she might discover within herself the buried impulse to kill her husband, or to break up the marriage, but she was sure that her overweight condition hid some unfortunate impulse.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，除非你将活生生的自我置于更广阔的背景下，赋予它比你现在所赋予的、或者比你对自然及其生物所赋予的更大的动机、目的和意义，否则你无法开始拥有真正的心理学。你们否定了许多冲动，或者对其他冲动进行了编程，使它们只能在某些特定形式的行动中得以表达。如果你们中任何人（强调）仍然相信弗洛伊德或达尔文式的自我，那么你们就会对审视自己意识的冲动保持警惕，害怕可能发现什么凶残的残骸。我并非仅仅在假设性地说。例如，最近这里来了一位好心的女士。她为自己的超重状况而烦恼，并因认为自己缺乏节食纪律而感到沮丧。在沮丧中，她咨询了一位心理学家，后者告诉她，她的婚姻可能部分导致了问题。这位女士说她再也没有回去过。她害怕自己内心可能埋藏着杀死丈夫或破坏婚姻的冲动，但她确信自己的超重状况隐藏着某种不幸的冲动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

SESSION 859, JUNE 6, 1979
9:14 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: In highly complicated cellular structures like yourselves (pause), with your unique mental properties, you end up with a vital inborn sense of shape and form. The ability to draw is a natural outgrowth of this sensing of shape, this curiosity of form. On a quite unconscious level you possess a biological self-image that is quite different from the self that you see in a mirror. It is a knowledge of bodily form from the inside out, so to speak, composed of cellular shapes and organizations, operating at the maximum. The simple cell, again, has a curiosity about its environment, and on your much more advanced cellular level your own curiosity is unbounded. It is primarily felt as a curiosity about shapes: the urge to touch, to explore, to feel edges and smooth places.
quotes.translation: 在像你们这样高度复杂的细胞结构中（停顿），凭借你们独特的心理属性，你们最终拥有一种与生俱来的对形状和形式的重要感知。绘画能力是这种形状感知、这种形式好奇心的自然产物。在相当无意识的层面上，你们拥有一个与镜中看到的自我截然不同的生物性自我形象。可以说，这是一种从内而外对身体形式的知识，由细胞形状和组织构成，以最大程度运作。再次强调，简单的细胞对其环境有好奇心，而在你们更高级的细胞层面上，你们的好奇心是无止境的。它主要体现为对形状的好奇心：触摸、探索、感受边缘和光滑地方的冲动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As far as these drugs are concerned there are a few points I would like to make. One of your purposes as I have said is to learn to organize energy units and to focus your own energy along those lines. That is, you are the energy that you use. The self-consciousness is not to be blotted out but taken along. It is so to speak to walk side by side with the inner self. The use of these drugs sweeps the personality off its feet. Often the personality does receive a rewarding glimpse of the inner reality, but more often the ego is merely sent tumbling through frightening images of chaotic phantom realities formed by a suddenly-released subconscious, and with no guide. The experience is often vivid and long remembered, but so unorganized and undisciplined that no inner order is arrived at, no other organization glimpsed, and the ordinary but necessary camouflage footing suddenly dispensed with.
quotes.translation: 关于这些药物，我想指出几点。正如我所说，你们的目的之一是学习组织能量单元，并沿着这些方向聚焦你们自己的能量。也就是说，你们就是你们所使用的能量。自我意识不应被抹去，而应被带着走。可以说，它应该与内在自我并肩而行。使用这些药物会使人格失去平衡。人格有时确实会获得对内在现实的有益一瞥，但更常见的是，自我只是被抛入由突然释放的潜意识形成的混乱幻象现实的可怕意象中，且没有向导。这种体验往往生动且长久难忘，但如此混乱无序，以至于无法达到内在秩序，无法瞥见其他组织，而普通但必要的伪装基础突然被抛弃。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It is the ego or directive consciousness behind all personified aspects of the subconscious; in dormant fashion however, and contrary to Jung's propositions, within the subconscious and in those personified aspects of it will be found remnant memory personalities of past reincarnated selves. They may be called shadows and yet they are not powerless. The inner ego, the directive organizer of the subconscious, also is the part of the self which is familiar with activities and methods of which the outer ego is ignorant. It is this organizer who directs not only the movements of the physical body from within, but directs from within those intimate survival mechanisms, without which the physical body could not exist, and upon which the existence of the outer ego is so dependent.
quotes.translation: 它是自我或指导性意识，位于所有潜意识人格化面向的背后；然而，以休眠的方式，且与荣格的命题相反，在潜意识及其人格化面向中，将发现过去转世自我的残余记忆人格。它们可被称为阴影，却并非无力。内在自我，即潜意识的指导性组织者，也是熟悉外在自我所不知的活动与方法的自我部分。正是这位组织者，不仅从内部指导肉体的运动，还从内部指导那些亲密的生存机制——没有这些机制，肉体无法存在，而外在自我的存在如此依赖它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: These dreams go unrecognized by the conscious ego; but they do not go unrecorded by the inner self, and they therefore exist, and they form electromagnetic channels of their own to which--make that by which--the physical body itself is affected. The physical organism is constantly changed by all stimuli, whether or not the ego is aware of the stimuli. And it is affected by stimuli, whether or not science realizes that the particular stimuli even exists. Therefore, the physical organism reacts to this continuous unconscious Mental activity in the form of continuing dream experiences, whether or not the individual is awake or asleep. All of these realities go into the formation of the personality, and unless they are all understood the personality itself will remain a mystery.
quotes.translation: 这些梦不被意识自我所识别；但它们不会被内在自我所忽略，因此它们存在，并形成自己的电磁通道——通过这个通道，肉体本身受到影响。无论自我是否意识到刺激，肉体组织不断被所有刺激所改变。并且，无论科学是否意识到特定刺激的存在，肉体都受到刺激的影响。因此，肉体组织以持续梦体验的形式，对这种持续的无意识心理活动做出反应，无论个体是清醒还是睡眠。所有这些现实都参与人格的形成，除非它们全部被理解，否则人格本身将保持神秘。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You must become more aware of your inner selves.... They are not all that horrible. You still fear, as our Lady of Venice, that there is a cellar door... a cellar door to your mind--to your inner self. And, if you open it, all sorts of demons will emerge. And if there are any angels, the demons will gobble them up before you get to see them. Instead, I tell you, as I have told you before, you are more than you know. And it is up to you to find your own reality. I cannot give it to you. I can point you in the direction... but the experience is personal and the experience is subjective and the journey is one that you must make and that you must make alone. I cannot make it for you. I have my own journeys to make... and detours here. And any problems that you have I have had them... so look at me and know how indestructible you are!
quotes.translation: 你必须更加意识到你的内在自我……它们并非那么可怕。你仍然像我们的威尼斯女士一样，害怕有一扇地窖门……一扇通往你心灵——通往你内在自我的地窖门。如果你打开它，各种恶魔就会涌现。如果有任何天使，恶魔会在你看到它们之前把它们吞噬。相反，我告诉你，正如我以前告诉你的，你比你认知的更多。找到你自己的现实取决于你。我不能把它给你。我可以为你指明方向……但体验是个人的，体验是主观的，旅程是你必须独自进行的。我不能替你走。我有自己的旅程要走……以及这里的绕路。你遇到的任何问题我都经历过……所以看着我，知道你是多么不可摧毁！
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: It is very unfortunate, therefore, when adults inadvertently undermine a child's sense of self-approval. A small boy might be caught in a lie, for example, and therefore labeled by an adult in the angriest of terms as a liar. Instead, a distinction should be made: the child made an error -- he lied -- but he himself is not the error or the lie. He can then determine to change his behavior while still saving his self-respect.
quotes.translation: 因此，当成年人无意中削弱孩子的自我认可感时，是非常不幸的。例如，一个小男孩可能被抓到撒谎，然后被成年人用最愤怒的词语标记为说谎者。相反，应该做出区分：孩子犯了一个错误——他撒谎了——但他本人并非错误或谎言。这样他就能在保持自尊的同时，决定改变自己的行为。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Your question about transmigration (in Session 903 in Dreams) was an excellent one, and did indeed make the matter clear. It is a temptation at times to use more specific scientific terms, but these would be as confusing as the various definitions and classifications (with humor) that you read in the dictionary, so overall we try to hit a "happy medium." (With more humor.)

quotes.text: "You" are not divorced from these other streams of consciousness in any basic way; only your focus of attention closes you off from them, and from the events in which they are involved. If you think of your stream of consciousness as transparent, however, then you can learn to look through and beneath it to others that lie in other beds of reality. You can also learn to rise above your present stream of consciousness and perceive others that run, for analogy's sake, parallel. The point is that you are only limited to the self you know if you think that you are, and if you do not realize that that self is far from your entire identity.
quotes.translation: “你”在任何基本层面上都与这些其他意识流没有分离；只是你的注意力焦点将你与它们隔绝，以及与它们所涉及的事件隔绝。然而，如果你将你的意识流视为透明的，那么你可以学会透过它看到并深入其他位于不同现实河床的意识流。你也可以学会提升到你当前的意识流之上，并感知其他平行流动的意识流，打个比方。关键在于，你只被你所知的自我所限制，如果你认为你是这样，并且如果你没有意识到那个自我远非你的全部身份。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Chemically in particular, the dependence of the physical body and the self to the planetary environment is obvious. It is known to your scientists that the chemical relationship between the personal physical self, and the chemical environment of the not-self is intimately connected.
quotes.translation: 特别是在化学方面，肉体与自我对行星环境的依赖是显而易见的。你们的科学家知道，个人物理自我与非自我的化学环境之间的化学关系是密切相连的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It goes without saying that the part that watches also belongs to the self, but it does not belong to the self as the self is thought of in usual psychological terms. You are bringing into operation, and exercising a higher faculty, and through these exercises this faculty matures.
quotes.translation: 不言而喻，那个观察的部分也属于自己，但它不属于通常心理学概念中的自己。你正在运作并运用一种更高的能力，通过这种练习，这种能力得以成熟。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: And each self is endowed with the agony and the impetus, for it is a fabric from which All That Is made itself, and all that you know. Perhaps now you can understand why it is so difficult to try to explain matters to you in terms that you can understand. All That Is loves all that it has created, down to the least, for it realizes the dearness, and the uniqueness of each consciousness which had been wrest (spelled) from such a state and at such a price; and it is triumphant and joyful at each new development taken by each consciousness, for this is an added triumph against that first state, and an added security against that first state, and it revels and (takes) joy in the slightest creative act of each of its issue.
quotes.translation: 每个自己都兼具痛苦与动力，因为它是一块织锦，万有即由此织就自身，以及你所知的一切。或许现在你能理解，为何用你能理解的术语来解释这些事如此困难。万有爱其所创造的一切，哪怕是最微小的，因为它明白每个意识从那种状态中挣脱出来所付出的代价与珍贵；每个意识取得的新进展都令它欢欣鼓舞，因为这既是对原初状态的又一次胜利，也是对原初状态的又一次安全保障，它为其每个造物最微小的创造行为而陶醉喜悦。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Following our analogy, you will be his guest and from his rooms look down into your own with some greater objectivity. This is possible, feasible, since you are all portions, in our analogy, of this same inner self who maintains all of the rooms. While each of you are egotistically focused within your own reality, the deeper layers of the self are aware of the quote "family" relationship. Now give me a moment. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 依循我们的类比，你将作为它的访客，从它的房间俯视自己的房间，获得更客观的视角。这是可能且可行的，因为在我们类比中，你们都是同一个内在自己的部分，这个自己维护着所有房间。当你们每个人以自我为中心聚焦于自己的实相时，自己的更深层面却知晓所谓的"家族"关系。现在请稍等片刻。（停顿。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: That inner, all-pervasive existence becomes known to the extent that you grow more responsive to your own inner environment. This does not mean that you become entirely self-centered, blind to the rest of the world. It does not mean that you must meditate for hours, or study your own thought processes with such vigor that you ignore other activities. It simply means that you are aware of your own life as clearly as possible -- in touch with your thought processes, aware of them but without overdue concern or overanalysis. They are as much a part of your inner environment as trees are of your exterior world. There are different species of selves in the same fashion. There are different species of worlds.
quotes.translation: 那个内在的、遍及一切的存在，会随着你对自己内在环境越来越敏感而逐渐被认知。这并不意味着你变得完全以自我为中心，对世界其余部分视而不见。也不意味着你必须长时间冥想，或如此用力地研究自己的思维过程以致忽略其他活动。它仅仅意味着你尽可能清晰地觉察自己的生命——与自己的思维过程保持联结，觉察它们但不过度关注或过度分析。它们是你内在环境的一部分，正如树木是你外在世界的一部分。同样地，存在着不同种类的自己。也存在着不同种类的世界。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 742
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You have built defenses so strongly that you have not heard the voice of the inner self. The inner self is hardly to be feared. You have allowed the ego to become a counterfeit self, and you take its word because you will not hear the muffled voice that is within you speak.
quotes.translation: 你建立的防御如此坚固，以至于听不见内在自己的声音。内在自己几乎不值得恐惧。你允许自我成为冒牌的自己，你相信它的话语，因为你不愿聆听内在那被压抑的声音。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: In other words, portions of the inner self have joined the strictly egotistical functions. The ego in such cases is so attuned that it becomes almost something else. We are coming here toward a definition of illumination in psychic terms. The ego is not, underlined, banished. It joins with portions of the inner self previously unconscious, and illuminates the whole personality. It is no longer primarily physically oriented. Therefore it is no longer an ego in the terms usually meant.
quotes.translation: 换言之，内在自己的部分已与严格的自我功能结合。在这种情况下，自我变得如此协调，几乎成了别的东西。我们在此接近灵性术语中"照亮"的定义。自我并未被抹除。它与先前无意识的内在自己部分结合，照亮整个人格。它不再主要面向物质导向。因此，它不再是通常意义上的自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: This (precognitive ability) steers the cell through mazes of probabilities, while allowing it to retain knowledge of its own greatest fulfillment -- the idea of itself, which is always alive in any given period of your time. On a different kind of scale, then, each individual has the same sort of idealized version of the self, and so does each species. Here I mean each species, and I am not simply referring to mankind. Obviously these are not apparent to the physical senses, yet they are strong energy centers that to some degree do stimulate the physical senses toward activity. To that degree, then, there are indeed "tree gods," gods of the forest, and "gods of being" connected with each person.
quotes.translation: 这种（预知）能力引导细胞穿越概率的迷宫，同时使其保留对自身最大成就的认知——即对自身理念的认知，这一理念在你时间的任何特定时期都始终鲜活。因此，在不同尺度上，每个个体都拥有同样理想化的自我版本，每个物种也是如此。这里我指的是每个物种，而不仅仅指人类。显然，这些对肉体感官而言并不明显，但它们却是强大的能量中心，在某种程度上确实会刺激肉体感官趋向活动。因此，在那种程度上，确实存在"树神"、森林之神，以及与每个人相连的"存在之神"。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 691
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The psychedelic experience, or any mystical experience, is sometimes apt to put you in touch with what may seem to you to be a future self. This self of course, again, always existed. The inner self has the ability to delve beneath all your camouflage data to perceive these other reality systems. For the fullest development however these perceptions must be made conscious. Even the ego must become aware of them.
quotes.translation: 迷幻体验或任何神秘体验，有时容易让你接触到可能看似未来自我的东西。当然，这个自我再次始终存在。内在自我有能力深入你所有伪装数据之下，以感知这些其他实相系统。然而，为了最充分的发展，这些感知必须被意识化。甚至自我也必须意识到它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Think of the greater you -- call it the entity if you want to -- as forming a psychic structure quite as real as your physical one, but composed of many selves. As each cell of your body has its position within your corporeal space and boundaries, so each self within the entity is aware of its own "time" and dimension of activity. The body is a temporal structure. The cells, however, while a part of this body, are not aware of the entire dimension in which your consciousness dwells. They do not perceive all of the elements that are available even in three-dimensional experience, yet your present consciousness -- seemingly so much more sophisticated -- physically rests upon cellular awareness.
quotes.translation: 将更伟大的你——如果你愿意，可以称之为存有——视为形成一个灵性结构，它像你的肉体结构一样真实，但由许多自我组成。正如你身体的每个细胞在你肉体空间和界限内都有其位置，存有中的每个自我也知晓其自身的"时间"和活动维度。身体是一个时间结构。然而，细胞作为这个身体的一部分，并不知晓你意识所居的整个维度。它们无法感知即使在三维体验中也可用的所有元素，然而你当前的意识——看似如此复杂得多——在肉体上却依赖于细胞的觉知。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is self-propelled, self-perpetuating as a psychological energy unit. It will indeed materialize itself upon the physical plane simultaneously and withdraw itself simultaneously, as I have explained earlier. Your time sense gives it and all matter the appearance of durability. The energy that composes the smallest or any physical unit, passes through the physical field where the outer senses can no longer perceive it. Some, and a large amount, of this energy passes through the physical field so that you do not perceive it at all, taking form that you do not distinguish, and that cannot be harnessed on the physical level. And this is where your scientists get the idea of entropy.
quotes.translation: 它作为心理能量单位是自我驱动、自我延续的。正如我之前解释过的，它确实会在物质层面上同时将自己物质化，并同时将自己撤回。你的时间感赋予它和所有物质一种持久性的表象。构成最小或任何物质单位的能量，会穿过肉体场域，超出外在感官的感知范围。其中一部分（且数量庞大）能量穿过肉体场域，以至于你完全无法感知它，它以你无法辨识的形式存在，且无法在物质层面上被利用。这正是你的科学家们得出熵概念的地方。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The confident inner self will let the ego manipulate in the physical world, but will not allow it to become fiercely overprotective. Your work contains the strength of your inner self in many ways. Your particular ego's function is to show this work to the world as you know it. I hesitate, and I mean this, to offer practical advice to one who tries to be so practical. But my dear Joseph, there is no true practicality in smothering your abilities by working in a position where you cannot use your abilities. You will not be paid for abilities you cannot use, since I know you must think in financial terms. Your ego's job is to help you trade your true abilities for your daily bread.
quotes.translation: 自信的内在自我会让自我在物质世界中运作，但不会允许它变得过度保护。你的作品在许多方面都蕴含着内在自我的力量。你特定自我的功能，是向你所知的世界展示这部作品。我犹豫着，且我是认真的，不愿向一个试图如此务实的人提供实际建议。但亲爱的约瑟夫，通过在一个无法发挥你能力的工作岗位上埋没你的才能，这并非真正的务实。你无法因无法使用的能力而获得报酬，因为我知道你必须从财务角度思考。你自我的工作，是帮助你用真正的才能换取每日的面包。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It is the ability of such a unit of consciousness to utilize or create other forms and dimensions of itself that regulates the dimensions of the mental enclosure and of the effective self. You understand that about this mental or psychic enclosure the other elements of self are constructed, the physical image and its extensions into physical environment. This process, in various fields, continually goes on.
quotes.translation: 正是这种意识单位利用或创造自身其他形式和维度的能力，调节着精神围场和有效自我的维度。你明白，围绕这个精神或灵性围场，自我的其他元素被构建起来，包括物质形象及其向物质环境的延伸。这个过程在各个领域中持续进行着。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now, to return to an earlier discussion. What you call suggestion is indeed expectation. You should understand by now how the physical image is constructed. This construction is from the inside out. The inner self attempts to construct a physical image in line with its own self-image. Any errors of construction have their origin not in the inner self, but in either the personal subconscious or in the ego.
quotes.translation: 现在回到先前的讨论。你们所谓的暗示其实就是期待。你们现在应该明白物质形象是如何构建的。这种构建是由内而外的。内在自体试图根据其自身形象构建一个物质形象。任何构建上的错误并非源于内在自体，而是源于个人潜意识或自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Distortions occur at transitionary points, or at points of imbalances. There is a meshing then of data from various levels. A projection obviously need not be conscious. The waking self may be engaged in normal activities while the inner self is someplace else entirely. There are more brief periods of forgetfulness however than people usually realize, and in these periods often projections have occurred.
quotes.translation: 扭曲发生在过渡点或失衡点。来自不同层面的数据在此交织。投射显然不必是意识层面的。当醒时自我忙于日常活动时，内在自体可能完全身处别处。然而，人们通常意识不到的短暂失忆时段比想象中更多，在这些时段中往往发生了投射。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: "At once" does not imply a finished state of perfection nor a cosmic situation in which all things have been done, for all things are still happening. You are still happening -- but both present and future selves; and your past self is still undergoing what you think is done. Moreover, it is experiencing events that you do not recall, that your linear-attuned consciousness cannot perceive on that level.
quotes.translation: "当下"并不意味着一种完成的完美状态，也不是一种所有事情都已完成的宇宙情境，因为所有事情仍在发生。你仍在发生——但包括现在和未来的自体；而你过去的自体仍在经历你认为已经完成的事情。此外，它正在经历你无法回忆的事件，那是你线性调谐的意识在那个层面无法感知的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Such communication between these various selves who compose an entity is natural, continuous. The ego does not perceive the communications, obviously; but the ego, you must understand, is not self one alone, it is only a portion of self one, or the physical self.
quotes.translation: 构成一个存有的这些不同自体之间的这种交流是自然的、持续的。自我显然无法感知这些交流；但你必须明白，自我并非唯一自体，它只是自体一的一部分，或者说物质自体的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: When people are convinced that the self is untrustworthy, for whatever reasons, or that the universe is not safe, then instead of luxuriating in the use of their abilities, exploring the physical and mental environments, they begin to pull in their realities -- to contract their abilities, to overcontrol their environments. They become frightened people -- and frightened people do not want freedom, mental or physical. They want shelter, a definite set of rules. They want to be told what is good and bad. They lean toward compulsive behavior patterns. They seek out leaders -- political, scientific (humorously), or religious -- who will order their lives for them.
quotes.translation: 当人们出于任何原因确信自体不可信赖，或宇宙不安全时，他们就不再尽情运用自己的能力、探索物质和精神环境，而是开始收缩自己的实相——收缩自己的能力，过度控制自己的环境。他们变成了恐惧的人——而恐惧的人不想要精神或物质的自由。他们想要庇护所，一套明确的规则。他们希望被告知什么是好什么是坏。他们倾向于强迫性行为模式。他们寻找领袖——政治的、科学的（幽默地说）或宗教的——来为他们安排生活。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json
