I have spoken of this before, but it is important to remember that you impose a certain kind of "artificial" sense of exaggerated continuity even to the self you know. Your experience changes constantly, and so does the intimate context of your life -- but you concentrate upon points of order, in your terms, that actually serve to scale down the context of your experience to make it more comprehensible. There are no such limits naturally set about your consciousness.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，演员们在一定程度上意识到他们扮演的角色，并感受到艺术家技艺背后真正的个人身份。我之前谈过这一点，但重要的是要记住，即使对你所知道的自体，你也施加了一种特定"人为"的夸张连续感。你的经验不断变化，你生活的亲密背景也随之改变——但用你的话说，你专注于秩序点，这实际上是为了缩小你经验的背景，使其更易于理解。你的意识自然没有设置这样的限制。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I evolved to form my own entity. Ruburt will also, but he is not at that stage yet, in your terms. In another frame of reference he is, of course. He also contains those portions of himself that are less developed, for they all exist as one. All the parts of himself are aware of this correspondence. In your terms only, I could be referred to -- and I have told Ruburt this -- as a sixth self of his in his future; but this is only to get the idea across, for he will not become what I am. That is impossible. I am myself.
quotes.translation: 我进化形成了自己的本体。鲁柏也会如此，但用你们的话来说，他尚未达到那个阶段。当然，在另一个参考架构中，他已达成。他也包含自身那些较不发展的部分，因为它们皆为一体。他自身的所有部分都知晓这种对应关系。仅就你们的角度而言，我可以被称为——我已告诉过鲁柏——他未来时空中的第六个自己；但这只是为了传达概念，因为他不会变成我现在的样子。那是不可能的。我就是我自己。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some extra care as you plan then will also serve to liven the spirits of the spontaneous self. Ruburt's particular personality can also find refreshment at his job; as the earlier, still somewhat lingering but largely vanishing sensitivity vanishes, it can be a source of refreshment. It is less symbolic in a negative manner, you see, than it was. While he insisted upon this rigorous discipline for himself, he was caught between trying to inflict it upon the children, and by his natural tendency to enter into their spontaneous ways. This largely contributing to the difficulties after work, as a result of the tension.
quotes.translation: 你们在规划时多加留意，也能激发自发自己的活力。鲁柏的特定人格也能在工作中找到焕然一新的感觉；随着早期那种仍有些许残留但已大致消退的敏感度消失，工作可以成为焕发活力的源泉。你看，它在负面意义上的象征性已不如从前。当他坚持对自己施以严苛纪律时，他既试图将这种纪律强加于孩子，又受制于自己融入他们自发方式的自然倾向。这很大程度上导致了工作后的困难，正是紧张情绪的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You have by now become acquainted with a self you did not know before our sessions, a self who keeps watch upon both the ego and the subconscious. This is no longer theory to you, for you are practically aware of the self in an ever-growing fashion.
quotes.translation: 到如今，你已熟悉了一个在我们课程前你并不认识的自己——一个同时观察着自我与潜意识的自己。这对你而言已不再是理论，因为你正以不断增长的方式实际感知到这个自己。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: While Ruburt tells himself he is weak or sick, he causes the organism to behave in that manner. Dreams of health are of great benefit. He can tell himself that his dream self becomes his physical image. He knows his dream self is real. (Long pause.)
quotes.translation: 当鲁柏告诉自己他虚弱或生病时，他便促使有机体以那种方式运作。健康的梦境大有裨益。他可以告诉自己，他的梦自己变成了他的物质形象。他知道他的梦自己是真实的。（长停顿。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: (Another one-minute pause.) Give us a moment ... The "unknown" reality sustains you and the web of life as you understand it. Your conscious concepts must enlarge so that the conscious self can understand its true nature. As you think of it, consciousness is barely -- barely -- half developed. It has learned to identify with one small group of neurologically accepted responses. Portions of the brain not used lie latent, waiting for the recognition that will trigger them into activity (intently). When this happens, the mind will become aware of the rich bed of probabilities upon which the ego now rides so blindly.
quotes.translation: （又一分钟停顿。）给我们一点时间……"未知"的实相维系着你们以及你们所理解的生命之网。你们的意识概念必须扩展，以便意识自己能够理解其真实本质。就你们所想，意识仅仅——仅仅是——半发展状态。它已学会认同于一小群神经学上可接受的回应。大脑未使用的部分潜伏着，等待能触发它们活动的认知（专注地）。当这发生时，心智将意识到概率的丰饶沃土——自我如今正如此盲目地在其上驰骋。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Abilities unused by the waking personality are utilized in the dream state. A study of dream activities will often allow the waking personality to recognize abilities of which it is not aware, to discover talents that are not being used. Such a study can be most beneficial in allowing the personality to utilize all of its capabilities. There is no doubt that the whole self is a composite formed by the various aspects of the personality as it is seen in the waking and dream states, and at other levels of operation.
quotes.translation: 醒时人格未使用的能力在梦境中被运用。对梦境活动的研究常能使醒时人格认识到它未曾察觉的能力，发现未被使用的天赋。这样的研究极有益处，能让人格运用其全部能力。毫无疑问，整体自己是由人格在醒时状态、梦境状态及其他运作层面所呈现的各个面向组合而成的复合体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Various portions then of the entire self are attuned to their own intensities, picking up signals, interpreting only portions of the whole dream experience. I am using the term dream experience because it is a familiar word to you. However, the dream experience, you see, represents only a portion or particular range of intensities from a larger experience. In dreams you perceive but part.
quotes.translation: 整个自体的各个部分于是调准到各自的强度，接收信号，只诠释整体梦经验的片段。我使用"梦经验"这个术语，因为这是你们熟悉的词汇。然而，梦经验，你看，只代表来自更大经验中的一部分或特定强度范围。在梦中你只感知到部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now. No (underlined) physical condition is irreversible, with the exception of loss of limbs and definite organs. The personality is always free to choose, in your terms, its future. But the choice is with the inner self, which is the real identity. There are of course other elements entering into any such situation. Telepathically received suggestions from others, and so forth. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 现在。没有（强调）任何身体状态是不可逆转的，除了失去肢体和特定器官。人格总是自由选择，用你们的说法，它的未来。但选择权在于内在自体，那才是真正的身份。当然还有其他因素进入任何这类情境。比如来自他人的心电感应建议等等。（停顿。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Dialogues (see the 639th session in Chapter Ten) is now a book, just completed, but it also represented a movement of the self through a question-and-answer format, through which Ruburt recognized and faced many diverse beliefs. Each reader can utilize the same method whether or not artistic achievement is also involved, through objectifying personal beliefs in a dialogue form. This also happens frequently in the dream state, when you allow your natural creativity so much freedom. Often there are dreams in which "you" are two separate people, either strangers or familiar, each asking questions of the other.
quotes.translation: 《对话》（见第十章第639节）现在是一本书，刚刚完成，但它也代表了自体通过问答形式的一次运动，通过这种形式，鲁柏认出并面对了许多不同的信念。每位读者都可以运用同样的方法，无论是否涉及艺术成就，通过以对话形式客观化个人信念。这在梦状态中也经常发生，当你允许你的自然创造力如此自由时。常常有梦，其中"你"是两个不同的人，无论是陌生人还是熟人，彼此互相提问。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... This is characterized perhaps most of all by more perceptive psychological organizations. In the predream state you participate in such organizations, although you bring back home to your physical self -- in the form of dreams -- only data that can be recognized and used in physical terms. It is highly important to remember that your experience and knowledge grow at those other levels of actuality. Even during your physical lifetime your experience is not confined to conventional physical events alone. Those usual events arise from the creative impetus that occurs at these other levels.
quotes.translation: 给我们一点时间……这或许最显著的特征是更具洞察力的心理组织。在梦前状态中，你参与这些组织，尽管你带回给物质自体的——以梦的形式——只有那些能在物质层面被认出和使用的数据。记住你的经验和知识在这些其他实相层面增长，这一点非常重要。即使在你的物质生命期间，你的经验也不仅限于常规的物质事件。那些通常的事件源于在这些其他层面发生的创造性推动。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The sensations are a physical demonstration of an inner sense reaction on a psychic basis, or level, which you have not yet achieved, and cannot be expected to. The self would expand. In your relationship with your parents for example, such a psychic expansion of self would allow you to absorb your own awareness of their sometimes painful existence, and you would feel no wound yourself.
quotes.translation: 这些感觉是内在感官反应在心灵基础或层面上的物质展现，你尚未达到，也不能期望达到。自体将会扩展。例如，在你与父母的关系中，这种自体的心灵扩展会让你吸收你对他们有时痛苦存在的觉察，而你自己不会感到受伤。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: So the whole self turns various portions of its whole consciousness on or off, according to the field in which the whole self is endeavoring to make contact, manipulations, and according to the field in which he is endeavoring to project himself.
quotes.translation: 所以整个自体根据它努力接触、操控的领域，以及根据它努力投射自身的领域，开启或关闭其整个意识的不同部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: In all instances of ill health, the psychic inner forces are being misdirected. The aim of medicine should then be to aid the inner self to direct its own energy along other lines. Now. With the above, the psychic forces may be misdirected in several main ways, and with several resulting malfunctions. Energy may not be properly directed toward releasing from the physical field of the body excess chemicals into other fields. A buildup of such chemicals can be deadly, leading to among other things forms of insanity, schizophrenia, and other disorders which show themselves in mental or personality symptoms.
quotes.translation: 在所有健康不佳的案例中，心灵内在力量正被误导。医学的目标应是协助内在自我将其能量导向其他方向。现在。基于上述，心灵力量可能通过几种主要方式被误导，并导致若干功能失调。能量可能未被适当导向，以将体内过剩的化学物质从物质场域释放到其他场域。此类化学物质的累积可能致命，导致精神错乱、精神分裂症及其他表现为精神或人格症状的失调。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In other words, your accepted concepts of selfhood would disappear if you ever allowed any significant subjective experience to intrude. "The Absent Self" -- the absent or unknown self -- is the portion of your own existence that you do not ordinarily perceive or accept, though there is within you a longing for it.
quotes.translation: 换言之，你若允许任何重大主观经验侵入，你所接受的自性概念便会消失。"缺席的自性"——缺席或未知的自性——是你自身存在中通常未察觉或未接纳的部分，尽管你内心对其怀有渴望。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 731
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You will not find yourself by running from teacher to teacher, from book to book. You will not meet yourself through following any particular specialized method of meditation. Only by looking quietly within the self that you know can your own reality be experienced, with those connections that exist between the present or immediate self and the inner identity that is multidimensional.
quotes.translation: 你不会通过从一个老师跑到另一个老师、从一本书跑到另一本书来找到自己。你不会通过遵循任何特定的冥想方法来遇见自己。只有通过静观你所知的自性，才能体验到你自身的实相，以及当下或直接自性与多维度内在身份之间的那些联系。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You see, the work involved in these experiments will of itself allow you to be extremely flexible, and you will discover finally that you can bring a portion of your consciousness with you while you sleep. A portion of the self will be investigating other portions of the self. We shall also have you using suggestion to bring about certain types of dreams in which we may be interested.
quotes.translation: 你看，这些实验所涉及的工作本身将允许你变得极其灵活，最终你会发现，你可以在睡眠时携带一部分意识。自性的一部分将探索自性的其他部分。我们还将让你运用暗示来引发某些我们可能感兴趣的梦境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: There would be no psychological avenues to connect my world and yours. There would be no extensions of the self that would allow you to travel such a psychological distance to those thresholds of reality that form my mental environment. If the universe were structured as you have been told, the probability of my existence would be zero as far as you are concerned. There would have been no unofficial roads for Ruburt to follow, to lead him from the official beliefs of his time. He would never have acknowledged the original impulse to speak for me, and my voice would have been unheard in your world.
quotes.translation: 将没有任何心理途径连接我的世界与你的世界。将没有任何自性的延伸允许你跨越如此心理距离，抵达构成我精神环境的那些实相门槛。如果宇宙如你所被告知的那样构建，就你而言，我存在的概率将为零。将没有非官方路径供鲁柏遵循，以引导他脱离其时代的官方信仰。他永远不会承认最初为我代言的本能冲动，而我的声音在你的世界中将无人听闻。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Our friend did not have cancer. See Note 2 for Session 805.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Emotion and imagination are being considered as far superior. The displaced powers of consciousness are still being assigned to the unconscious, and great efforts are being made to reach what seem to be normally inaccessible areas of awareness. To this end drugs are utilized, cults set up, and there are methods and training manuals galore. Period. Yet there is nothing basically inaccessible about such "inner knowledge or experience." It can all be quite conscious, and utilized to enrich the reality that you know. The conscious mind is not some prodigal child or poor relative of the self. It can quite freely focus into inner reality when you understand that it can. You, again, have a conscious mind. You can change the focus of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 情感与想象力正被视为远为优越。意识中被置换的力量仍被归于无意识，人们正付出巨大努力去触及那些看似通常无法触及的意识领域。为此，药物被利用，教派被建立，还有大量方法与训练手册。句号。然而，这类"内在知识或经验"本质上并无不可触及之处。它们完全可以成为有意识的，并被用来丰富你所知的实相。有意识心智并非自性的某个浪子或穷亲戚。当你理解它能够时，它可以相当自由地聚焦于内在实相。再次强调，你拥有有意识心智。你可以改变自身意识的焦点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: What I am saying here applies to the greater identity of each reader. Give us a moment ... Because you are usually so worried about preserving what you think of as your identity, we use terms like reincarnational selves or counterparts. If you truly understood the nature of your individuality, however, you would clearly see that there is no contradiction if I say that you are uniquely yourself, that your individuality has an indestructible validity that is never assailed, and when I also say that you are at the same time connected with other identities, each as sacredly inviolate as your own.
quotes.translation: 我在此所言适用于每位读者的更大身份。请稍等片刻……由于你们通常过于担忧维护自认为的身份，我们便使用转世自我或对应人物这类术语。然而，若你们真正理解自身个体性的本质，便会清楚看见：当我说你们是独一无二的自己、你们的个体性具有永不磨灭的有效性且永不受侵犯时，同时又说你们与其他身份相连——每个身份都如你们自身般神圣不可侵犯——这两者之间毫无矛盾。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 740
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The basic source of your difficulty was not new. It did not even, except superficially, represent a dangerous lapse or relapse on your part, into those truly dangerous and quite disastrous negative battles of thoughts, which eventually in any individual can and often do lead the integrated self into annihilation. The illness did represent, however, a needed warning, materialized into physical reality as illness. A warning that after all there had been a recent tendency on your part, though slight, to slide into negative thinking. The illness was meant to bring you up short, to make you think.
quotes.translation: 你困境的根本源头并非新事。表面上它甚至不构成你思想中危险倒退或复发——那种最终在任何个体中都可能导致整合自我湮灭的真正危险且极具破坏性的负面思想战役。然而，这场疾病确实代表了必要的警告，以疾病形式物质化为物理实相。这警告表明：毕竟你近期存在（虽轻微）滑向负面思维的倾向。疾病旨在让你猛然警醒，迫使你思考。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The physical brain as you know it is but the camouflage mechanism belonging to the physically oriented self. In some cases, severe damage is done to the brain, and yet rationality continues. In these cases the mind behind the brain operates as always. When the individual is convinced that his activities are completely dependent upon the physical brain, then he is incapacitated almost completely by any injury to it. The brain itself contains knowledge that comes through the inner senses, and this knowledge would simply not be available to it, if it were dependent upon physical data completely.
quotes.translation: 你所知的物质大脑，不过是属于物质导向自我的伪装机制。某些情况下，大脑遭受严重损伤，理性却依然延续。此时大脑背后的心智如常运作。当个体坚信其活动完全依赖物质大脑时，任何大脑损伤都会使其几乎完全丧失能力。大脑本身包含通过内在感官获得的知识——若完全依赖物质数据，这些知识根本不可能为大脑所获取。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: For one thing, while pain is unpleasant it is also a method of familiarizing the self against the edges of quickened consciousness. Any heightened sensation, pleasant or unpleasant, has a stimulating effect upon a consciousness to some degree. It is a strong awareness of activity and life. Where the stimulus may be extremely annoying, and humiliatingly unpleasant, certain portions of the psychological framework accept it indiscriminatingly because it is a sensation, and a vivid one. This acquiescence to even painful stimuli is a basic part of the nature of consciousness, and a necessary one.
quotes.translation: 首先，痛苦虽令人不适，却也是让自我熟悉意识加速边缘的方法。任何强烈感受（无论愉悦与否）都在某种程度上对意识产生刺激作用。这是对活动与生命的强烈觉知。当刺激可能极度恼人且令人羞辱地不适时，心理架构的某些部分会不加区分地接纳它——因为它是种感受，且是鲜活的感受。这种对痛苦刺激的默许，是意识本质的基本组成部分，且是必要的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Such alternative reactions frighten the ego because they seem to injure the ego's self-image. Yet all characteristic reactions, whether denied by the ego or not, are kept for use as alternative actions. In many cases actions unacceptable to the ego may be precisely those actions that are necessary for whole other areas of the personality. When too many actions are restricted by the ego, they may begin to form impulse patterns or groupings of various rejected impulses. These then adhere through attraction, and attempt to find expression regardless of the ego's attempts to restrict expression.
quotes.translation: 这类替代性反应令自我恐惧，因其似乎损害自我的自我形象。然而所有特征性反应（无论是否被自我否认）都被保留作为替代行动。在许多情况下，自我无法接受的行动，恰恰可能是人格其他完整领域所需的行动。当过多行动被自我限制时，它们可能开始形成各种被拒绝冲动的冲动模式或集群。这些冲动通过吸引力相互依附，并试图不顾自我限制表达的企图而寻求表达。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: While you go about your daily chores and endeavors, beneath normal waking consciousness you are constantly focused in other realities also, reacting to stimuli of which your physical conscious self is not aware, perceiving conditions through the inner senses, and experiencing events that are not even registered within the physical brain. That whole last portion should be underlined.
quotes.translation: 当你处理日常琐事与努力时，在正常清醒意识之下，你同时持续聚焦于其他实相，对物质意识自我未察觉的刺激做出反应，通过内在感官感知状况，经历甚至未在物质大脑中记录的事件。最后整段应加下划线。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This allows the inner self to work much more quickly and effectively. You can trick normal consciousness quite easily with baubles, take its attention into other areas so that the inner self is left alone to do its work. Now give us a moment.
quotes.translation: 这能让内在自体更快速有效地运作。你可以用些小玩意轻易欺骗普通意识，将它的注意力引向其他领域，好让内在自体不受干扰地完成工作。现在请稍等片刻。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: What you call negative suggestions represent discordant actions. Unless the main reasons are discovered, the distortive actions will keep reoccurring. In many cases quite a simple exercise will almost immediately begin to make an improvement. With these exercises you are familiar, for in your psychological time the self is momentarily in connection with its more extensive portions.
quotes.translation: 你所谓的负面暗示代表着不协调的行动。除非找出主要原因，否则扭曲的行为会不断重复。在许多情况下，一个相当简单的练习几乎能立即开始改善状况。这些练习你都很熟悉，因为在你的心理时间里，自体暂时与它更广大的部分取得了联系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Now when you use the word I, more and more, this is the portion of the self of which I spoke in our last session. You are aware of the ego and able to see yourself as something more than the ego is. This is the I that you have been using in the pendulum sessions, that questions both the ego and subconscious. These are all portions of the self, however; the difference is functional.
quotes.translation: 现在当你越来越多地使用"我"这个字时，这正是我们在上次课中谈到的自体部分。你觉察到自我，并能将自己视为比自我更宏大的存在。这就是你在钟摆课程中一直使用的那个"我"，它既质疑自我也质疑潜意识。然而这些都是自体的不同部分；区别在于功能层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Again, when you are in a state that is not the normal waking one, when you have forsaken this daily self, you are nevertheless conscious and alert. You merely block out the memory from the normal waking ego. So when the attributes of consciousness are given, creativity is largely ignored. It is assigned instead primarily to the unconscious. My point is that the unconscious is conscious. Creativity is one of the most important attributes and aspects of consciousness. We will differentiate between normal ego consciousness then, and consciousness that only appears unconscious to that ego.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，当你处于非正常清醒状态时，当你暂时放下这个日常自体时，你依然保持清醒与警觉。你只是阻断了正常清醒自我的记忆。因此当意识的属性被赋予时，创造力在很大程度上被忽视了。它反而被主要归因于无意识。我的重点是：无意识是有意识的。我们将区分正常的自我意识，以及那些在自我看来似乎无意识的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: (11:39.) Generally you experience the self as isolated from nature, and primarily enclosed within your skin. Early man did not feel like an empty shell, and yet selfhood existed for him as much outside of the body as within it. There was a constant interaction. It is easy to say to you that such people could identify, say, with the trees, but an entirely different thing to try to explain what it would be like for a mother to become so a part of the tree underneath which her children played that she could keep track of them from the tree's viewpoint, though she was herself far away.
quotes.translation: （11:39）通常你体验到的自体是与自然隔绝的，主要封闭在皮肤之内。早期人类并不觉得自己像个空壳，但对那时的他们而言，自体存在于身体之外与之内同样真实。存在着持续不断的互动。对你们说这些人能与树木认同很容易，但要解释一位母亲如何能成为孩子玩耍的树的一部分，即使她身在远方仍能从树的视角看顾孩子，这完全是另一回事。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner self, which has been called the soul, has connections through the entire physical organism, and is not concentrated in any one portion. More adjustments are made by the physical organism than you know, and when I say that it exists in many fields, I mean that it actually not only appears within them, but is a part of them in an intimate manner, that the physical organism as it is materialized within your universe is actually a coming together and merging that has its existence, and is a blending of data from many planes, that would be considered foreign by the intellect.
quotes.translation: 被称为灵魂的内在自体，通过整个生理有机体建立连接，并不集中于任何特定部位。生理有机体所做的调整远比你认知的更多，当我说它存在于多个场域时，我的意思是它不仅出现在这些场域中，而且以亲密的方式成为它们的一部分——这个在你们宇宙中物质化的生理有机体，实际上是来自多个层面的数据汇聚融合而成，这些数据在知性看来会显得陌生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You are midway here. In the future the ego and the intellect will expand sufficiently so that they can contain and use and appreciate all the other functions and portions of the self which they now mistrust. Individual identity will then expand to include a greater variety of impulses and stimuli, which do not necessarily come from the self, and yet maintain specialized identity.
quotes.translation: 你正处于中间地带。未来，自我与智性将充分扩展，足以容纳、运用并珍视它们如今所不信任的自我其他功能与部分。届时个体身份将扩展至包含更丰富的冲动与刺激——这些未必源自自我本身，却仍能维系专精化的身份认同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: This predictive picture is then set against two models. First it is checked against the body's ideal standard of health in its individual case -- its own greatest fulfillment. Then it is checked against the image of the body sent to it by the conscious self. Correlations are made instantaneously. In an organizational framework that would certainly be envied by the most advanced technological concern, communications spring back and forth with great rapidity. The body makes whatever changes are necessary in order to bring the two images in line with the present corporeal condition.
quotes.translation: 这个预测性图像随后与两种模型进行比对。首先对照个体案例中身体健康的理想标准——其自身最极致的圆满状态。其次对照意识自我传递给它的身体意象。这些关联在瞬间完成。在令最先进科技企业都艳羡的组织架构中，信息以惊人速度往复传递。身体会进行必要调整，使两种意象与当前肉体状态保持一致。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "His ideas as he tried to explain them to you earlier this evening are excellent. They revive him psychologically, particularly with your help, as he discusses them. The sinful-self material is 'timed' in its own fashion so that although there is a good deal of material already written, its effects are periodic--that is, they are clued to spring into even greater insight, which may not be apparent at any one given time. It is important that he tells you when he does feel panicky. However, the feeling itself does not last long. Relaxation, again, is a part of the creative process.
quotes.translation: "他今晚早些时候试图向你解释的那些想法非常出色。这些想法在心理上令他焕发新生——尤其在你协助讨论时。'罪孽自我'资料以独特方式'定时'呈现：尽管已积累大量文字，其效应却是周期性的——即它们被设计为在特定时刻迸发更深层的洞见，这种洞见未必在任一给定时间点显现。重要的是，当他确实感到恐慌时能及时告知你。不过这种感受本身不会持续太久。放松，再次强调，是创造过程的一部分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 7. In Chapter 2 of Dreams, in Volume 1, see Note 1 for Session 885, which we held on October 24, 1979. Through a series of misunderstandings, the people at Ankh-Hermes had published an abridged translation of Seth Speaks without having permission for the cutting from Jane and me. We still feel regret that the company had to go to all of that extra expense in order to publish a second edition of the book. I also wrote that "all concerned must wait for at least another year before a full-length version of Seth Speaks will be published in the Dutch language." Actually, almost two years passed before we received our copies of the new edition.

quotes.text: This is why I have mentioned earlier that the individual was supremely important. Yet the self limits itself through boundaries that are arbitrary and erected through fear, and also through habit that originally had its source in the necessity for physical survival. Physical survival does not demand these habits any longer, and they remain as shackles. You should know by now that individual thought does not remain within the boundary of the physical individual. This indeed has actually been proven, in so far as telepathy is recognized as a fact, at least by some, and soon by all.
quotes.translation: 正因如此，我先前才强调个体的至高重要性。然而自我通过恐惧筑起的武断边界，以及源于肉体生存必需却已成桎梏的旧习，限制了自身。肉体生存已不再需要这些习惯，它们却仍如枷锁般存在。此刻你应当明白：个体思想并不囿于肉体个体的边界。事实上，当心灵感应被至少部分人——很快将是所有人——承认为事实时，这已被实际证明。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Nevertheless, when you think in terms of the entity and its various personality manifestations throughout your earth time then you realize that the basic self, the entity, cannot be destroyed in any particular earth life. You are, I know, acquainted with the idea of regeneration. If you will imagine the various reincarnated personalities--and this is a most unpleasant analogy--as the various limbs and other faculties of the entity, then you will see why if one fragment is disintegrated it can be regenerated in the same manner that a physical cell of your body can be regenerated.
quotes.translation: 然而，当你从本体及其在地球时间中各种人格显化的角度思考时，便会领悟：基本自我（即本体）不会在任何特定地球生命中消亡。我知道你熟悉"再生"的概念。若你将各种转世人格——这是个极不恰当的类比——想象为本体的不同肢体与官能，便会明白为何当某个碎片瓦解时，它能以与你身体细胞再生相同的方式重生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Your own environment includes far more than you may have supposed. Earlier I referred to your environment in terms of the daily physical existence and surroundings with which you are currently connected. In actuality, you are aware of very little of your larger, more extensive environment. Consider your present self as an actor in a play; hardly a new analogy, but a suitable one. The scene is set in the twentieth century. You create the props, the settings, the themes; in fact you write, produce, and act in the entire production -- you and every other individual who takes part.
quotes.translation: 你自身的环境远比你想象的广阔。此前我提及的环境，是指你当前关联的日常物质存在与周遭环境。实际上，你对自身更宏大、更广袤的环境所知甚少。将你当下的自我视为戏剧中的演员——这虽非新颖比喻，却颇为贴切。场景设定在二十世纪。你创造道具、布景、主题；事实上，整场演出都由你编写、制作并主演——你与每位参与者皆如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The ego takes care of physical matters so that the inner self can go about its other concerns. To shunt it aside is highly dangerous. The inner self is then in a precarious position, for it must also attempt to deal with physical reality.
quotes.translation: 自我处理物质事务，以便内在自体得以处理其他关切。将其搁置一旁是极其危险的。内在自体因此处于岌岌可危的境地，因为它也必须尝试应对物质实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The inner self does not feel threatened, therefore, only the part of the personality that is momentarily unaware of the true situation. Others also respond, and are in this drama for their own reasons. (Long pause.) The drama has a theme, and through working out the problems therein presented the theme becomes clear. In some psychological circles there are therapeutic groups that operate by setting up such dramas. Remember, the purpose is therapeutic, though it may not appear so. And none of this should be taken to mean that the sufferings are not quite real to those involved.
quotes.translation: 因此，内在自体并不感到威胁，只有人格中暂时不明真相的部分才会如此。其他人也做出回应，并出于各自的原因参与这场戏剧。（长停顿。）这场戏剧有一个主题，通过解决其中呈现的问题，主题变得清晰。在某些心理学圈子里，有治疗团体通过设置此类戏剧来运作。记住，目的是治疗性的，尽管可能看起来并非如此。这也不应被理解为对参与者而言，痛苦并非真实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: When you leave the physical system after reincarnations, you have learned the lessons, and you are literally no longer a member of the human race in those terms, for you elect to leave it. Only the conscious self dwells within it in any case, and it is other portions of your personality who simultaneously dwell within the other training systems. In other more advanced systems, thoughts and emotions are automatically and immediately translated into action, into camouflage, into whatever approximation of matter there exists. Therefore the lessons must be taught and learned well.
quotes.translation: 当你在转世后离开物质系统时，你已经学会了功课，从那些角度来说，你实际上不再是人类种族的一员，因为你选择离开它。无论如何，只有意识自我栖居其中，而你人格的其他部分同时栖居于其他训练系统。在其他更先进的系统中，思想和情感会自动且立即转化为行动、伪装、以及任何近似物质的存在。因此，功课必须被教导并学好。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Science delegates the world of nature as the realm of exterior natural events. Its view of nature is therefore mechanistic. The natural self, however, like the rest of nature, possesses a rich dimension of inside psychological depth, that science, because of its own definitions, cannot perceive. Telepathy and clairvoyance, for example, are a part of natural effects, but they belong to a nature so much more expansive than science's definitions that they have been made to appear as highly unnatural eccentricities of behavior, rather than as natural components of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 科学将自然世界视为外在自然事件的领域。因此，它对自然的看法是机械论的。然而，自然自体，如同自然的其他部分一样，拥有丰富的内在心理深度维度，而科学因其自身的定义无法察觉。例如，心灵感应和千里眼是自然效应的一部分，但它们属于一种远比科学定义更广阔的自然，以至于它们被表现为高度不自然的异常行为，而非意识的自然组成部分。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: Because Ruburt's background in this life was particularly bizarre in a way and certainly unpleasant, he became beautifully efficient as far as sensing the approach of psychic storms is concerned and very adept at sensing moods, and also amazingly efficient in learning self-protective devices. It is true that Ruburt can also be trapped by his own capacity for protection. In his case this amounts to a stubborn refusal to see what he does not want to see. However this mechanism seldom actually goes haywire in this manner. This represents merely a possibility that could occur.
quotes.translation: 因为鲁柏在此生的背景在某种程度上特别怪异且当然不愉快，他在感知心灵风暴来临方面变得异常高效，非常擅长感知情绪，并且在学习自我保护机制方面也惊人地高效。确实，鲁柏也可能被他自己的保护能力所困。就他而言，这相当于固执地拒绝看到他不愿看到的东西。然而，这种机制很少真的以这种方式失控。这仅仅代表一种可能发生的情况。

quotes.text: Because you identify your experience with the regular line of consciousness with which you are familiar, you are rarely able to "bring in" any "other-self" material and hold it while retaining your own sense of identity. Such material may at times bleed or intrude into your own thought, where it blends and is not recognized. In such cases, it takes on the coloration of your own thought patterns. It adds to the overall atmosphere of your being. Without understanding or training, you would have to "lose" your own consciousness in order to perceive the "other-consciousness."
quotes.translation: 因为你将你的体验与你熟悉的常规意识线认同，你很少能在保持自身身份感的同时“引入”任何“其他自体”材料并保持它。这类材料有时可能渗入或侵入你的思想，在那里混合而不被识别。在这种情况下，它会染上你自身思维模式的色彩。它为你存在的整体氛围增添内容。没有理解或训练，你将不得不“失去”你自己的意识才能感知“其他意识”。

quotes.text: The unknown reality and the psyche's greater existence cannot be separated from the intimate knowledge of the flesh, however, for the life of the flesh takes place within that framework. As earlier mentioned,4 the conscious self generally focuses in but one small dimension. Period. That dimension is experienced as fully as possible, its clear brilliance and exquisite focus possible only because you tune in to it and bring it to the forefront of your attention. In your terms, when you understand how to do this, then you can begin to tune in to other "stations" as well.
quotes.translation: 未知实相与心灵更广大的存在，无法与肉身的亲密知识分离，因为肉身生命正是在那个架构内发生的。如前所述，有意识的自我通常只聚焦于一个小维度。仅此而已。这个维度被尽可能充分地体验，其清晰的璀璨与精微的聚焦之所以可能，只因你调准频率进入它，并将其带到你注意力的最前沿。用你们的话说，当你理解如何做到这一点时，你就能开始调准进入其他"频道"。

quotes.text: The inner self therefore uses the physical system to express its own inclinations and ideas of reality. It is the inner self that unfolds those patterns of behavior spoken about in the article you read on infant growth. (In a recent issue of Time magazine.) Such unfoldings always occur from within, responding to signals not from the exterior but from the interior environment.
quotes.translation: 因此，内在自己运用物质系统来表达自身的倾向与对实相的理念。正是内在自己展开了你读到的那篇关于婴儿成长的文章中所说的行为模式（见于近期《时代》杂志）。这种展开总是从内在发生，响应来自内在环境而非外在环境的信号。

quotes.text: The theory of evolution,4 for instance, is an imaginative construct, and yet through its lights some generations now have viewed their world. It is not only that you think of yourselves differently, but you actually experience a different kind of self. Your institutions change their aspects accordingly, so that experience fits the beliefs that you have about it. You act in certain ways. You view the entire universe in a fashion that did not exist before, so that imagination and belief intangibly structure your subjective experience and your objective circumstances.
quotes.translation: 例如，进化论是一个富有想象力的建构，然而透过它的光芒，几代人已经这样看待他们的世界。不仅是你以不同的方式思考自己，而且你实际上体验到了不同种类的自己。你们的制度相应地改变了面貌，使经验符合你们对它所持有的信念。你们以特定方式行动。你们以过去不存在的方式看待整个宇宙，因此想象与信念无形中构建了你们的主观经验与客观环境。

quotes.text: My remark has nothing to do with your accepted concepts of the unconscious portions of the self. Your ideas of the unconscious are so linked to your limited ideas of personhood as to be meaningless in this discussion. It is as if you used only one finger of one hand, and then said: "This is the proper expression of my personhood." It is not just that there are other functions of the mind, unused, but that in those terms you have other minds. You have one brain, it is true, but you allow it to use only one station, or to identify itself with only one mind of many.
quotes.translation: 我的评论与你们所接受的自我的无意识部分的概念无关。你们对无意识的观念如此局限于你们对人格的有限概念，以至于在此讨论中毫无意义。这就像你只用一只手的一根手指，然后说："这是我人格的恰当表达。"不仅存在未被使用的其他心灵功能，而且就那些术语而言，你们拥有其他心灵。你们确实有一个大脑，但你们只允许它使用一个频道，或只让它认同众多心灵中的一个。

quotes.text: One symbol will be meaningful to many portions of the personality. The symbol will be the same. That is, any given symbol will be the same, but it will be so chosen by the mind that it will have definite meanings to various portions of the self, and the meanings may be quite different. In the breaking-down process the intensities are separated into the most minute values, each value an electrical impulse representing any one of many references. And all of these references are contained within a given impulse, electrical impulse, that will be decoded by the mind.
quotes.translation: 一个象征将对人格的许多部分有意义。这个象征将是相同的。也就是说，任何给定的象征都是相同的，但它会被心灵如此选择，以至于对自我的各个部分具有明确的意义，而这些意义可能大不相同。在分解过程中，强度被分离成最细微的值，每个值都是一个电脉冲，代表众多参照中的任何一个。所有这些参照都包含在一个给定的脉冲、电脉冲中，而心灵将解码这个脉冲。

quotes.text: The subconscious, so-called, is aware to some extent of the ego, regarding it as an extension of itself, over which it does not have as much control as it would like. This is precisely however the way the ego views the subconscious, as a rule. These two self-conscious portions of the self simply happen to coincide or to coexist with some proximity, psychologically speaking.
quotes.translation: 所谓的潜意识在某种程度上觉察到自我，将其视为自身的延伸，但它对自我的控制并不如它所愿。然而，这正是自我通常看待潜意识的方式。从心理学角度来说，自我的这两个有意识部分只是恰好以某种接近度共存或并存。

quotes.text: Now the inner ego is the organizer of experience that Jung would call unconscious. The inner ego is another term for what we call the inner self. As the outer ego manipulates within the environment and physical reality, so the inner ego or self organizes and manipulates within an inner reality. The inner ego creates that physical reality with which the outer ego then deals.
quotes.translation: 内在自我是荣格称为无意识的经验组织者。内在自我是我们所称的内在自性的另一个术语。如同外在自我在环境和物质实相中运作，内在自我或自性则在内在实相中组织与运作。内在自我创造了外在自我随后要应对的物质实相。

quotes.text: The releases in his personality could have been achieved in other, easier methods. Nevertheless without these alterations our work could not be fulfilled in all the ways possible. His rigidity of attitude resulted in a trend for self destruction, tempered it is true, but dangerous. There was a resultant manufacture of various chemicals within his system that tended to reproduce and perpetuate the depressed state of mind. An overproduction of adrenaline that kept him stirred up, but also an overproduction of certain chemicals that physically slowed him down.
quotes.translation: 他性格中的释放本可通过其他更简单的方式达成。然而没有这些改变，我们的工作就无法在所有可能的层面圆满实现。他僵化的态度导致了自我毁灭的倾向——虽有所缓和，但依然危险。这造成他体内产生各种化学物质，不断复制并延续抑郁心境。肾上腺素过量分泌使他持续亢奋，但某些化学物质的过度产生又使身体机能迟缓。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: The side which is open allows free flow of energy from the inner self, but momentarily the outlet for this energy is blocked up. This holding back, this gathering and collection without instant outlet, is one of the natural and constant processes involved not only in the construction of energy into matter, but in the construction of energy into any other form, and it is closely allied to the pause and pulsation that I have mentioned, as atoms and molecules appear and disappear imperceptibly, even while they seem to give continuity to any particular object.
quotes.translation: 敞开的那一面允许内在自性的能量自由流动，但此刻能量的出口暂时被阻塞。这种阻滞、这种能量聚集而无法即时释放的过程，不仅是能量转化为物质时必经的自然过程，也是能量转化为任何其他形态时持续发生的机制。这与我曾提及的原子分子在赋予物体连续性时，看似稳定实则隐微地生灭起伏的节律密切相关。

quotes.text: It is this basic feeling about the book and Saratoga that suddenly activated past associations and brought on some identification with his mother. He should remember here that he is not the person who lived in Saratoga now, unless he chooses in a self limiting way to be so. The book itself, oddly enough, provides a certain protection for it informs others of his basic strength. It shocked him to know that people of the past were reading the book in his present, and seemed to draw him closer to those original associations that caused him to leave Saratoga.
quotes.translation: 正是对这本书和萨拉托加的基本感受，突然激活了过往的联想，引发了他与母亲的某种认同。他应当记住，此刻他并非当年住在萨拉托加的那个人——除非他选择以自我设限的方式认定如此。奇怪的是，这本书本身提供了某种保护，因为它向他人揭示了他的根本力量。得知过去的人们正在他当下的时空阅读这本书，令他震惊，这似乎将他拉近那些促使他离开萨拉托加的原初联想。

quotes.text: Ideas of good and evil are exaggerated, cut off from each other. Yet here again the creative abilities are allowed some expression. The person does not feel able to express them otherwise. Such people are afraid of the brunt of their own personalities. They have been taught that energy is wrong, that power is disastrous, and that the impulses of the self are to be feared.
quotes.translation: 善恶观念被夸大且彼此割裂。然而在此情况下，创造能力仍获得某种表达空间。当事人无法以其他方式表达这些能力。这类人畏惧自身人格的冲击力。他们被教导能量是错误的，力量是灾难性的，自性的冲动应当被恐惧。

quotes.text: Your attentions are indeed focused elsewhere. You are in a trance as well as Ruburt is in a trance state now. This is far from unusual. I use you, dear Doctor Instream, only as an example. Consciousness of any kind is merely the direction in which the self looks. I told you this at our brief meeting. Consciousness is the focus, the direction of focus. Your ordinary consciousness is as much a trance state as any trance state induced through hypnotism. Therefore it is nearly impossible to convince a subject in trance that something he does not see exists.
quotes.translation: 你的注意力确实聚焦在别处。你此刻与鲁柏一样处于出神状态。这绝非罕见现象。亲爱的因斯庄博士，我仅以你为例。任何形式的意识都只是自性注视的方向。在我们短暂的会面中我已告知你：意识即是焦点，是聚焦的方向。你平常的意识状态与催眠诱导出的任何出神状态同样是一种出神状态。因此，几乎不可能让处于出神状态的受试者相信他看不见的事物确实存在。

quotes.text: Now: I bring up these matters here because such changes in habitual patterns would definitely result in greater understanding of the nature of the self. The inner dreaming portions of the personality seem strange to you not only because of a basic difference of focus, but because you clearly devote opposite portions of a twenty-four hour cycle to these areas of the self.
quotes.translation: 现在：我在此提出这些议题，是因为习惯模式的改变必然会导致对自我本质更深刻的理解。人格的内在梦境部分对你而言显得陌生，不仅因为关注焦点的根本差异，更因为你将二十四小时周期中截然不同的时段专门用于探索自我的这些领域。

quotes.text: The whole self not only perceives these limitless moment points, but being a part of action, each whole self projects fragments and personalities from itself to all these points, creating therefore other egos, other intense focus points which are independent, which work out their own destinies and experiences, which in turn perceive any given moment point in slow motion.
quotes.translation: 完整自我不仅感知这些无限的时间点，作为行动的一部分，每个完整自我都会从自身投射出片段与人格至所有这些时间点，从而创造出其他自我、其他独立的强烈焦点——它们各自谱写命运与经历，反过来以慢动作感知任何特定时间点。

quotes.text: The self that you are aware of represents only one "position" in which those invisible particles happen to intersect, gain mass, build up form. Scientists can only perceive an electron as it is to them. They cannot really track it. They cannot be certain of its position and its speed at the same time, and to some extent the same applies to your consciousness. The speed of your own thoughts takes those thoughts away from you even as you think them--and you can never really examine a thought, but only the thought of a thought (with quiet amusement).
quotes.translation: 你所觉察的自我仅代表那些不可见粒子偶然交汇、获得质量、构建形态的某个"位置"。科学家只能感知电子对其显现的样貌，无法真正追踪它。他们无法同时确定其位置与速度，某种程度上你的意识也是如此。你思想的速流甚至在你思考时就将思想带离——你永远无法真正检视一个思想，只能检视思想的影子（带着温和的戏谑）。

quotes.text: Its sense of identity spontaneously goes beyond the change of its own form. It has no ego to cut the "I" identification short. Creatures without the compartment of the ego can easily follow their own identities beyond any changes of form. The inner self is aware of this integrity of identity, but the ego focused so securely in physical reality cannot afford this luxury.
quotes.translation: 其身份认同感自发超越自身形态的变化。它没有自我意识来截断"我"的认同。没有自我意识隔间的生灵能轻易追随自身认同超越任何形态变化。内在自我深知这种身份的完整性，但固着于物质实相的自我意识却无福消受这份奢侈。

source_raw.title: (First I thought of Seth's assertion in the 92nd session for September 28, 1964, that "trees have their dreams"; [in Volume 1, see the quotations in Note 1 for Session 698]. Then I remembered that he'd come through with extensive material on tree consciousness in a much earlier session. With Jane's help the next day I found that information in the 18th session, which had been held on January 22, 1964. Most of that session is unpublished, although someday we'd like to print it in full. It contains many intriguing ideas -- as, for example: "A tree knows a human being also ... [yet it] does not even build up an image of a man, which is why this is so difficult to explain ... And the same tree will recognize the same man who passes it by each day."

quotes.text: The thought may be rejected, but there will be something to reject. The thought may be relatively meaningless to any given probable self and it may be very fleeting. The probable selves in one way or another will react, forming other action events. Constant creativity applies to every system, then, and what you create in one system has its effects in other systems also.
quotes.translation: 思想可能被拒绝，但总存在可拒绝之物。思想对任何特定可能自我可能相对无意义，也可能转瞬即逝。可能自我会以某种方式反应，形成其他行动事件。持续的创造力适用于每个系统，因此你在一个系统中所创造之物也会在其他系统产生效应。

quotes.text: One aside here: while I am myself as you know me, I am also the other personality that sometimes speaks. This does not mean that what I am as you know me, ends, or is finished, in what he is. (Humorously.) I am overjoyed to be the self that you know (. smile•), and I do indeed take pleasure in that existence, and in meeting its responsibilities. (Pause.) The other personality (smile), which is also myself, has a warm spot in its heart for me (stronger, forceful voice), though again, he would not put it in those terms. He enjoys his own existence.
quotes.translation: 插叙：虽然我是你所知的那个我，但同时也是偶尔发言的另一个人格。这并不意味着你所知的我终结于他之中。（幽默地）我欣喜于成为你所知的自我（微笑），确实享受这份存在及其责任。（停顿）另一个人格（微笑）——同样是我——心中对我怀有温情（更坚定有力的声音），尽管他不会用这种措辞。他享受自己的存在。

quotes.text: Those who understand thoroughly that reality is self-created will have least difficulty. Those who have learned to understand and operate in the mechanics of the dream state will have great advantage. A belief in demons is highly disadvantageous after death, as it is during physical existence. A systematized theology of opposites is also detrimental. If you believe, for example, that all good must be balanced by evil, then you bind yourself into a system of reality that is highly limiting, and that contains within it the seeds of great torment.
quotes.translation: 那些彻底明白实相是自我创造的人将遇到最少的困难。那些学会理解并运作梦境机制的人将拥有巨大优势。对恶魔的信仰在死后极为不利，正如在肉身存在时一样。系统化的二元对立神学同样有害。例如，若你相信所有善必须由恶来平衡，那么你便将自己束缚在一个高度受限的实相系统中，其中蕴含着巨大痛苦的种子。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The health and psychic condition of an individual is not primarily determined by the ego, however. It is only when the ego is allowed too much power that the individual is deprived of much of the inner vitality of the whole self. For the ego is acquainted with only its role. It can find refreshment only within the limitations of the reality which it was formed to meet; and when it looks about with the best of intentions and sees disasters and terrors, it does not know that these others also play their roles, and that the roles are temporary.
quotes.translation: 然而，个体的健康与心灵状态并非主要由自我决定。只有当自我被赋予过多力量时，个体才会被剥夺整个自性的内在活力。因为自我只熟悉自己的角色，它只能在为应对特定实相而设的局限中找到更新。当它怀着最佳意图环顾四周，看到灾难与恐惧时，它不知道这些角色也各有其位，且这些角色都是暂时的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It is not understood that before life an individual decides to live. A self is not simply the accidental personification of the body's biological mechanism. Each person born desires to be born. He dies when that desire no longer operates. No epidemic or illness or natural disaster -- or stray bullet from a murderer's gun -- will kill a person who does not want to die.
quotes.translation: 人们并不明白，在生命之前，个体已决定要活。自性并非身体生物机制的偶然人格化。每个出生的人都渴望出生。当这个渴望不再运作时，他便死去。没有任何流行病、疾病、自然灾害——或凶手枪中流弹——能杀死一个不想死的人。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 801, APRIL 18, 1977
9:31 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Your conception of time is dependent upon your perception of action, and that portion of action which you can perceive and appreciate. There is no doubt here that the intuitions can at times perceive action far more completely than can the intellect. And time, therefore, physical clock time, is much more alien to the intuitive self than it is to the intellectual self.
quotes.translation: 你对时间的观念取决于你对行动的感知，以及你能感知和欣赏的那部分行动。毫无疑问，直觉有时能比理智更完整地感知行动。因此，时间——物理时钟时间——对直觉性自性而言，比对理智性自性更为陌生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: We are also helped here by several characteristics of the personality, in that it is forever changing, and its flexibility will be of benefit. We merely want to change the direction in which it moves, or rather the direction in which some of its energy moves. It must be seen by the personality that the impeding action is a hardship on the part of the whole structure, and that this particular portion of the self is not basic to the original personality structure, but only adopted.
quotes.translation: 人格的若干特性在此也对我们有帮助，因为它永远在变化，其灵活性将带来益处。我们只想改变它移动的方向，或更确切地说，改变它部分能量移动的方向。人格必须看到，阻碍性的行动对整个结构造成了困难，而自性的这一特定部分并非原始人格结构的基础，只是后来才被采纳的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: If you "read yourselves" sideways in such a manner, you would discover portions of your own consciousness stretching out across the entire fabric of the earth as you understand it -- becoming a part of the earth's material, even as those materials become part of the self that you recognize. Your consciousness would be far less hemmed in. Time would expand adjacently. You think of yourselves physically as "top dogs," however, separate from the other species and kinds of life, so that in effect you limit your own experience of your psyche.
quotes.translation: 若你们以这种方式"横向阅读自己"，便会发现自己的意识片段延伸至你所理解的整个地球织锦——成为地球物质的一部分，正如那些物质成为你所认知的自性的一部分。你的意识将远不受束缚。时间会以相邻方式扩展。然而，你们在肉体上将自己视为"顶级掠食者"，与其他物种和生命形态分离，这实际上限制了你对自身心灵的体验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: A very delicate network of imbalances is here maintained. The ego must not be too rigid, or too much a disciplinarian, or it ceases to speak for the whole personality, and becomes a warden, imprisoning the main expressive urges of the deeper self. It must not on the other hand be composed of too disorganized a system of actions, for in this case it is not capable of maintaining a consistent sense of identity or purpose, and is not strong enough to act in a magnetic manner, that will attract or hold the basic energies of the personality.
quotes.translation: 此处维持着一套极为精微的不平衡网络。自我既不能过于僵化，也不能成为严苛的纪律执行者，否则它将不再代表整个人格发声，反而沦为典狱长，囚禁深层自性中主要的表达冲动。另一方面，自我也不能由过于混乱的行动系统构成，否则它将无法维持连贯的自我认同感或目标感，也缺乏足够的力量以磁吸方式运作，从而吸引或维系人格的基本能量。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: For a portion of the self that is relatively so powerless, this is quite presumptive. It is obviously no coincidence that almost the entire survival of the organism is held under the responsibility of the subconscious. To be somewhat prosaic here, it is nevertheless true that the whole efficiency of the personality can be improved if the inclinations of the subconscious are taken into consideration, in the manner of clothes and accessories and living surroundings in general. The personality is simply not pulling against itself so much.
quotes.translation: 对于自性中相对如此无力的部分而言，这实在颇为僭越。有机体几乎全部的生存机能都由潜意识掌管，这显然绝非巧合。说得平淡些，但事实如此：若在衣着配饰及整体生活环境方面顾及潜意识的倾向，人格的整体效能便能得到提升。人格便不会如此严重地自我对抗。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The ego itself attempts as its function to be the director and center of other functions. The ego, while considering itself apart from action, is obviously not apart from it. As dreams allow the inner self great freedom, and as in dreams great perspectives of time are available, and great freedom in space, though no space as you know it is involved, so it is possible for the ego itself to achieve the experience of freedom from time and space, if it would only allow itself for a short while to relax the intensity of its objective focus.
quotes.translation: 自我本身试图以其他功能的指挥者与中心自居。自我虽自认与行动分离，实则显然无法脱离行动。正如梦境赋予内在自性极大自由，使时间与空间的广阔视角得以呈现——尽管其中并不涉及你所认知的空间——同样地，自我若能暂时放松其客观聚焦的强度，也能获得超越时间与空间的自由体验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: At the bottom so to speak, will be found a layer of the subconscious dealing with racial heritage, concerning the dim first materialization of man; and beneath this a heritage dealing with a comprehension of reality as concerned in physical matter before the physical evolution of man, following backward to that first inner self within its mental enclosure, that was adept enough to translate and transform its psychic energy to form the first, most minute physical materialization, from which all other physical constructions then flowed.
quotes.translation: 可以说，在最底层会发现一层涉及种族传承的潜意识，关乎人类最初模糊的物质化形态；其下还有一层传承，涉及人类物理进化之前对物质实相的理解，追溯至那个居于心灵围场中的最初内在自性——它足够精妙地将心灵能量转化变形，形成最初最细微的物质化形态，所有其他物质构造皆由此衍生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Fake humility can cause you to tear down the value of others, because if you accept no worth in yourself you cannot see it in anyone else either. True self-pride allows you to perceive the integrity of your fellow human beings and permits you to help them use their strengths. Many people make a great show out of helping others, for example, encouraging them to lean upon them. They believe this to be a quite holy, virtuous enterprise. Instead they are keeping other people from recognizing and using their own strengths and abilities.
quotes.translation: 虚假的谦卑会导致你贬低他人的价值，因为若你无法接纳自身价值，便也无法在他人身上看见它。真正的自重让你能感知同胞的完整性，并允许你帮助他们运用自身力量。许多人以助人为名大张旗鼓，例如鼓励他人依赖自己。他们以为这是极为神圣高尚的事业，实则是在阻碍他人认识并运用自身的力量与能力。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Others of a more placid nature will take their problems one at a time. Rest periods may also be taken, and they are highly therapeutic. For example, an excellent, satisfying life with a minimum of problems may be chosen either as a prelude to a life of concentrated challenge or as a self-adopted reward for a previous difficult life. Those that thoroughly enjoy the physical medium, without being obsessed by it, however, do very well indeed. The "laws" of reincarnation are adapted by the individual personalities to suit themselves.
quotes.translation: 性情更为平和者会逐一处理问题。休息时段亦可穿插其中，且极具疗愈效果。例如，可以选择一种问题极少、令人满足的卓越生活，作为集中挑战人生的前奏，或作为对先前艰难人生的自我奖赏。那些充分享受物质媒介却不受其困扰之人，确实过得相当好。转世的"法则"由个体人格自行调整以适应自身。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What you must understand is that the psychedelic experience represents but a glimmering of the constant state of the inner self: not constant in terms of something static, however. With drugs, there are some dangers. There are dimensions in which you are completely incapable, and if through some molecular disturbance you fell into one of these it is possible that you could not find your way back. You do not understand me here, and it is difficult to explain. Your vocabulary is not equipped to deal with the concept. I will try.
quotes.translation: 你必须明白，迷幻体验只是内在自我恒常状态的一丝微光——但并非指静止不变的恒常。使用药物存在某些危险。有些维度你完全无法应对，若因某种分子紊乱坠入其中，你或许再也找不到归途。此刻你无法理解我的话，这很难解释。你们的词汇体系尚不足以承载这个概念。我会尽力说明。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You do not know the self as it is within physical existence, and until you do you cannot hope to know what survives physical death, or what part of you is awake while the ego sleeps. When I refer to the ego I do so for simplicity's sake, since the term has meaning to you. There are obviously portions of the self that never operate directly within physical reality.
quotes.translation: 你连物质存在中的自我都尚未认清，在此之前，休想知晓肉体死亡后什么得以存续，或当自我沉睡时你哪部分依然清醒。我提及"自我"只为简便起见，毕竟这个术语对你有意义。显然，自我的某些部分从未直接在物质实相中运作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: This, when it occurs, and this particular formation into a self may or may not occur, but when it occurs it is a result of our second previously mentioned dilemma. The self as you know it is in actuality a self plus an ego.
quotes.translation: 这种情形发生时——这种特定的自我构型可能发生也可能不发生——一旦发生，便是我们先前所述第二个困境的结果。你所认知的自我，实则是"自我"加"自我"的复合体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Its own stability and awareness can be vastly deepened and strengthened. In times of seemingly calamitous encounters with nature, individuals may find themselves amazed at their capacity to relate with other people, but in the artificially induced psychic disaster area of massive LSD therapy, the situation is reversed. Consciousness finds itself in a crisis situation; not [because of one coming] from the exterior world, but because it is forced to fight on a battleground for which it was never designed and cannot understand, where basically counted-upon allies of association, memory and organization, and all the powers of the inner self, are suddenly turned into enemies.
quotes.translation: 其自身的稳定性与觉知力可被极大深化强化。当遭遇看似灾难性的自然境遇时，个体常惊异于自己与他人联结的能力；但在大规模LSD治疗所人为诱发的精神灾难区中，情况截然相反。意识发现自己陷入危机：并非因外部世界的冲击，而是被迫在它从未被设计应对、也无法理解的战场作战——那些本应信赖的盟友：联想、记忆、组织能力，以及内在自我的全部力量，突然都化作了敌人。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The experience that you wrote of was significant on several levels, and of course was meant to reassure you ahead of time because of the events you knew would occur. The experience was to inform you emotionally and spiritually of the great meaning of each individual, portray the lovely brilliance that is within each human being, and let you know that the integrity of the self and the soul exists beyond the possibility of annihilation, as you yourself will continue to exist regardless of which path you choose to take -- dying within two years, or living physically on for many more. In other words, you will continue to exist and to be fulfilled within that love you sensed.
quotes.translation: 你记述的那次体验在多个层面意义重大，自然也是为预先安抚你——因你已知晓即将发生之事。那次体验旨在从情感与灵性层面让你领悟每个个体的伟大意义，描绘每个人内在闪耀的璀璨光辉，并让你明白自我与灵魂的完整性超越了一切毁灭的可能——正如无论你选择哪条道路（两年内离世，或继续肉身存活多年），你自身都将持续存在。换言之，你将在所感知的那份爱中持续存在并臻于圆满。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In ways almost impossible to describe, the ground rules were not as yet firmly established. Gravity itself did not carry its all-pervasive sway, so that the air was more buoyant. Man was aware of its support in a luxurious, intimate fashion. He was aware of himself in a different way, so that, for example, his identification with the self did not stop where his skin stopped: He could follow it outward into the space about his form, and feel it merge with the atmosphere with a primal sense-experience that you have forgotten.
quotes.translation: 以几乎无法描述的方式，当时的基本规则尚未稳固确立。重力本身尚未施展其无所不在的支配力，空气更为轻盈。人类以奢侈而亲密的方式感知着空气的支撑。他以截然不同的方式觉察自身——例如，他对自我的认同并不止于皮肤边界：他能将这种认同延伸至形体周围的空间，感受其与大气融合，体验你们早已遗忘的原初感官经验。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You are biologically connected, chemically connected with the earth that you know; but since it is also formed naturally and spontaneously from your own projected psychic energy, since you and the seasons even have a psychic interaction, then the self must be understood in a far greater context. Such a context would allow you to share in life experiences of many other forms, to follow patterns of energy and emotion of which you barely conceive, and to sense a world-consciousness in which you have your own independent part.
quotes.translation: 你与所认知的大地有着生物性与化学性的联结；但由于大地也是由你自身投射的心灵能量自然自发形成的，由于你与季节甚至存在着心灵互动，因此必须在更广阔的脉络中理解自己。这样的脉络将允许你分享其他生命形态的经验，追随你几乎无法想象的能量与情感模式，并感知一个你作为独立个体参与其中的世界意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now I understand that to one closely and daily involved with the woman, it is difficult to see purpose or reason in the condition. Since the egotistical knowledge of the woman does not include this data, there are of course difficulties here also. But the inner self of the woman knows that in facing such a condition and finding nevertheless some moments of joy, even some compassion for others, that the personality has satisfied many of those feelings of inadequacy from the past existence. (One minute pause, eyes closed.)
quotes.translation: 我明白，对于每日与这位女士密切相处的人来说，很难看出她处境中的目的或理由。由于这位女士的自我认知不包含这些数据，这里当然也存在困难。但这位女士的内在自我知道，在直面这样的处境并仍能寻得片刻欢愉、甚至对他人怀有慈悲时，人格已满足了前世许多关于不足的感受。（停顿一分钟，闭眼。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: This arbitrary limitation set upon the individual self is put upon it by its reliance upon the outer senses, as a method of perceiving reality. The outer senses are excellent tools of perception for limited circumstances. However, man has relied upon them so long, and with such cringing dependence, that now they threaten to hamper his own growth and development.
quotes.translation: 这种强加于个体自我的武断限制，源于其对外在感官的依赖——这是感知实相的方法。外在感官是有限情境中极佳的感知工具。然而，人类依赖它们太久，且带着如此畏缩的依附，以至于如今它们反而可能阻碍自身的成长与发展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Through the years a history of personal projections will serve to prove to you the nonphysical aspect of the self. You may even meet me （smile, eyes open wide） in some future projection. My meetings with you here involve to some extent projections on my part and on Ruburt's part. As yet he is not consciously aware of these projections. When I am here, he is often somewhere else. Part of him remains to keep check on me, and unfortunately this is the part that could be, but is not as yet, aware of his other surroundings.
quotes.translation: 多年来的个人投射经历将向你证明自我的非物质面向。你甚至可能在未来的投射中遇见我（微笑，睁大眼睛）。我在此与你们的会面，某种程度上涉及我自身与鲁柏的投射。目前他尚未有意识地觉察这些投射。当我在此处时，他常身处别处。他的一部分仍留在此处监督我，不幸的是，这部分本可觉察其他环境却尚未做到。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The dream experience is felt directly by the inner self. Dreams have an electric actuality, as I have told you. In this electrical actuality they then exist independently of the dreamer, although he still applies the dream to himself. In the same manner do thoughts and emotions have the electric reality of which I have spoken. Therefore, within the electric system, dreams, thoughts and emotions exist as actualities, and in what you may call a tangible form, though not in the form of matter as you are familiar with it.
quotes.translation: 梦境体验由内在自我直接感受。如我曾告知，梦境具有电性实相。在此电性实相中，它们独立于做梦者而存在，尽管做梦者仍将梦境应用于自身。同样地，思想与情感也具备我提及的电性实相。因此，在电性系统内，梦境、思想与情感作为实相存在，且以你们可称为"可触形式"的状态呈现，尽管并非你们熟悉的物质形态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The following basic subjects are necessary for an understanding of what we are trying to say, particularly for any practical application: the construction of physical matter; the psychological and electrical gestalt that results in the formation of a self; the nature of the dream universe; the electrical system, as it is related to both the physical universe and the inner psychic gestalt; the nature of action; mental enclosures; mental genes; and again, all of these subjects in relationship to their reality as action.
quotes.translation: 要理解我们试图传达的内容，特别是进行实际应用，以下基础主题不可或缺：物质实相的建构；形成自我的心理与电性完形；梦境宇宙的本质；与物质宇宙及内在心灵完形相关的电性系统；行动的本质；心灵围场；心灵基因；以及所有这些主题与其作为行动之实相的关联。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The experience of any given unit, constantly changing, affects all other units ... Give us time ... It is difficult to explain because your concepts of selfhood are so limited ... These units contain within themselves, in your terms, all "latent" identities, but not in a predetermined fashion. Selves may be quite independent within the framework of their own reality, while still being a part of a larger reality in which their independence works not only for their own benefit, but for the sake of a greater structure.
quotes.translation: 任何特定单元的经验都在不断变化，并影响所有其他单元……请给我们时间……因为你们对自我性的概念如此有限，所以很难解释……在你们的术语中，这些单元自身包含着所有"潜在"的身份，但并非以预定方式。自我在其自身实相框架内可能相当独立，同时仍是一个更大实相的一部分，在这个实相中，它们的独立性不仅为自身利益服务，也为一个更宏大结构的目的而运作。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 683
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: I may sound irascible, and if I do then indeed certainly I shall try to blame it on Ruburt, and say it is a distortion, though it is no distortion. I find it, however, a rather painful duty, self imposed, to delve into personal backgrounds, and consider myself rather dignified to adopt the pose of a Peeping Tom. But there are too few who can, either, look within themselves with candor, and if this material does anything, it must be oriented toward knowledge; and knowledge must be applied in your case in human terms.
quotes.translation: 我听起来可能有些暴躁，如果真是这样，我当然会归咎于鲁柏，说这是一种扭曲，尽管这并非扭曲。然而我发现，探究个人背景是一项相当痛苦的自愿职责，并认为自己采取偷窥者的姿态颇为有失尊严。但能够坦诚审视内在的人实在太少，而如果这份资料有任何作用，它必须指向知识；而知识在你们的情况中必须以人类术语来应用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: They will be formed to impress world conditions at any given time, and therefore couched in symbols and events that will most impress the populace. This is craftily done, for the inner self knows exactly what will impress the ego, and what kinds of personalities will be best able to personify the message at any given time. When such a personality appears in history then, he is intuitively recognized, for the way has long been laid, and in many cases the prophecies announcing such an arrival have already been given.
quotes.translation: 它们将被塑造成在任何特定时期都能给世界状况留下深刻印象的形式，因此会以最能打动大众的象征和事件来表达。这是巧妙安排的，因为内在自我确切知道什么会打动自我，以及什么类型的人格最能体现特定时期的信息。当这样的历史人物出现时，人们会凭直觉认出他，因为道路早已铺就，而且在许多情况下，宣告其到来的预言早已给出。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your probability you did allow the inner self some freedom. Therefore, the so-called egotistical consciousness was not given complete sway. It remained flexible enough so that even hidden in its god concepts4 there were symbols of greater reality. Your system deals with physical manipulability, again, and the translation of creativity into physical form. An exterior separation had to occur for a while, in which consciousness forgot, egotistically speaking, that it was a part of nature, and pretended to be apart.
quotes.translation: 在你们的概率中，你们确实允许内在自我拥有一定自由。因此，所谓的自我意识并未被完全掌控。它保持足够灵活，以至于即使隐藏在其神祇概念中，也存在着更伟大实相的象征。你们的系统再次涉及物理可操作性，以及将创造力转化为物理形式。必须暂时发生一种外在分离，在这种分离中，从自我角度而言，意识忘记了它是自然的一部分，并假装与之分离。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: This is simply an analogy because you are steeped in that other reality constantly; but its illuminations and nature are transmitted to the self that you know through the formation of dreams, and in your terms, "it takes time to dream." This larger experience, from which your dreams are finally formed, involves you in a kind of journey. Period. Using another analogy, it is as if you joyfully leave the normal paraphernalia of usual life behind, and ride aboard your own greater psyche into vaster seas of experience.
quotes.translation: 这只是一个类比，因为你们始终沉浸在那另一个实相中；但其启迪与本质通过梦境的形成传递给你们所知的自我，用你们的话说，"做梦需要时间。"这种最终形成你们梦境的更宏大体验，使你们参与某种旅程。句号。用另一个类比来说，仿佛你们愉快地将日常生活的常规装备抛在身后，乘着自身更伟大的心灵驶向更广阔的经验之海。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: （When I was a few car lengths from the light, the dream suddenly popped into my mind. Just as in the dream, I found myself stopping for a red light. The effect was so startling that I at once began to wonder whether it was possible to act out dream suggestions in waking life. My self questioning was particularly intriguing because I could have chosen from a half-dozen routes to work; yet I had picked a course that enabled me to act out the dream of the night before, while not being consciously aware of the dream.）
quotes.translation: （当我离红绿灯还有几辆车距离时，那个梦突然浮现在脑海中。就像在梦中一样，我发现自己为红灯停下了车。效果如此惊人，以至于我立刻开始怀疑是否可能在清醒生活中演绎梦中的暗示。我的自我质疑特别引人入胜，因为我本可以从六条路线中选择去上班；然而我却选择了一条让我能够演绎前一晚梦境的路线，同时并未有意识地意识到那个梦。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You each have your own earth god. The term may not be the best, but it is meant to express that portion of you that is as yet unexpressed in your terms -- the idealized earth version of yourself, which you are becoming. The idealized earth version is not meant to mean a perfect self in flesh at all; instead, it represents a psychic reality in which your own abilities fulfill themselves in relationship with your earthly environment to the fullest extent possible, within the time and place you have already chosen.
quotes.translation: 你们每个人都有自己的土地神。这个术语或许不够贴切，但它旨在表达你们尚未在自身术语中展现的那部分自我——你们正在成为的理想化尘世版本。理想化尘世版本绝非指肉体层面的完美自我；相反，它代表一种心灵实相，在其中，你们自身的能力能在已选择的时间与地点内，与尘世环境达成最大程度的圆满实现。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 691
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: In prehistoric times mankind evolved the ego and self-consciousness to help him deal with the camouflage patterns that he had created. This is no contradiction, and will be explained later. He did the job so well that even when he had things under control he was not satisfied. He developed at a lopsided level. He used himself as a tool to dissect himself. The inside senses led him to a reality he could not manipulate as easily as he could a camouflage world, and he feared what he thought of as a loss of mastery.
quotes.translation: 史前时期，人类演化出自我意识与自我觉知，以协助处理自己创造的伪装模式。这并非矛盾，日后会详细解释。人类做得太过成功，即便已掌控局面仍不满足。他们以失衡的层次发展，将自己当作解剖自身的工具。内在感官引领他们触及一个无法像操控伪装世界般轻易掌控的实相，他们恐惧那种所谓"失去掌控"的状态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I can then, therefore, enter the limits of your psychic comprehension. This is a very simplified version, but I must, so to speak, therefore journey through the various areas of Ruburt's whole self, or inner self, until I reach a point where entry into your consciousness is possible. This happens to be the third undifferentiated layer of Ruburt's subconscious.
quotes.translation: 因此，我能进入你们心灵理解的界限。这是个极度简化的版本，但我必须——容我这么说——穿越鲁柏整体自我或内在自我的各个领域，直到抵达能进入你们意识的节点。这恰好是鲁柏潜意识第三层未分化的区域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The beginnings of human consciousness, on the other hand, began as soon as multicellular groupings began to form in field patterns of a certain complexity. While there was no specific point of entry as far as human consciousness was concerned, there was a point before which human consciousness as such did not exist. Self-consciousness did exist. The consciousness of being human in your terms was fully developed in the caveman, but--and I cannot emphasize this enough--the human conception was alive in the fish.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，人类意识的起源始于多细胞群在特定复杂度的场域模式中形成之时。就人类意识而言，虽无特定进入点，但存在一个人类意识本身尚未存在的临界点。自我觉知确实存在。以你们术语而言的"人类意识"在穴居人时期已完全发展，但——我必须强调——人类概念早在鱼类时期就已活跃。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
