It exists within all camouflage, since the camouflage itself is merely the form that is adopted. The inhabitants of any dimension, or whatever makeup, are still whole-self constructions.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，伪装元素由生命力构成，而生命力本身形成所有维度与所有实相。它存在于一切伪装之中，因为伪装本身只是被采用的形式。任何维度或构成的居民，仍是全我结构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Consciousness is, among other things, a spontaneous exercise in creativity. You are learning now, in a three-dimensional context, the ways in which your emotional and psychic existence can create varieties of physical form. You manipulate within the psychic environment, and these manipulations are then automatically impressed upon the physical mold. Now our environment is in itself creative in a different manner than yours. Your environment is creative in that trees bear fruit, that there is a self-sustaining principle, that the earth feeds its own, for example. The naturally creative aspects are the materializations of the deepest psychic, spiritual, and physical inclinations of the species, set up in your terms eons ago, and a part of the racial bank of psychic knowledge.
quotes.translation: 意识本质上是创造力的自发练习。你正在三维背景下学习，你的情感与心灵存在如何创造各种物质形态。你在心灵环境中运作，这些运作随后自动印刻在物质模具上。我们的环境本身以不同于你们的方式具有创造力。例如，你们的环境具有创造力，因为树木结出果实，存在自我维持的原则，大地滋养自身。这些自然创造性的面向，是物种最深层的心灵、精神与物质倾向的物质化，按你们的说法，在亿万年前就已建立，并成为种族心灵知识库的一部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The man called Christ was not crucified. In the overall drama however it made little difference what was fact, in your terms, and what was not -- for the greater reality transcends facts and creates them. You have free will. You could interpret the drama as you wished. It was given to you. Its great creative power still exists and you use it in your own way, even changing your own symbolism as your beliefs change. But the main idea is the affirmation that the physical being, the self that you know, is not annihilated with death. This comes through even in the distortions. The whole concept of God the Father, as given by Christ, was indeed a "new testament." The male image of God was used because of the sex orientation of the times, but beyond this the Christ personality said, "... the kingdom of God is within (among) you" (Luke 17:21).
quotes.translation: 被称为基督的人并未被钉十字架。然而在整体戏剧中，按你们的说法，事实与否并无差别——因为更大的实相超越事实并创造事实。你们拥有自由意志。你们可以按自己的意愿诠释这出戏剧。它被赐予你们。其伟大的创造力依然存在，你们以自己的方式运用它，甚至随着信念改变而改变自身的象征体系。但核心思想是肯定：物质存在，即你所知的自我，不会随死亡而湮灭。这一点即使在扭曲中也能显现。基督所传达的天父概念，确实是一部"新约"。使用神的男性形象是由于时代的性别取向，但基督人格超越于此，说道："神的国就在你们心里（或：在你们中间）"（路加福音17:21）。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (11:02.) Give us a moment ... I told you (in the last session) that in one probability Ruburt was a nun, expressing mysticism in a highly disciplined context, where it must be watched so that it does not get out of hand. Because there is an unconscious flow of information and experience here, you have one of the reasons for Ruburt's caution in some psychic matters, and his fear of leading people astray. There were three offshoots: one, the nun, with mysticism conventionally expressed, but under guarded circumstances; one, the writer who veiled mystical experience through art; and one, the Ruburt you know, who experienced mystical experience directly, teaches others to do the same, and forms through writing a wedding of the two aspects. You have known two of those selves, then, and you were present at Ruburt's birth with Idea Construction.
quotes.translation: （11:02）稍等片刻……我告诉过你（在上次课程中），在一种概率中，鲁柏曾是修女，在高度纪律化的背景下表达神秘主义，必须被严密监视以防失控。由于此处存在无意识的信息与经验流动，这解释了鲁柏在某些灵性事务上的谨慎，以及他害怕误导他人的原因。有三个分支：其一，修女，以传统方式表达神秘主义但处于受控环境；其二，作家，通过艺术掩盖神秘体验；其三，你所认识的鲁柏，直接体验神秘体验，教导他人同样去做，并通过写作将两者结合。那么，你已认识其中两个自我，并在鲁柏诞生时与"意念建构"一同在场。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 680
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: These basic units move toward organizations then of a selective nature. Having an unpredictable field to draw from, they select activity according to those significances. Period. Various kinds of significances are the result of the units' individual natures. The body that you have is a probable body. It is the result of one line of "development" that could be taken by your particular earth personality in flesh. All of the other possible lines of development also occur, however. They occur at once, but each one simultaneously affects every other. There is actually far greater interaction here than you realize, because you are not used to looking for it. The harder you work to maintain the official accepted idea of the self in conventional terms, the more of course you block out any kind of unpredictability.
quotes.translation: 这些基本单元随后朝向选择性本质的组织移动。由于拥有不可预测的场域可供汲取，它们根据那些意义选择活动。句号。各种意义是单元个体本性的结果。你所拥有的身体是一个概率身体。它是你特定地球人格在肉身中可能采取的一条"发展"线的结果。然而，所有其他可能的发展线也同时发生。它们同时发生，但每一条都同时影响其他所有线。此处实际存在的交互远比你意识到的更为强烈，因为你不习惯寻找它。你越努力以传统方式维护官方接受的自我概念，自然就越会屏蔽任何不可预测性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 682
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The CU's, forming the structure later in its entirety, form all the atoms, molecules, cells, and organs that make up your world. Land changes and alterations of species are conditions brought about in line with overall patterns that involve all species, or land and water masses, at any given "time." There is a great organization of consciousness involved on such occasions -- sometimes creative cataclysms, in which, again from its own precognitive information, nature brings about those situations best suited to its needs. Such biological precognition is firmly based in the chromosomes and genes, and reflected in the cells. As mentioned earlier (in the 684tb session), the present corporal structure of any physical body of any kind is maintained only because of the cells' innate precognitive abilities. To the self the future, of course, is not experienced as future. It is simply one of the emerging conditions of an experienced Now (you had better capitalize that). The cells' practically felt "Now" includes, then, what you would think of as past and future, as simple conditions of Nowness. They maintain the body's structure in your poised time only by manipulating themselves in a rich medium of probabilities. There is a constant give-and-take of communication between the cell as you know it in present time, and the cell as it "was" in the past, or "will be."
quotes.translation: 形成完整结构的CU，构成了构成你们世界的所有原子、分子、细胞和器官。陆地变迁与物种变化，是在任何特定"时间"内，依据涉及所有物种或陆水体的整体模式而带来的状况。这类事件中涉及庞大的意识组织——有时是创造性的剧变，在其中，自然再次根据自身的预知信息，带来最适合其需求的情境。这种生物预知深深植根于染色体和基因中，并反映在细胞里。如先前（第684节）所述，任何种类物理身体的当前肉体结构之所以能维持，完全是因为细胞与生俱来的预知能力。对自体而言，未来当然不被体验为未来。它只是体验中的"当下"（你最好大写）的涌现状态之一。因此，细胞实际感受到的"当下"，包含了你们视为过去和未来的东西，作为当下性的简单状态。它们仅通过在概率的丰沛媒介中操纵自身，来维持你们稳定时间中的身体结构。在你们认知的当下时间中的细胞，与它"曾经是"或"将会是"的细胞之间，存在着持续不断的交流互动。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 690
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Now in the case of an animal who hibernates, the body is in the same state. But in the greater hibernation of your own experience, the body as a whole becomes inoperable. The cells within you obviously die constantly. The body that you have now is not the one that you had 10 years ago; its physical composition has died completely many times since your birth, but, again, your consciousness bridges those gaps (with gestures). They could be accepted instead, in which case it would seem to you that you were, say, a reincarnated self at age 7 (intently), or 14 or 21. The particular sequence of your own awareness follows through, however. In basic terms the body dies often, and as surely as you think it dies but once in the death you recognize. On numerous occasions it physically breaks apart, but your consciousness rides beyond those "deaths." You do not perceive them. The stuff of your body literally falls into the earth many times, as you think it does only at the "end of your life."
quotes.translation: 现在，以冬眠动物为例，其身体处于相同状态。但在你们经验中更宏大的冬眠里，整个身体变得无法运作。你们体内的细胞显然在不断死亡。你们现在的身体已非十年前的躯体；自出生以来，其物理构成已完全死亡多次，但你们的意识再次跨越了那些间隙（伴随手势）。它们本可以被接受，那样的话，你们就会觉得自己仿佛是7岁（专注地）、14岁或21岁时的转世自体。然而，你们自身觉知的特定序列会贯穿始终。从根本上说，身体经常死亡，其确定性如同你们认为它在你们所认知的死亡中只死一次。在许多情况下，它会在物理层面分崩离析，但你们的意识超越了那些"死亡"。你们并未感知到它们。你们身体的物质确实多次落入大地，正如你们认为它只在"生命终结"时才会发生的那样。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I have said (as at 9:48) that the body can indeed carry on, performing necessary maintenance activities while the main consciousness is detached from it. To some extent it can even perform simple chores. (Pause.) In sleep, in fact, it is not at all necessary that the main consciousness be alert in the body. Only in certain kinds of civilizations, for that matter, is such a close body-and-main-consciousness relationship necessary. There are other situations, therefore, in which consciousness ordinarily strays much further, returning to the body as a home station and basis of operation, relying upon it for certain kinds of perception only, but not depending upon it for the entire picture of reality. Physical life alone does not necessarily require the kind of identification of self with flesh that is your own.
quotes.translation: 我曾说过（如9:48所述），当主要意识脱离身体时，身体确实能继续运作，执行必要的维护活动。它甚至能在一定程度上完成简单的杂务。（停顿。）事实上，在睡眠中，主要意识完全不必在身体内保持警觉。只有在某些类型的文明中，才需要如此紧密的身体与主要意识关系。因此，还存在其他情境，其中意识通常会游离得更远，将身体作为归航站和运作基地，仅依赖它进行特定类型的感知，但并不依赖它获取实相的全貌。仅就物质生命而言，并不必然需要你们那种将自体与肉身等同的认同。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (10:55: Jane's delivery had become somewhat faster and very absorbed as it progressed. Just as they had before our three-month layoff from book work [after Session 707], her trances for "Unknown" Reality were proving to be more "difficult" to initiate than those for the previous Seth books.9 She often had to wait for just the right moment to get back into dictation following a break, too; so tonight, after we'd shared an apple, she sat rather impatiently anticipating Seth's return. Resume, finally, at 11:25.)

quotes.text: The upstart, for instance, may be displaying all of the bold aspects inhibited by other family members. Through this person the others may vicariously share the excitement or suspense of those experiences that are otherwise blocked. On the other hand the achiever may be completely hiding such impulses, while expressing faithfully the desires of other family members for "excellence" and discipline. Now the same can apply to counterparts, and those in your experience can show to you, in exaggerated form, comma, abilities of your own upon which you have not chosen to concentrate. You can learn much from your counterparts, therefore, and they from you. Those counterparts that you meet will be working, playing, and being more or less within your own culture. This does not mean that you are bits and pieces of some hypothetical whole self.
quotes.translation: 例如，暴发户可能展现出其他家庭成员所抑制的所有大胆面向。通过此人，其他人可以间接分享那些被阻隔经历中的兴奋或悬念。另一方面，成就者可能完全隐藏此类冲动，同时忠实地表达其他家庭成员对"卓越"和自律的渴望。现在，同样的情况也适用于对应体，你们经验中的那些人可以以夸张的形式向你们展示你们自己选择不专注的能力。因此，你们可以从对应体身上学到很多，他们也能从你们身上学到很多。你们遇到的对应体将在你们自己的文化范围内或多或少地工作、玩耍和存在。这并不意味着你们是某个假设的完整自体的碎片。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now there are books programming out-of-body activity; millions of you are told that when you leave your body you will meet this demon or that demon, or this or that angry god. So, instead, we will form a free city to which those travelers can come, and where those who enter can read books about Buddhism if they prefer, or play at being Catholic. There will also be certain beloved traps set about the city, that will be of an enlightening nature ... Now listen: You think there is nothing intrinsically impossible about building a platform in [your] space ... I am suggesting, then, a platform in inner reality. It is as valid -- far more valid -- as an orbiting city in the sky, in physical terms, and it challenges your creative abilities much more. You need a good challenge -- it is fun! Not because you should do it, but because you desire it ... It is a great creative challenge that you can throw down to yourselves from your future selves.
quotes.translation: 现在，有些书籍规划着出体活动；数百万人被告知，当你们离开身体时，会遇到这个恶魔或那个恶魔，或这个或那个愤怒的神祇。因此，我们将建立一个自由之城，让那些旅行者可以前来，让进入者可以阅读关于佛教的书籍（如果他们偏好），或扮演天主教徒。城中还将设置某些受人喜爱的陷阱，这些陷阱将具有启迪性质……现在听着：你们认为在你们的空间中建造一个平台并非本质上不可能……我建议的，是内在实相中的一个平台。它在物理层面与天空中的轨道城市同样有效——远为有效——并且它更能挑战你们的创造能力。你们需要一个好的挑战——这很有趣！不是因为你们应该做，而是因为你们渴望它……这是你们可以向自己未来的自体抛出的伟大创造挑战。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "Several interesting -- and frustrating -- questions are raised by today's episode. As stated, this makes the second time that I've had an experience involving the violent death of a Roman soldier in the earlier part of the first century A.D. (I never did arrive at names for those two militant individuals.) Perhaps both instances are merely my own psychological reflections of present concerns or challenges, although I think that more is involved. Given Seth's concept of simultaneous time, the best connection I've made so far between the two soldiers is that as counterparts of mine they explore questions having to do with authority. As I rebel against authority now -- a characteristic remarked upon by Seth in the 721st session -- so do my Roman selves in their times.
quotes.translation: "今天的经历引发了几个有趣且令人沮丧的问题。如前所述，这是我第二次经历公元一世纪早期一名罗马士兵的暴力死亡。（我始终未能为那两个好斗的个体找到名字。）也许这两个实例都只是我对当前关切或挑战的心理投射，尽管我认为其中涉及更多。鉴于赛斯的同时性时间概念，我目前对这两个士兵的最佳关联是，作为我的对应体，他们探索与权威相关的问题。正如我现在反抗权威——赛斯在第721节中提到的特征——我的罗马自体们也在他们的时代如此行事。"

quotes.text: Dreams deal with associations and with emotional validities that often do not seem to make sense in the usual world. I said before that no one can really give you a definition of the psyche. It must be experienced. Since its activities, wisdom and perception rise largely from another kind of reference, then you must often learn to interpret your encounter with the psyche to your usual self. One of the largest difficulties here is the issue of organization. In regular life, you organize your experience very neatly and push it into accepted patterns or channels, into preconceived ideas and beliefs. You tailor it to fit time sequences. Again: The psyche's organization follows no such learned predisposition. Its products can often appear chaotic simply because they splash over your accepted ideas about what experience is.
quotes.translation: 梦境处理的是联想与情感的真实性，这些在寻常世界里往往看似不合逻辑。我曾说过，没有人能真正为你定义心灵——它必须被体验。由于心灵的活动、智慧与感知大多源自另一种参照体系，你常常需要学会将自身与心灵的相遇，转译给日常自我。此处最大的困难之一在于组织方式。在常规生活中，你极其整齐地组织经验，将其塞入既定的模式或渠道，塞入先入为主的观念与信念。你裁剪经验以符合时间序列。再说一次：心灵的组织方式并不遵循这种习得的预设。它的产物常常显得混乱，仅仅因为它们泼溅在你对经验本质的固有认知之上。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: That world has many languages. Physically you are like one country within your psyche, with a language of your own. People are always searching for master languages, or for one in particular out of which all others emerged. In a way, Latin is a master language. In the same manner people search for gods, or a God, out of which all psyches emerged. Here you are searching for the implied source, the unspoken, invisible "pause," the inner organization that gives language or the self a vehicle of expression. Languages finally become archaic. Some words are entirely forgotten in one language, but spring up in altered form in another. All of the earth's languages, however, are united because of characteristic pauses and hesitations upon which the different sounds ride.
quotes.translation: 那个世界拥有多种语言。在肉体层面，你就像心灵国度中的一个国家，拥有自己的语言。人们总在寻找母语，或寻找一种所有其他语言皆由其衍生而出的特定语言。从某种角度说，拉丁文是一种母语。同样地，人们寻找诸神或一位神祇，所有心灵皆由其衍生而出。在此，你寻找的是那隐含的源头，那未言明、不可见的"停顿"，那赋予语言或自我以表达载体的内在组织。语言终将变得古旧。某些词汇在一种语言中被完全遗忘，却以变形之姿在另一种语言中涌现。然而，地球上所有语言皆因承载不同声音的独特停顿与迟疑而联结。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am speaking particularly of epidemics that are less than deadly, though danger is involved. In your times, hospitals, you must realize, are important parts of the community. They provide a social as well as a medical service. Many people are simply lonely, or overworked. Some are rebelling against commonly held ideas of competition. Flu epidemics become social excuses for much needed rest, therefore, and serve as face-saving devices so that the individuals can hide from themselves their inner difficulties. In a way, such epidemics provide their own kind of fellowship -- giving common meeting grounds for those of disparate circumstances. The [epidemics] serve as accepted states of illness, in which people are given an excuse for the rest or quiet self-examination they desperately need but do not feel entitled to otherwise.
quotes.translation: 我特别要谈的是那些虽具危险性却非致命的流行病。在你们的时代，你必须认识到，医院是社群的重要组成部分。它们既提供医疗服务，也提供社会服务。许多人只是感到孤独，或过度劳累。有些人在反抗普遍持有的竞争观念。因此，流感疫情成为急需休息的社会借口，成为保全面子的手段，使个体得以向自己隐藏内心的困境。从某种角度说，这类疫情提供了它们特有的联结——为处境迥异的人们提供共同的交汇点。它们成为被认可的疾病状态，人们借此获得他们迫切需要的休息或静心自省，而若在平时，他们觉得自己并无资格享有这些。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Once scientists theorized the ether as the medium in which the physical universe existed.1 Framework 2 is the psychological medium in which the consciousness of the world exists. The word "ego" is much bandied about, and in many circles it has a poor reputation. It is, however, as I use it, a term meant to express the ordinarily conscious directive portion of the self. It is your conscious version of what you are -- an excellent description, if I do say so myself (with amusement). It is directed outward into the physical world. It is also aware, however, of some of your "unconscious" activities. It is the you you identify with, so it is as aware of your dreams, for example, as you are, and it is quite conscious of the fact that its existence rests upon knowledge that it does not itself possess.
quotes.translation: 科学家曾理论化以太作为物理宇宙存在的介质。框架二是世界意识存在的心理介质。"自我"一词被频繁使用，在许多圈子里名声不佳。然而，正如我所使用的，这个词意在表达自我通常有意识的指导性部分。它是你对自身是什么的有意识版本——若容我自夸，这是个绝妙的描述（带着戏谑）。它向外指向物质世界。然而，它也意识到你某些"无意识"活动。它是你认同的那个"你"，因此它与你一样意识到你的梦境，并且它很清楚，它的存在依赖于它自身并不拥有的知识。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner ego is fully conscious. It is a portion of you, however, that deals with the formation of events, that glories in a rather rambunctious and creative activity that your specifications of time and place physically preclude. The unconscious, so-called, is -- and I have said this before2 -- quite conscious, but in another realm of activity. There must be a psychological chamber between these two portions of the self, however -- these seemingly undifferentiated areas, in which back-and-forth translations can occur. Dream periods provide that service, of course, so that in dreams the two egos can meet and merge to some extent, comparing notes like strangers who perhaps meet on a train at night, and are amazed to discover, after some conversation, that they are indeed close relatives, each embarked upon the same journey though seemingly they travelled alone.
quotes.translation: 内在自我是完全有意识的。然而，它是你的一部分，处理事件的成形，在一种相当喧闹而富有创造性的活动中欢欣——这种活动被你们对时间与地点的特定规格在物质层面所排除。所谓的无意识——我以前说过——是相当有意识的，只是在另一个活动领域。然而，在这两个自我部分之间必须有一个心理腔室——这些看似未分化的区域，在其中可以进行来回转译。梦境时段当然提供了这种服务，因此在梦中，两个自我可以在某种程度上相遇并融合，交换笔记，就像两个陌生人在夜车上相遇，交谈一番后惊讶地发现他们竟是近亲，各自踏上同一旅程，尽管看似独自旅行。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some people are only aware of -- or largely aware of -- impulses toward anger, because they have inhibited those natural impulses toward love that would otherwise temper what seemed to be aggressive desires. When you begin trusting yourselves, you start by taking it for granted that to some extent at least you have not trusted yourself or your impulses in the past: You have thought that impulses were dangerous, disruptive, or even evil. So as you begin to learn self-trust, you acknowledge your impulses. You try them on for size. You see where they lead you by allowing them some freedom. You do not follow urges through that would hurt others physically, or that seem in direct contradiction to your present beliefs -- but you do acknowledge them. You do try to discover their source. Behind them you will almost always find an inhibited impulse -- or many of them -- that motivated you to move in some ideal direction, to seek a love or understanding so idealized in your mind that it seemed impossible to achieve. You are left with the impulse to strike out.
quotes.translation: 有些人只意识到——或主要意识到——愤怒的冲动，因为他们抑制了那些本可调和看似攻击性欲望的自然之爱冲动。当你开始信任自己时，你首先会理所当然地认为，至少在一定程度上，你过去并未信任自己或自己的冲动：你认为冲动是危险的、破坏性的，甚至邪恶的。因此，当你开始学习自我信任时，你承认自己的冲动。你试穿它们，看看是否合身。你通过给予它们一些自由，观察它们将你引向何方。你不会追随那些会伤害他人身体、或与你当前信念直接矛盾的冲动——但你确实承认它们。你确实试图发现它们的源头。在它们背后，你几乎总会发现一个被抑制的冲动——或许多个——它们曾激励你朝向某个理想方向移动，去寻求一种在你心中如此理想化以至于看似不可能实现的爱或理解。于是你只剩下出击的冲动。

quotes.text: "His poetry acted in some regards as a stimulator. That breakthrough, you might say, with perhaps some exaggeration, was a lifesaver, for without some such expansion Ruburt would have felt unable to continue the particular brand of his existence. It is not possible to say in words what one person or another looks for in life, or what unique features best promote his or her growth and development. Even two plants of the same kind sometimes require completely different treatments. The sessions, then, opened the door to a particular kind of value fulfillment that was natural to Ruburt's being. Now to some extent it was that poor, unhappy sinful self, a psychological structure formed by beliefs and feelings, that was also seeking its own redemption, since even it had outgrown the framework that so defined it.
quotes.translation: 他的诗作在某种程度上起到了激发作用。你可以说，那个突破——或许有些夸张——是救命的，因为如果没有某种这样的扩展，鲁柏会觉得自己无法继续他那特定形式的存在。无法用言语说清一个人在生活中寻求什么，或者什么独特特质最能促进他或她的成长与发展。即使是同一种植物的两株，有时也需要完全不同的照料。于是，那些课打开了一扇门，通向一种鲁柏存在本质中自然的价值完成。现在，在某种程度上，正是那个可怜、不快乐、有罪的自我——一个由信念和情感形成的心理结构——也在寻求自身的救赎，因为连它也已经超越了那个如此定义它的框架。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 11. When Mass Events is published, see Session 835 for February 7, 1979, in Chapter 6.

quotes.text: "Now Ruburt had only one parent available most of the time, and he did not feel secure in that relationship--a situation chosen ahead of time, now. There is great leeway in the nature of such bondings.... With some people, they are so secure that they provide an overall, fairly permanent inner and outer framework. Ruburt's relationship with his mother left much to be desired. The bonding did not secure him that vital sense of safety, and he felt threatened by abandonment. His bonding to the cultural beliefs of religion was very strong to make up for that initial lack. The sinful-self material represents those ideas that were a strong element in his original belief structures. The 'troublesome' material remained relatively inactive until his curiosity and ability led him to actively challenge those ideas while [he was] also in a situation where the natural fear of abandonment might be suggested. At certain points, the assimilation of new information is so qualifiedly different from the original belief structure that in order to assimilate it the personality is left for a time between belief systems.
quotes.translation: 现在，鲁柏大部分时间只有一个家长可用，而且他在那段关系中感到不安全——这是事先选择好的情况，现在来看。这种联结的本质有很大的余地……对某些人来说，它们如此安全，以至于提供了一个整体上相当持久的内在和外在框架。鲁柏与他母亲的关系令人大失所望。那种联结没有给他那种至关重要的安全感，他感到被遗弃的威胁。他与宗教文化信念的联结非常强烈，以弥补那种最初的缺失。有罪自我的材料代表了那些在他原始信念结构中作为强有力元素的观念。这些“麻烦的”材料相对不活跃，直到他的好奇心和能力引导他积极挑战那些观念，同时他也处于一种可能暗示对遗弃的自然恐惧的情境中。在某些点上，新信息的同化与原始信念结构如此显著不同，以至于为了同化它，人格会暂时处于信念系统之间。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 17. See the opening notes for Session 920, in this Chapter 9.

quotes.text: The intellect is extremely important in the manipulation of camouflage patterns once they are created. You have made your world, and your intellect should help you deal with what you have created. It has other vital functions which I shall enumerate at a later date. However, I cannot say this too often: You are more than your conscious mind, much more, and the self which you do not admit happens to be the portion of yourself which not only insures your own survival in the physical universe which it has made, but which is also the connective portion of yourself with inner reality. Which is, when all is said and done, the only basic reality; and which also continually enables you to create these camouflage patterns, and which contains knowledge and intuitions and memories which you need in a most desperate manner if you are ever to understand yourselves, and if the race of mankind is ever to evolve to its fullest.
quotes.translation: 智力在操纵伪装模式方面极为重要，一旦这些模式被创造出来。你们创造了你们的世界，你们的智力应该帮助你们处理你们所创造的东西。它还有其他重要的功能，我将在日后列举。然而，我再怎么强调也不为过：你们比你们的意识心更多，多得多，而你们不承认的那个自己，恰好是你们自身的一部分，它不仅确保你们在它所创造的物理宇宙中的生存，而且还是你们与内在实相相连的部分。归根结底，内在实相是唯一的基本实相；并且它也不断使你们能够创造这些伪装模式，它包含你们最迫切需要了解的知识、直觉和记忆，如果你们要理解自己，如果人类种族要进化到其最充分的状态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The part that translates inner data sifts it down through the subconscious, which is a barrier and also a threshold to the present personality as it operates on the camouflage plane. I have mentioned many times how vitality changes as it approaches and forms various planes. I have said that the topmost part of the subconscious contains personal memories. That beneath these are racial memories and so forth. Things are simply not layered in the way I speak of them. But continuing with the necessary analogy, on the other side of （or beneath to you） the racial memories, you no longer exist within your plane, and look out upon another with the face of this other self-conscious part of you. This part receives inner data, is in contact with the entity, to some greater degree than you are in contact with your dreams, and actually directs all the important functions that you think are either automatically controlled, or unconsciously controlled.
quotes.translation: 翻译内在资料的部分将其筛选下来，通过潜意识，而潜意识对于在伪装层运作的当前人格来说，既是屏障也是门槛。我曾多次提到活力在接近和形成各个层面时如何变化。我曾说过，潜意识的最上层包含个人记忆。在这些之下是种族记忆等等。事物并非像我描述的那样分层。但继续用必要的类比来说，在种族记忆的另一边（或对你们而言之下），你们不再存在于你们的层面内，而是以你们这个其他自我意识部分的面孔向外看向另一个层面。这部分接收内在资料，与存有接触，其程度比你们与自己的梦接触更大，并且实际上指导着所有你们认为要么自动控制、要么无意识控制的重要功能。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: However, as we know the cell is a part of the body, giving nourishment and receiving nourishment from it. Its outer rim, more correctly, connects it to the body. Parts of it literally travel throughout the body. It is yet an individual. It possesses condensed consciousness and comprehension, it partakes of value fulfillments through the gestalt of which it would not otherwise be capable. If you considered the body as a closed system, which it is not, then you could say that the self of the cell had as its limitations only the limits of the whole closed system. But the system is not closed, and through the participation of the cell in the activities of the body, which is an open system, then you could truly say that the cell itself had no limitations.
quotes.translation: 然而，如我们所知，细胞是身体的一部分，从身体获取营养并给予营养。它的外缘，更准确地说，将其连接到身体。它的部分实际上在整个身体中游走。它却是一个个体。它拥有浓缩的意识和理解力，它通过它否则无法做到的完形来参与价值完成。如果你把身体视为一个封闭系统——它并非如此——那么你可以说，细胞的自己其限制仅限于整个封闭系统的限制。但系统不是封闭的，通过细胞参与身体的活动——身体是一个开放系统——那么你可以真正地说，细胞本身没有限制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The pieces to the puzzle are at mankind's fingertips, but he has put together an awkward, ill-fitting miniature model universe of a puzzle with which he is afraid to part. If the physical universe existed on a physical field only, indeed this would be the greatest miracle of all, for it would be impossible. The inner vitality of which we have so often spoken, and whose ways I have described, this inner vitality is the force which itself forms the physical universe, and without which no such manifestation would be possible. Yet this inner self, this inner vitality, is one of the main clues which man refuses to recognize, calling it an unreasonable assumption, but not willing to examine it for those characteristics which show it to be the most reasonable and logical of phenomena.
quotes.translation: 拼图的碎片就在人类指尖，但他却拼凑了一个笨拙、不合身的微型模型宇宙拼图，他害怕与之分离。如果物理宇宙只存在于一个物理场上，那确实会是最伟大的奇迹，因为那是不可能的。我们经常谈到的内在活力，以及我描述过其方式的内在活力，正是这种力量本身形成了物理宇宙，没有它，任何这样的显化都不可能。然而，这个内在自己，这个内在活力，是人类拒绝承认的主要线索之一，称其为不合理的假设，却不愿检查它的那些特征，这些特征表明它是最合理、最合乎逻辑的现象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: These dream activities have a pulsing quality that could be compared with the pulsing of heartbeat, and that are quite as vital to the human system. So much telepathic communication is received at this level that the lines of individuality are not as brilliant. Yet individuality does exist here, but it takes a strong organizing system, with amazing powers of discrimination, to handle such data; and it takes the human personality to stand firm upon its own identity while it is, on this other dreaming level, open to so many communications that are not its own. It is the ego, practically speaking, who attempts to do the distinguishing here; but the inner core of the self, the inner ego of which we have spoken, manages the basic chore. The setting up of the ego represented at once the necessity of boundaries, represented a cutting apart from, a divorcing, and a rigid limiting function. Initially this was necessary while this new sort of creature learned to maintain itself as a separate unit.
quotes.translation: 这些梦境活动具有一种脉动特质，可与心跳的脉动相比，对人类系统同样至关重要。在此层面接收到的灵犀沟通如此之多，以至于个体性的线条不再鲜明。然而个体性确实存在于此处，但它需要一个强大的组织系统，具备惊人的辨识能力，才能处理这类数据；同时需要人格在另一个梦境层面中，对诸多非己的沟通保持开放时，仍能稳固立足于自身身份。实际上，是自我意识在此试图进行区分；但我们所提及的自我内在核心——内在自我——则负责处理基本工作。自我意识的建立同时代表了界限的必要性，代表了一种切割分离、一种割裂，以及一种严格限制的功能。起初，当这种新型生物学习如何维持自身为一个独立单元时，这是必要的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: It constructs ideas instead within another electrical system. Yet because of the nature of the personality, no dream exists in a vacuum, and every dream is recorded by the inner self. I am making an attempt here this evening, since we have three present, to give a very brief, and I am afraid inadequate explanation, that will however serve as a basis so that these three, at least, will be able to have some sort of a standing ground for other discussions.
quotes.translation: 它反而在另一个电性系统内构建想法。然而由于人格的本质，没有梦境存在于真空中，每个梦境都被内在自我记录。今晚我在此尝试，鉴于我们有三位在场，给出一个非常简短且恐怕不够充分的解释，但这将作为基础，至少让这三位能在其他讨论中拥有某种立足点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: At the same time the animal was denied the usual constructive psychic atmosphere in which it usually is allowed to operate. The situation in the bedroom was not important in itself. The cat was nervous, bewildered and somewhat frightened however to begin with. The situation was simply the point at which the cat, in self-defense, threw back the destructive energy which it had received, and I must say in fury and full measure, and in the only way it knew.
quotes.translation: 与此同时，动物被剥夺了通常允许其运作的建设性心灵氛围。卧室中的情境本身并不重要。然而猫起初感到紧张、困惑且有些害怕。这个情境只是猫出于自卫，以它唯一知晓的方式，将所接收的破坏性能量——我必须说是愤怒且全然地——反弹回去的触发点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Animals have varying degrees of self-consciousness, as indeed people do. The consciousness that is within them is as valid and eternal as your own, however. There is nothing to prevent a personality from investing a portion of his own energy into an animal form. This is not transmigration of souls. It does not mean that a man can be reincarnated in an animal. It does mean that personalities can send a portion of their energy into various kinds of form.
quotes.translation: 动物拥有不同程度的自我意识，正如人类一样。然而它们内在的意识与你们的一样真实且永恒。没有什么能阻止人格将其部分能量注入动物形态。这不是灵魂转世。这并不意味着人可以转生为动物。这确实意味着人格可以将部分能量送入各种形态之中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Actually, you consider action which seems to be at least partially self-directive as living matter. Action that seems to be of a static nature, you refer to as inert matter. It should go without saying that all action is indeed self-directed action to some degree, and therefore should be termed as living matter.
quotes.translation: 实际上，你们将至少看似具有部分自我导向性的行动视为生命物质。看似静态性质的行动，你们则称之为惰性物质。不言而喻，所有行动在某种程度上确实是自我导向的行动，因此应被称为生命物质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The subconscious merely is aware of, and operates within, a very valid psychological perspective, with which the so-called conscious self is not familiar. Before physical construction can occur therefore, psychological perception, manipulation and construction of inner data or inner structures must be performed.
quotes.translation: 潜意识仅仅是在一个非常有效的心理视角中觉察并运作，而所谓的意识自我对此并不熟悉。因此，在物质构建发生之前，必须进行内在数据或内在结构的心理感知、操控与构建。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: This knowledge helps you use and direct physical laws, not ignore them. You use them more efficiently, and for the purposes of the inner self. You begin to create physical matter so that it does indeed reflect the inner self, and in so doing, you effect changes that are noticed by others and serve as an example.
quotes.translation: 这份知识帮助你运用并引导物理法则，而非忽视它们。你更高效地运用它们，为内在自我的目的服务。你开始创造物质实相，使其确实反映内在自我，如此你带来的改变会被他人察觉，并成为典范。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: It is this dilemma, precisely between identity's constant attempts to maintain stability, and actions inherent drive for change, that results in the imbalance, the exquisite creative by-product that is consciousness of self. We have a series of creative strains. Identity must seek stability while action must seek change, yet identity could not exist without change, without action, for it is the result of action, and not apart from it but a part of it.
quotes.translation: 正是这个困境——身份认同不断试图维持稳定，与行动固有的改变驱力之间的张力——导致了失衡，以及那精妙的创造性副产品：自我意识。我们有一系列创造性的张力。身份认同必须寻求稳定，而行动必须寻求改变，然而没有改变、没有行动，身份认同便无法存在，因为它是行动的结果，并非独立于行动，而是行动的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Since these selves exist simultaneously, it is then possible for consciousness to enter, or really form, such an image, undergo experiences within the characteristic pattern of reality, and then project to another image. In all cases consciousness forms the image. When consciousness changes, or travels at certain frequencies, it automatically changes its form. It is indeed possible to be involved with more than one form at once as in usual projection.
quotes.translation: 由于这些自我同时存在，意识便有可能进入——或更确切地说，形成——这样一个形象，在实相的特征模式中经历体验，然后投射到另一个形象。在所有情况下，意识都形成形象。当意识改变或以特定频率移动时，它会自动改变形态。确实有可能像通常的投射那样同时涉及多个形态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The inner ego or the inner self-conscious self directs such experiences and uses the inner senses in much the same way that you use the outer senses, except that the inner ego knows all of the mechanics involved in the use of the inner senses, and you know little of the mechanics involved with the outer senses.
quotes.translation: 内在自我或内在自性自我引导这类体验，并使用内在感官，其方式与你使用外在感官大致相同，区别在于内在自我知晓运用内在感官的所有机制，而你对外在感官的机制却知之甚少。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: For as many layers of the self compose the whole self, so many selves form a gestalt of which you know relatively little, and of which I am not yet prepared to tell you.
quotes.translation: 因为组成整体自我的自我层次有多少，就有多少自我形成一个你相对知之甚少的完形，而我尚未准备好告知你这些。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Ruburt has been reading old poetry of his own, and he was appalled to find such beliefs in rather brutal, concentrated form. Until our sessions began, he followed the official line of consciousness, and though he railed against those precepts he could find no other solution. The self, so spectacularly alive, seemed equipped with reason to understand the great import of its own certain extinction. Such a tragedy to project upon the living personality.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏一直在重读自己早年的诗歌，他震惊地发现其中竟以相当残酷而集中的形式存在着那些信念。在我们的课程开始之前，他遵循着意识的官方路线，尽管他抨击那些戒律，却找不到其他出路。那如此生机勃勃的自我，似乎被赋予了理性，去理解自身必然消亡的重大意义。将这样的悲剧投射到活生生的人格上，何其悲哀。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 859, JUNE 6, 1979
9:14 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The alpha level is undifferentiated. The energy there is available to be used as you want to. It is the fountainhead or pool in which reservoirs of energy are held in reserve, pulled between the more interior self and the outer self. Into this area come signs and portents from deeper levels of the personality.
quotes.translation: α层面是未分化的。那里的能量可依你所需而使用。它是能量储备的源头或蓄水池，在内在自我与外在自我之间被牵引。来自人格更深层面的征兆与预兆会进入这个领域。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you let your attention waver, however, you may often fall into a state in which you momentarily perceive glimpses of another alternate present. The whole self, the soul, knows of its reality in all such systems, and you, as a part of it, are working toward the same state of self-awareness and development.
quotes.translation: 然而，当你让注意力游移时，常会坠入一种状态，瞬间瞥见另一个交替的当下。整体自我、灵魂，知晓自身在所有此类系统中的实相，而你作为其一部分，正朝着相同的自我觉知与发展状态迈进。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The body and mind together do present a united, self regulating, healing, self-clearing system. Within it each problem contains its own solution if it is honestly faced. Each symptom, mental or physical, is a clue to the resolution of the conflict behind it, and contains within it the seeds of its own healing.
quotes.translation: 身体与心智确实共同呈现一个统一、自我调节、自愈、自我净化的系统。其中每个问题若被诚实面对，都包含其自身解决方案。每个症状——无论是心理还是生理的——都是其背后冲突的线索，并蕴含自愈的种子。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You know now that so-called inert form has consciousness. To some degree it even possesses self-consciousness, and so there is no point at which self-consciousness entered, so to speak, with the sound of trumpets. Consciousness, to a degree, was inherent in the first materialization physically upon your plane.
quotes.translation: 你现在知道所谓惰性形态具有意识。某种程度上它甚至拥有自我意识，因此自我意识并非在号角声中突然降临。意识在某种程度上，本就内在于你们物质层面首次物质化之中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I am not comparing personality to an orange or an onion, but I want to emphasize that as these things grow from within outward, so does each fragment of the entire self. You observe the outside aspect of objects. Your physical senses permit you to perceive the exterior forms to which you then react, but your physical senses to some extent force you to perceive reality in this manner, and the inside vitality within matter and form is not so apparent.
quotes.translation: 我并非将人格比作橙子或洋葱，但我想强调：正如这些事物由内向外生长，整体自我的每个碎片也是如此。你观察物体的外在面向。物质感官让你感知外在形态并对其反应，但物质感官在某种程度上迫使你以这种方式感知实相，而物质与形态内在的生命力并不明显。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: After you have managed to feel yourself above your body in your astral form, you again tell yourself to see the image of the same past self, you may then imagine its image merging with your own, and its memories a part of your own consciousness. When you have succeeded, then still feeling yourself in the astral form, tell yourself that you will next see the image of the life before last: the image of the person you were, then repeat the other steps.
quotes.translation: 当你成功以星光体形态感受自己悬浮于身体之上时，再次告诉自己观想同一过去自我的影像。随后可想象其影像与你的影像融合，其记忆成为你意识的一部分。成功之后，保持星光体形态的感知，告诉自己接下来将观想前前世的生命影像：你曾是的那个人，然后重复其他步骤。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The subconscious in sleep has great freedom, and has at its command information gained through past experience in this life and in other lives. In the intense focus of awareness that occurs within the dream state, the inner self is able to direct all of its energies for the purpose of solving a given problem.
quotes.translation: 睡眠中的潜意识拥有极大的自由，并能调取此生及前世经验所获得的信息。在梦境状态中高度聚焦的觉知里，内在自体能够调动全部能量以解决特定问题。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You will not feel free enough to allow yourself freedom for any psychic adventures. With confidence in your inner abilities however your practical problems will effectively be taken care of when you do not dwell upon them, and greater mobility will be achieved as far as other layers of the self are concerned.
quotes.translation: 若缺乏对内在能力的信任，你将无法获得足够的自由去进行任何心灵冒险。但当你不再执着于现实问题时，这些实际问题反而会因你内在能力的信任而得到有效解决，同时你将在自体其他层面获得更大的流动性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Such people--in a fashion, now--play a game of quite serious hide-and-seek with themselves and with the world. They believe in the dictum: "Divide and conquer." It is as if, for reasons I hope to discuss, they refuse to put themselves together properly, refuse to form one fairly united self. The idea behind this is: "If you cannot find me, then I cannot be held accountable for my actions--actions which are bound in one way or another to betray me."
quotes.translation: 这类人——以某种方式——正与自己及世界玩着相当严肃的捉迷藏游戏。他们信奉"分而治之"的箴言。仿佛出于我希望能探讨的原因，他们拒绝将自身各部分妥善整合，拒绝形成一个相对统一的自体。其背后的逻辑是："若你找不到我，我便无需为那些注定会以某种方式背叛我的行为负责。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: At the beginning of the notes for this 920th session I wrote that the session isn't dictation for Dreams. Yet it is, of course, since in it Seth illuminates from still another perspective his concept of value fulfillment.8 The session grew out of the encounter we had yesterday afternoon with an unexpected visitor from out of state. Seth hardly mentions the individual involved, however, but instead goes into the subject of mental illness in more inclusive terms. I see many correlations between this evening's material and that in Session 917 for Chapter 8; there, among other things, Seth had discussed the reasoning mind, the imaginative mind, and schizophrenia. That session had been triggered, at least in part, by a letter Jane had found to be quite upsetting.

quotes.text: There are a few points that I would like to make of a general nature. Ruburt has been reading Jung, though not consistently. The libido does not originate in the individual subconscious of the present personality. It originates instead in the energy of the entity and inner self, and is directed by means of the inner senses, outward so to speak, through the deeper layers of the individual subconscious mind, then through the outer or personal layers.
quotes.translation: 我想提出几个普遍性观点。鲁柏虽未持续研读，但确实接触过荣格理论。力比多并非源自当前人格的个体潜意识，而是源于存有与内在自体的能量，通过内在感官向外传导——即经由个体潜意识深层，再穿透至外层或个人层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: ... in certain terms the theory of evolution, as it is conventionally held, has caused unfortunate beliefs. For how can you look at yourselves with self-respect, with dignity or with joy, if you believe that you are the end product of forces in which the fittest survive? Being the fittest implies those given most to what would appear to be murderous intent -- for you must survive at the expense of your fellows, be you leaf, frog, plant, or animal.
quotes.translation: 从某种角度而言，传统进化论已导致不幸的信念。若你相信自己是"适者生存"法则的终极产物，又怎能以自尊、尊严或喜悦看待自身？所谓"最适者"意味着最具杀戮意图者——无论你是叶片、青蛙、植物还是动物，都必须以同类为代价才能生存。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 705)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Time is one of your most obvious camouflages, and the study of time will lead you in a fairly direct manner from the camouflaged physical self to the inner self, which you ignore. Even now your psychologists speak of the difference between physical time, by which you set your clocks, and psychological time.
quotes.translation: 时间是你最明显的伪装之一，对时间的研究将引导你从伪装化的物质自体，相对直接地抵达被你忽视的内在自体。即便现在，你们的心理学家仍在讨论物理时间（你们据此校准时钟）与心理时间的差异。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: If this sounds difficult then indeed it is, because I will not deal in platitudes, and the exercise of the inner abilities demand, as a bulwark, the sometimes difficult achievement of an ego that can adapt itself in the outer environment, and so hold its own while the inner self is then freed to go its way.
quotes.translation: 若这听起来困难，那确实如此，因为我不愿陈词滥调，而内在能力的运用需要以自我为堡垒——这个自我必须能在外在环境中灵活调适、站稳脚跟，如此内在自体才能获得解放，踏上自己的旅程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The sense, as I said, operates in two directions, and this is difficult to explain. Operating in a contrary manner, for example, there is a pulling together of the self into an ever smaller and more minute capsule that enables the self to enter into other fields, and experience various rather alien planes.
quotes.translation: 如我所言，这种感知是双向运作的，这很难解释。例如，当它以相反方式运作时，自体会被压缩成越来越微小的胶囊，从而得以进入其他领域，体验各种相当陌生的层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Each self is therefore not only ejecting almost in missile fashion such energy from his own core, but he is also constantly impinged by such energy from others. He chooses to translate whatever portions of this energy he so chooses, back into forms that can be picked up and understood by his own mechanism.
quotes.translation: 因此，每个自体不仅像导弹般从自身核心向外投射能量，同时也持续承受着他人能量的冲击。他选择将所接收的能量中任意部分，重新转化为自身机制能够捕捉并理解的形态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Death operates in the same fashion. The animals in particular realize this because they organize time differently from you. Dreaming provides all the conditions of life and death, therefore -- a fact that often frightens the waking self. But here is a creative mixture: the perceptive organizations from which prosaically tuned conscious life emerges. Here are the raw materials for all the daily events you recognize privately and on a world scale.
quotes.translation: 死亡以同样方式运作。动物尤其明白这点，因为它们对时间的组织方式与你们不同。梦境提供了生与死的全部条件——这个事实常令醒时自我感到恐惧。但这里存在着创造性的融合：那些孕育出平淡意识生活的感知组织，正是你们在私人层面与全球范围内所认知的所有日常事件的原始素材。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you find severe errors of judgment, you may then correct them. You may perfect, in other words, but you cannot again enter into that frame of reference as a completely participating consciousness following, say, the historic trends of the time, joining into the mass-hallucinated existence that resulted from the applied consciousness of your self and your "contemporaries."
quotes.translation: 若发现严重判断失误，你们便可加以修正。换言之，你们可以完善，但无法再次以完全参与的意识进入那个参照系——比如追随时代的历史潮流，融入由你与"同时代人"的集体意识所投射出的群体幻觉存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The movement through space is a distortion. I expect to say much more about this in further sessions, as it is extremely important; and you can see now perhaps why our eighth sense, disentanglement, is so important, since the inner self must disentangle itself from a particular camouflage before it can change. It must ignore, so to speak, one set of camouflage and be able to either adopt another smoothly or to dispense with camouflage entirely.
quotes.translation: 穿越空间的运动是一种扭曲。我期待在后续课程中对此详述，因其至关重要；此刻你们或许能明白为何我们的第八感——"解离"如此重要——因为内在自体必须先从一个特定伪装中解脱出来，才能进行转化。可以说，它必须忽略一组伪装，同时能平稳地采用另一组伪装，或彻底摒弃伪装。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Pretend a particular event happened that greatly disturbed you. In your mind imagine it not simply wiped out, but replaced by another event of more beneficial nature. Now this must be done with great vividness and emotional validity, and many times. It is not a self-deception. The event that you choose will automatically be a probable event, which did in fact happen, though it is not the event you chose to perceive in your given probable past.
quotes.translation: 假设某个特定事件曾令你极度困扰。在脑海中想象它并非被简单抹除，而是被另一个更具建设性的事件所取代。这必须带着极其生动的画面感和情感真实度反复进行，绝非自欺欺人。你所选择的事件自动成为可能事件——它确实发生过，只是并非你在既定可能过去中选择感知的那个事件。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The ego chooses channels of reception with great discrimination; and, again, it censors anything which it feels is a threat to its own dominance. In sleep however many dreams are of a telepathic nature, with strong clairvoyant overtones. It is the ego's persistent discrimination in choosing the stimuli to which it will react that in a large measure determines the nature of physical time as it appears to the personality. The ego, because of its function and basic characteristics, cannot make swift decisions as can the intuitive self. Therefore it perceives events in a peculiar manner, almost in slow motion, so that the whole effect is of a series of separate events, one happening before or after the other.
quotes.translation: 自我以高度选择性筛选接收渠道，并再次审查任何威胁其主导地位的内容。然而在睡眠中，许多梦境具有心灵感应特质，并带有强烈的千里眼色彩。正是自我在筛选刺激时持续的选择性，在很大程度上决定了人格所感知的物理时间本质。由于自我功能与基本特性，它无法像直觉自我那样快速决策，因此以特殊方式感知事件——近乎慢动作，使整体效果呈现为一系列独立事件，一个接一个发生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: It is an instant calculator that can tell you your relative position. It is this portion of the self that initiates many of the creative ideas that seem to spring from the subconscious. These will always appear through the subconscious, but the original concepts originate with this portion of the self.
quotes.translation: 它是能告知你相对位置的即时计算器。正是自我的这一部分启动了看似源自潜意识的诸多创意。这些创意始终通过潜意识显现，但原始概念源于自我的这一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: It constantly expands, as does your own universe. It is never static. From it you choose those patterns of thought which you will weave into the physical matter of your universe. The dreaming self sees both fields, and operates in each. It should be realized that the probable self also has its dreams.
quotes.translation: 它如同你的宇宙般持续扩张，永不停滞。你从中选择那些将编织进宇宙物质实相的思想模式。做梦的自我能同时看见两个场域，并在其中运作。需知可能自我同样拥有其梦境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: It does not matter that I am a part of more. What I am as you have known me is legitimate. I go beyond the apparent boundaries of that self by which you have known me, but it is that self that could communicate with you before you could know anymore about my reality or understand that I might be more.
quotes.translation: 我属于更宏大存在的事实无关紧要。你所认知的那个我具有正当性。我超越了你所认知的那个自我的显性边界，但正是这个自我在你进一步了解我的实相或理解我可能更宏大之前，得以与你沟通。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: In other words, a dream allows the inner self to view itself within the spacious present. Now, chemically the physical body does need to dream. That is, dreaming is a necessity if the physical body is to survive. This is the result of certain chemical reactions and chemical necessities, chemical excesses that build up during the days, inciting the mental dream mechanism.
quotes.translation: 换言之，梦境让内在自我在广袤当下中观照自身。从化学层面而言，肉体确实需要做梦——若肉体要存活，做梦是必要条件。这是由日间积累的特定化学反应与化学需求、化学过剩所引发，进而刺激心智梦境机制的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: We have seen that all experience is retained in electrically coded data within the cells, and that the material of the cells forms about this coded experience. We have seen that the ego begins, sparked into being, by the inner self, greatly influenced by heredity and physical environment; and that this ego as it continues to exist gradually builds up an electrical reality of its own, as its experiences form into coded data within the cells.
quotes.translation: 我们已经看到，所有经验都以电性编码数据的形式保留在细胞内，而细胞物质则围绕这些编码经验形成。我们也看到，自我是由内在自己激发而生，深受遗传与物质环境的影响；随着自我持续存在，它逐渐建立起自身的电性实相，因为其经验在细胞内形成编码数据。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Gradually psychological structures are able to focus upon vaster areas, and in order to achieve proficiency in this manner you do indeed begin to build up layered selves that have been independent identities. These varying perceptive abilities organize so that their perceptive powers are pooled in a gestalt that eventually forms a new identity, a more complicated psychological structure that is capable of perceiving larger areas of reality.
quotes.translation: 逐渐地，心理结构能够聚焦于更广阔的领域，为了在此过程中达到熟练，你确实开始构建起曾作为独立身份存在的分层自己。这些不同的感知能力进行组织，使它们的感知力量汇聚成一个完形，最终形成一个新的身份——一个能够感知更广阔实相领域的更复杂的心理结构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: In denying life to yourself you end up by denying life to others. Now I wish this session was recorded so you could hear me, for I hold you, as Ruburt does, closely and dearly as a friend. But you are bound to misinterpret what I say in some sessions, and so it behooves me to speak more plainly. You do not realize now, you do not let yourself realize, the beauty and the complicated reality of your being. You do not let yourself realize the spontaneity and joyful burst that is your inner self, that results in this present human personality that you call yourself. Nor the effort and creative energy that has gone into your making, and that sings within your being like the first morning of creation.
quotes.translation: 当你否定自己的生命时，最终也会否定他人的生命。我希望这次课能被录下来，这样你就能听到我的声音，因为我像鲁柏一样，将你视为亲密而珍爱的朋友。但你在某些课中难免会误解我的话，因此我有必要说得更直白些。你现在尚未意识到，也不允许自己意识到，你存在的美丽与复杂实相。你不让自己意识到那内在自己的自发性与喜悦迸发——正是这内在自己造就了你称之为自己的当前人格。你也不让自己意识到投入你形成过程中的努力与创造力，那创造力在你存在中歌唱，如同创世的第一缕晨光。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I would suggest that you both in summertime indulge in physical activity in the yard--quick activity of a game variety. The fact that Ruburt begins to remember his dreams shows that the inner self is being allowed more freedom, and it responds to physical touch, pampering, denial, as the case may be.
quotes.translation: 我建议你们俩在夏季时在院子里进行体育活动——快速的活动，如游戏类。鲁柏开始记住自己的梦境，这表明内在自己被允许拥有更多自由，而它会对身体接触、纵容、拒绝等情况做出相应反应。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The division between the two aspects of experience begins to take on the characteristics of completely diverse behavior. The unconscious becomes more and more unfamiliar to consciousness. Those beliefs build up about it, and the symbolisms involved are exaggerated. The unknown seems to be threatening and degenerate. The color black assumes stronger tendencies in its connection with evil -- something to be avoided. Self-annihilation seems to be a threat ever-present in the dream or sleep state. At the same time all of those flamboyant, creative, spontaneous, emotional surges that emerge normally from the unconscious become feared and projected outward, then, upon enemies, other races and creeds.
quotes.translation: 经验的两个方面之间的分野开始呈现出完全不同的行为特征。无意识对意识来说变得越来越陌生。关于无意识的信念逐渐累积，所涉及的象征也被夸大。未知似乎具有威胁性且堕落。黑色与邪恶的联系愈发强烈——成为需要回避的事物。自我毁灭似乎成为梦境或睡眠状态中始终存在的威胁。同时，所有那些从无意识中正常涌现的华丽、创造、自发、情感冲动都变得令人恐惧，并被投射到外部，投射到敌人、其他种族和信仰上。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This does not mean that you learn what in larger terms you already know; as for example, if you learn a skill. Without the triggering desire, the skill would not be developed; but even when you do learn a skill, you use it in your own unique way. Still, the knowledge of mathematics and the arts is as much within you as your genes are within you. You usually believe that all such information must come from outside of your self, however. Certainly mathematical formulas are not imprinted in the brain, yet they are inherent in the structure of the brain (intently), and implied within its existence. Your own focus determines the information that is available to you. I will here give you an example.
quotes.translation: 这并不意味着你学习那些在更大层面上已经知道的东西；例如，当你学习一项技能时。没有触发欲望，技能就不会被发展；但即使你学会了技能，你也会以自己独特的方式使用它。尽管如此，数学和艺术的知识如同你的基因一样存在于你之内。然而，你通常相信所有这些信息必须来自你自身之外。当然，数学公式并非印在大脑中，但它们内在于大脑的结构中（专注地），并隐含在其存在之中。你自己的焦点决定了你可获得的信息。这里我将给你一个例子。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner senses allow you to follow some of these actions into other realities. The moment point on the one hand is of course a minute division of action. On the other hand it is an entrance into unknown possibilities and new dimensions. As you probe into any given moment point, you automatically become a part of it and change it accordingly as it makes its impressions upon you. These interchanging's occur on many levels: psychologically, chemically, electromagnetically, psychically. There are subtle variations, as you know, in your known self from one instant to another as you affect your physical environment and form it, and as it in turn forms you. And this is just on the physical level.
quotes.translation: 内在感官让你得以跟随这些行动进入其他实相。片刻点一方面当然是行动的一个细微划分，另一方面它也是通往未知可能性与新维度的入口。当你探索任何一个给定的片刻点时，你自动成为它的一部分，并随之改变它，同时它也在你身上留下印记。这些交互发生在多个层面：心理层面、化学层面、电磁层面、灵性层面。如你所知，在你已知的自我中，从一瞬到另一瞬存在着微妙的变异，因为你影响并塑造着你的物质环境，而环境也反过来塑造你——而这还仅仅是在物质层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (We received a letter from Pat Norelli today in which she substantiates some clairvoyant material concerning her, given by Seth in Session 340. Notes are included in that session.)

quotes.text: Now all of this sounds complicated, but only because we must deal in words. Intuitively, I hope, you will be able to understand it. In the meantime the overall self is forming new threads of activity, you see. The frameworks that it leaves behind can be used by others. (Pause. As I'd asked earlier.)
quotes.translation: 这一切听起来复杂，但只因我们必须用语言来表述。我希望你能凭直觉理解它。与此同时，整体自性正在编织新的活动线索，你看。它留下的那些框架可以被他人所用。（停顿。如我之前所问。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You may find some of your most cherished conceptions to be misconceptions in the light of your new experiences. Since explorations are highly personal, you will most likely begin them from the framework of your current beliefs. Symbols may be utilized, and these may change their meaning for you as you progress. The symbols may evolve, therefore. In the beginning of this work I "warned" the reader that here in these sessions we would go beyond ideas of one god and one self.3 I stated that your ideas of personhood would be expanded. As "Unknown" Reality is being produced Ruburt and Joseph are having their own experiences, and uncovering the nature of the unknown reality as it applies to them.
quotes.translation: 你可能会发现，在全新体验的光照下，某些你最珍视的观念其实是误解。由于探索极具个人性，你极可能从当前信念的框架开始。可能会运用象征，而这些象征的意义会随着你的进展而改变。因此，象征本身也会演化。在这项工作之初，我曾"警告"读者：在这些课程中，我们将超越单一神祇与单一自性的观念。我曾声明，你们对人格的概念将被扩展。随着《未知实相》的创作，鲁柏与约瑟正在经历他们自己的体验，并揭示未知实相适用于他们自身的本质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 725
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: By recognizing these differences and honestly following the feelings through -- in other words, by riding the emotions -- you will be led to the beliefs behind them. A series of self-revelations will inevitably result, each leading you to further creative psychological activity. At each stage you will be closer to the reality of your experience than you have ever been.
quotes.translation: 通过辨识这些差异并诚实地跟随感受——换言之，乘着情绪之流——你将被引向它们背后的信念。一系列自我启示将不可避免地发生，每一步都引领你进入更具创造性的心理活动。在每个阶段，你都将比以往任何时候更贴近你经验的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There is always an overall order to the personality, even though it is in the background, so that in any given case all of the separate "selves," or other sources with whom the individual feels in contact, would together point toward the totality, or unity, that lies beneath. The outstanding mental phenomena, therefore, show in isolated fashions those elements of the personality that are not to be assimilated in the usual smooth fashion.
quotes.translation: 人格始终存在着一种整体秩序，即便它处于背景之中，因此在任何特定案例中，所有分离的"自我"或个体感觉与之接触的其他源头，都会共同指向潜藏于下的整体性或统一性。因此，那些突出的心灵现象，以孤立的方式展现了人格中那些无法以通常平滑方式被同化的元素。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: At the beginning of the notes for this 920th session I wrote that the session isn't dictation for Dreams. Yet it is, of course, since in it Seth illuminates from still another perspective his concept of value fulfillment.8 The session grew out of the encounter we had yesterday afternoon with an unexpected visitor from out of state. Seth hardly mentions the individual involved, however, but instead goes into the subject of mental illness in more inclusive terms. I see many correlations between this evening's material and that in Session 917 for Chapter 8; there, among other things, Seth had discussed the reasoning mind, the imaginative mind, and schizophrenia. That session had been triggered, at least in part, by a letter Jane had found to be quite upsetting.

quotes.text: It floundered like a wet rag in a foul wind. This all ties in with the personality fragments which we discussed earlier. In like manner the personality is divided, one part conscious of the primary self and another disconnected and waiting formation into something new. On a subconscious basis this is exactly how you both created the images at York Beach, the difference being that the personality split occurred on the subconscious level.
quotes.translation: 它像恶风中的湿抹布般挣扎。这全都与我们之前讨论的人格碎片有关。同样地，人格被分裂了：一部分觉知到主要自性，另一部分则断开连接，等待着形成某种新事物。在潜意识层面上，这恰恰就是你们两人在约克海滩创造那些意象的方式——区别在于，那次人格分裂发生在潜意识层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: When the personality is well integrated, then even when it operates as a separate unit the ego still fulfills the basic purposes of the personality as a whole. It is the communication between the very areas of the self which is so important here, as in many other matters. It is possible for the ego to realize its position as but one part of the whole personality, while it still behaves in a directive manner toward physical manipulation.
quotes.translation: 当人格整合良好时，即使自我作为独立单元运作，它仍能完成人格整体的基本目的。此处自我各领域间的沟通至关重要，正如其他许多事务一样。自我有可能认识到自己只是整体人格的一部分，同时仍以指导性的方式处理物质层面的操作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （In Volume I of The Early Sessions this sensation, as I call it, is dealt with following Session 23, in Session 24, and following Session 25, among others. I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: Each inner self is a portion of the basic inner reality. It cannot know itself however, except through experience, and it must create in order to experience. The experience constantly deepens the value fulfillment of basic reality itself. There is no alternative between diversity and nothingness.
quotes.translation: 每个内在自我都是基本内在实相的一部分。然而，除非通过经验，它无法认识自己，而为了获得经验，它必须创造。经验不断深化基本实相本身的价值完成。在多样性与虚无之间别无选择。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: In a larger manner, the identity of the soul can be seen from the same viewpoint. It knows who it is, and is far more certain of its identity, indeed, than your physical self is of its identity. And yet now where in this electromagnetic energy field can the identity of the soul as such be found?
quotes.translation: 从更宏观的角度看，灵魂的身份也可用相同观点审视。它知晓自己是谁，且远比物质自我更确定自身身份。然而，在这电磁能量场中，灵魂的身份究竟何处可寻？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Through the inner senses, therefore, some enlightenment can be received as to both the actual existence of dimensions that the physical self cannot perceive; and also through the inner senses, a sense of confidence and continuity can be achieved through the inner self's knowledge of its freedom.
quotes.translation: 因此，通过内在感官，既能领悟物质自我无法感知的维度之真实存在，也能通过内在自我对其自由的认知，获得信心与连续性的感受。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The practical nature of his own dreams was also more apparent, for again, his dreams sent him precise visions as to where food might be located, for example, and for some centuries there were human migrations of a kind that now you see the geese make. All of those journeys followed literal paths that were given as information in the dream state. [But] more and more man began to identify himself with his exterior environment. He began to think of his inner ego almost as if it were a stranger to himself. It became his version of the soul, and there seemed to be a duality--a self who acted in the physical universe, and a separate spiritlike soul that acted in an immaterial world.
quotes.translation: 他自身梦境的实用本质也更为明显——例如，他的梦境会精确预示食物可能的位置。数个世纪前，人类曾进行如今日雁群迁徙般的迁移。所有旅程都遵循梦境中作为信息提供的实际路径。但人类逐渐开始将自身与外在环境等同，开始将内在自我视为陌生人。这成为他版本的灵魂，仿佛存在二元性：一个在物质宇宙中行动的自我，与一个在非物质世界中行动的独立灵性灵魂。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To that extent natural guilt projected man into the future. This is of course a learning process, natural within the time system that the species adopted. Unfortunately, artificial guilt takes on the same attributes, utilizing both memory and projection. Wars are self-perpetuating because they combine both natural and unnatural guilt, compounded and reinforced by memory. Conscious killing beyond the needs of sustenance is a violation.
quotes.translation: 至此程度，自然罪疚将人类投射向未来。这当然是物种采用的时间系统内的自然学习过程。不幸的是，人造罪疚也具备相同特质，同时运用记忆与投射。战争之所以自我延续，是因为它们结合了自然与非自然的罪疚，被记忆强化与复合。超出生存需求的有意识杀戮是一种侵犯。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The body cannot act tomorrow, today. Its sense data must be clear. This resulting feeling of powerlessness to act leads to a state of hopelessness of varying degrees -- and that mood does not tie itself to specific details, but pervades emotional life if it is allowed to. To whatever degree, the condemning, critical material too often becomes self-prophesying -- for those who put merit on it (loudly) allow it to cloud their reactions.
quotes.translation: 身体无法在今天就做出明天的行动。它的感官数据必须清晰。这种无力行动的感觉会导致不同程度的绝望状态——而这种情绪并不局限于特定细节，若任其发展，便会弥漫整个情感生活。无论程度如何，那些谴责性的、批判性的材料往往成为自我实现的预言——因为看重它们的人（大声地）允许它们蒙蔽自己的反应。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He believed in the specific nature of the creative self, so that it could only be trusted in certain areas. He believed he needed strong mental barriers as well as physical ones, set up against his own spontaneity. He is beginning to understand that the spontaneous and creative aspects of personality are the life-giving ones. They can and must be trusted. He knows now he does not have to slow down, and that relaxation leads to motion.
quotes.translation: 他相信创造性自我的特定本质，认为它只能在某些领域被信任。他相信自己需要强大的心智屏障和身体屏障来对抗自身的自发性。他正开始理解，人格中自发和创造性的面向才是赋予生命的部分。它们能够且必须被信任。他现在知道自己不必放慢脚步，而放松反而会带来行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: We held the session in the living room, as usual. Jane yawned, then laughed as we waited for Seth to come through. "I keep trying to change that title, though...." After a prolonged juggling of titles and themes on her own, she'd finally acquired the book's title directly from Seth some seven weeks ago, or shortly before July 30, 1979; see the closing note for Session 869 in Chapter 10 of Mass Events. "I just think that 'value fulfillment' is a strange phrase to use in a book title," Jane said. "It's too unfamiliar--I'm afraid it'll confuse the reader. I keep thinking of something simpler, like Dreams and Evolution: A Seth Book. And without 'Evolution' being in quotes, too. Or how about Dreams, Evolution, and Creativity ...?"
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Only when such nourishment is for some reason cut off to a considerable degree, is the ego threatened by starvation... We will have more to say concerning the ego's relationship with the so-called unconscious. In a healthy personality the inner self easily projects all experience into EE units, where they are translated into actuality. Physical matter therefore acts as a feedback. Now we will end our session unless you have questions.
quotes.translation: 只有当这种滋养因某种原因被大幅切断时，自我才会受到饥饿的威胁……关于自我与所谓潜意识的关系，我们还有更多要说的。在健康的人格中，内在自我轻松地将所有经验投射到EE单位中，在那里它们被转化为现实。因此，物质身体充当了反馈机制。除非你们有问题，否则我们现在结束课程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: These forms do exist, as your physical body does. They are fused however with your inner self. They are not physical, but they do exist in the whole package of the self, perhaps like the skins of an onion, you see. They are merely the forms that your own consciousness takes in different dreams.
quotes.translation: 这些形态确实存在，就像你们的物质身体存在一样。然而它们与你们的内在自我融合在一起。它们不是物质的，但确实存在于自我的整体包裹中，或许就像洋葱的层层外皮。它们只是你们意识在不同梦境中采取的形式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: (Slowly:) In the same way, it is possible to love your fellow human beings on a grand scale, while at times hating them precisely because they so often seem to fall short of that love. When you rage against humanity it is because you love it. To deny the existence of hate then is to deny love. It is not that those emotions are opposites. It is that they are different aspects, and experienced differently. To some extent you want to identify with those you feel deeply about. You do not love someone simply because you associate portions of yourself with another. You often do love another individual because such a person evokes within you glimpses of your own "idealized" self.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）同样地，人可以在宏大尺度上爱自己的同胞，同时有时又憎恨他们——恰恰因为他们似乎常常辜负了那份爱。当你对人类感到愤怒时，那是因为你爱他们。因此否认恨的存在就是否认爱。这些情绪并非对立面，而是不同的面向，以不同的方式被体验。在某种程度上，你希望与那些你深深在意的人认同。你爱一个人不仅仅是因为你将自身的部分与对方关联。你常常爱另一个人，是因为这样的人在你心中唤起对自己"理想化"自我的惊鸿一瞥。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If -- now, a brief innocuous-enough example -- you meet an individual often enough and think, "He gives me a pain in the neck," it is surely no coincidence that you find yourself with a painful neck in future encounters with this person. The suggestion is quite a conscious one, however (emphatically), given by yourself and carried out not symbolically but most practically, most literally. In other words, the conscious mind gives its orders and the inner self carries them out.
quotes.translation: 如果——现在举个简短无害的例子——你经常遇到一个人并想："他让我脖子疼"，那么在未来与这个人相遇时发现自己真的脖子疼，这绝非巧合。然而（强调地），这个暗示是你自己有意识地发出的，并且不是象征性地而是最实际、最字面地被执行。换句话说，意识心智下达指令，内在自我执行它们。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This sixth sense is too important to skim over, and yet I wanted to mention it this evening because it fits in with our discussion. Without this sixth sense, and without its constant use by the inner self-conscious ego, you could not even construct the physical camouflage universe of your own plane. This sense, again, is used constantly beneath the outer egos awareness, and forms the basis for camouflage constructions on every plane.
quotes.translation: 这第六感太重要了，不能轻描淡写，但我今晚想提它，因为它与我们的讨论相符。没有这第六感，没有内在自我意识自我（inner self-conscious ego）持续运用它，你甚至无法构建自己层面的物质伪装宇宙。这感觉再次在外部自我意识之下持续运作，并构成每个层面伪装构建的基础。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json
